name: "Horthy Miklós" Air Force Academy
reviews: ["This is a very historic place. There are plenty of sites to see for a military retiree like myself. They have a full commissary and base exchange with many other shops and services. In passing some of the military members and cadets, they were all very, very polite. This is a very land expansive place as it's located in the prairies of Colorado, which makes it very beautiful.", 'When people think of the United States Air Force (USAF), they often picture fighter jets, huge cargo planes, and long runways stretching through the desert. But the Air Force also runs some massive bases outside the mainland United States.\n\nThese sites play a major role in America’s global military presence. Some are in Europe, some in Asia, and one is even near the Arctic Circle.', "Visited the United States Air Force Academy in early August and was simply awestruck by the history, mission, character and quality of people who first organized the institution, all the graduates over time, and the proud character of the individuals attending the academy. Beautiful campus nestled in the north part of Colorado Springs, with plenty of space and neat, air-themed buildings. The chapel was under renovation/construction when we visited so we could not view it, but there are plenty of images and models around to get an idea of how beautiful and majestic the building is. We don't have any family members in the military, but we greatly appreciated the valuable opportunity to learn about this wonderful institution and the amazing work of the people associated with it. Highly recommended if you're in the area.", 'Visiting the United States Air Force Academy (USAFA)is a unique experience that allows you to observe the training of future U.S. Air Force officers and explore its iconic architecture and aviation facilities.', "What an impressive place!  Not just the beauty and the organization of it, but the respect for the academy and the respect for the service is present.  The size of the grounds is immense, and we only stayed on the roads, not having time to go on any of the walking paths.  Even though the chapel is currently closed, we still learned a lot through the visitor's center, and the video they showed was informative as well.  Would have loved to have had more time here."]

name: 1 Decembrie 1918 University, Alba Iulia
reviews:
['If you're searching about this University for Erasmus, I can say some words about my experience.

I was proudly a part of an Erasmus+ program in this institution for a week, where we and all the other students were treated like kings, 2 meals a day (breakfast and lunch) and stay (in one of the multiple college dorms) for free, we traveled by bus a lot, went to have dinner in fancy places, went to Salina Turda and had classes with great PCs and great internet (the world could learn from Romania about that) - all free of charge (per se).
You can clearly see that they put a lot of effort in the program and spent the EU funds on providing a very good experience.
I can say with all confidence I was very very well treated in Romania, the teachers were great, the students were friendly (but very shy, which is totally fine), the food was great (they love their meat! Come open minded!), groceries and food in markets were crazy cheap, VISA and Mastercard are accepted in pretty much every single place you go, so you can totally avoid ATMs in your stay unless you REALLY want some crazy street souvenirs (even those accept cards most of the times lol)
I would say that, from what we seen in Romania, Alba Iulia was one of the safest places we visited, it had amazing sights, and a 24h hypermarket right next to the dorm was insane (there is no such thing in Portugal).
Don't forget Romania still has some battle scars, and you'll see reminders of that in a lot of places, but you'll also see a lot of work being done in roads, buildings and infrastructure. If you see sketchy people (I would advise strongly to stay away from gambling houses, there are TONS of them. It's an issue for sure) just avoid them, cross the road, and, obviously, don't "lend" them money. I have had the "heaviest" encounters in Romania, in Bucharest and Alba Iulia, but in the end it never got even close to being violent. My heart aches for all those who are less fortunate in a country where it's still too common to being as poor as one can be, and the fact that drugs are heavily criminalized clearly incentivize users to go heavy (if you're going to jail anyway, why would you do soft ones, right? - sarcasm, btw)
With all this being said, if you're wondering if you should come here to do Erasmus (I cannot speak for locals), I think you will be very well treated without a doubt, if you have any trouble I'm sure that the teachers will have you covered.
Thank you, December 1 1918 University, thank you Alba Iulia, and thank you Romania, it was a pleasure and I can say with confidence it won't be my last time here!

Some small notes I forgot to include:
- The university is located right in the heart of the town, which is SO cool.
- If your language derives from latin, you will probably understand romanian to some extent, as a portuguese, spanish and english fluent speaker, I can say that it is really hard to understand when spoken, but when written things were quite easy to divide mentally and understand, I could basically understand all signs and simple 2/3/4/5 worded phrases, as soon as it reached close to a paragraph my brain gives up from trying to translate it 🤣, I feel like I have to say this because never in my life I thought I would understand a word of romanian.
- if you like KFC (random, I know), they have FULL menus for 3 EURO. Meal, fries and drink. They even have mocktails??? 3 EURO. buying KFC in my country is a 3-times-a-year type of thing, I ate KFC like 4 times when I was there 🤣🤣🤣
- If you're a night owl like me, Romania will feel crazy, everything works earlier there, the meals are VERY EARLY (we went to have dinner once at 4PM... 4!!!!!!, and I was embarrassed to leave food on my plate because they expect you to eat FAST - and everyone does, except you, maybe). seeing restaurants close at 8 PM when I'm used to have dinner from 9 PM to like 2 AM is just pure insanity. my sleep schedule was BROKEN. so be prepared for that!
- the public transport in Romania is so cheap it's genuinely crazy. go nuts and travel a lot if you have the chance to.', 'I had a great experience at this university. The faculty members are knowledgeable and supportive, the campus is well-maintained, and the learning environment is both engaging and inspiring. The university also offers good resources and opportunities.', "I did Erasmus in this school for 4 months and it was terrible. Don't come here if you have another option. The city is very small, the distance between the dorm and the school is very long and you don't have any transportation options (except bolt or your feet), there were almost no activities. It didn't add anything in terms of academics and education. The teachers' level of English is very low and despite this they don't try to help. One teacher gave a grade without any evaluation. I have no idea what I got the grade based on. Despite this, I missed my 2nd Erasmus chance because she gave a low grade. Don't come if you don't want a sad and empty experience.", "It's a pretty bad university for foreign students if u have any other choices, don't come here! I will tell u why:
1) some teachers are great but most of them are arrogant, u can feel that they do not want to work.
2) the (secretariat) is the worse, they do not speak English 🤦🏻 for foreign students it can be so challenging just to take a paper or something.
3) the international office does the minimum to help international students.
4) the food in the restaurant is great and pretty affordable for everyone.
5) they do not have any small association of Romanian students that helps international students to learn Romanian, they opened one on teams, but unfortunately, none of them is enough motivated.
So in conclusion besides the food and some good teachers, this university isn't fine at all, we came here cuz we didn't have a choice, but if u will, don't waste your money here and the city is super small (a big disadvantage for foreign students)", 'I studied for erasmus. This university has really perfect education']

name: Aalborg University
reviews:
['Horrible. Professors are lazy and care only about their own research to the massive detriment of practically every course taught and every project.

Many standard courses are unavailable for many degrees because of rigid and inflexible rules as well as awful scheduling. If you have choices do NOT go here.', 'As they are heavily censoring real reviews, I am updating this post repeatedly to share my experience.

I had the misfortune of starting the Nanobiotechnology MSc course, but I dropped out after just two months. I highly recommend that you thoroughly research any program before enrolling because you cannot change your studies after the semester has begun.

The lab facilities are generally run-down, and the teachers' attitudes are appalling. They seem either forced to work here or simply hate their own lives, and this is conveyed to the students. Furthermore, they lack pedagogical and communication skills, and they make derogatory remarks about students and talk about them in a disgusting way in Danish, thinking that students don't understand.

One instructor, in particular, an old man named P. Fojan, is a prime example of what a university instructor should not be. He is cranky, malicious, ignorant, and has poor personal hygiene. With their poor demeanor and performance, these teachers would be quickly dismissed from any proper workplace. However, at AAU, they have been professors for decades. It is not surprising that graduates of this course are unable to find jobs in Denmark.

The course lacks structure and organization, and students are expected to work on projects without any introduction to laboratory methods. Classes are frequently canceled, and there is a complete lack of interest from the institution. There are no manuscripts or manuals for the projects, and the instructors offer contradictory instructions. Students must resort to watching YouTube videos to learn how to handle equipment because there is no guidance available. Mr. P. Fojan did not even know the proper name of a particular microscope (AFM-SEM), so good luck trying to learn how to use it. He also locks away samples and disappears from the institution, making it virtually impossible to make progress or take measurements. Students must book appointments with him in advance, but he frequently shows up 30-90 minutes late while doing nothing throughout the week. When I showed him some pictures of my sample to ask for guidance, he became heavily agitated when he couldn't magnify the image on my phone. That was the moment when I decided to drop out of the course.

It is beyond me that Denmark, a country that claims to have great quality higher education, tolerates such a poor course. I feel completely scammed and deceived, and the experience was not cheap (I funded it myself).

If you are interested in Nanotechnology or Nanomedicine, there are far better programs available at other universities. Please don't waste your time, money, energy, and sanity on this "course"; you would only find misery here. Updated, again, for better visibility. 99% of the positive reviews are made up here

Probably this is the  worst course at the worst university in the world.', 'Im a young student from Albania and i wanted to start ky studies in this university although i dont know the language im great in english', "Attended their in early 2000's now my son is.. ha.. Good luck to all in 2023 academic year...", "Great University to study with a huge selection of different programs.
Teachers really approachable and friendly.
Based on PBL, so if you don't like to work in groups this is maybe not for you (unless you want to learn it)."]

name: Aalto University
reviews:
['I’ve been visiting the Marine Department for the past few days and found it incredibly active — not only in research and science but also in sports! They have great teams in badminton and football. Truly inspiring to see such a dynamic environment.', "I attended a conference at Aalto University. People-oriented architecture both inside and out, and a massive campus. I appreciate the diversity of dining options on campus. There's a restaurant to cater to everyone's tastes. Additionally, the convenient accessibility of transportation is a significant advantage. Finland has put in a lot of effort to make students feel comfortable and stress-free on campus. The campus is clean and a pleasant place. I wouldn't hesitate to come back here. Also, their lecture halls are massive compared to those at my home university. They even have vending machines and a charming cafeteria with delicious food. A complete buffet, in fact. Not just food, but a buffet. A place where you can sit and eat slowly and comfortably before your next class. The metro station has a small mall, as is usual, for convenient shopping. Lecture halls are big and ventilated. After all it was a nice experience and I really enjoyed.", 'I recall being presented the campus map via email and was gobsmacked by the massive size, plus the plentiful amenities.

Aalto has provided beyond students’ basic needs. The architecture of every building held a unique shape. I was impressed by the number of eateries and visit a few, to list, "Jungle Juice Bar" and "Ravintola Fat Lizard.” One can taste the health, freshness and fruitiness packed into the Jungle Juice Bar’s beverage.

School is located close to the transportation system, which is one of the aspects of living in Finland I was fond of. Never fretting over transport was a massive benefit of living in this country. Aalto has provided generous effort in ensuring students comfort on campus. Premises are pristine, as it was cumbersome to locate even an iota of litter on site.

Lecture halls were massive. Moreover, they provided vending-machines and a cafeteria with a buffet of divine food, which ensured proper nutrition. One event which made the university stand out, was the appearance of the students who were members of guilds in their vibrant costumes.

As a short-term student, they offered a cordial welcome, as on my first day, I recall there were individuals outside directing newbies on where to be.', 'This university is easily the most beautiful university I have visited or attended in Finland. The architecture of the buildings, the designs and decor of the rooms, the proximity of the Metro to the major buildings, applaudable feat.', 'A TOP FINNISH University specialising in Busines and Innovation as well as Sciences and Engineering Science. The facilities are top notched and are the campus is so serene and most conducive for research and academia.']

name: Abasyn University Peshawar
reviews:
['This university lacks proper academic planning. Rather than prioritizing the completion of courses with appropriate standards, the administration focuses more on collecting money from students. The semester is completed within three months, which negatively affects the quality of education.', "Zero staff behaviour, irresponsible staff,  faculty and totally waste of money. They just collect to much money from students with zero percent of students grooming and skills. Faulty is incompetent with zero skills .

Stay away from abasyn university and don't waste your money.", 'A Great University in Private Sector in Peshawar. The Vice Chancellor is doing Very good job. Prof. Dr. Sayed Umar Farooq Sb. Moreover the University has done excellent work in academics and as well as faculty.', "Almas Khan
I had the privilege of studying at Abasyn University of Peshawar, and I must say it was a transformative experience, especially in the field of software engineering. The university's commitment to delivering high-quality education is commendable.

The software engineering program at Abasyn University is top-notch. The curriculum is well-structured and covers a wide range of topics relevant to the industry. From programming fundamentals to advanced software development methodologies, the courses are designed to equip students with the skills and knowledge needed to succeed in the ever-evolving tech landscape.

One of the standout features of Abasyn University is its faculty. The professors are not only highly qualified but also deeply passionate about teaching. They go above and beyond to ensure that students not only understand the material but also develop critical thinking and problem-solving abilities.

The university's emphasis on practical learning is another highlight. Students have access to state-of-the-art labs equipped with the latest software and hardware. Additionally, the university fosters a collaborative environment where students can work on real-world projects, gaining valuable hands-on experience.

Moreover, Abasyn University has strong industry connections. Through internships, guest lectures, and industry partnerships, students get exposure to the actual workings of the software industry, preparing them for successful careers post-graduation.

Overall, I highly recommend Abasyn University of Peshawar, especially for those interested in pursuing a career in software engineering. The quality of education, dedicated faculty, practical learning opportunities, and industry connections make it an excellent choice for aspiring software engineers.", 'abasyn uni is a very good uni. the education system of the abasyn uni is very very fabulous 👌']

name: Abdul Wali Khan University Mardan
reviews:
['Abdul Wali Khan University Mardan is a reputable and well-established institution known for its strong academic standards and quality education. The university offers well-structured classes, experienced faculty, and a supportive learning environment for students from various fields. Its campus is spacious, clean, and equipped with modern facilities that enhance both academic and personal development. The sports area is also impressive, providing students with excellent opportunities for physical activities, fitness, and team sports. Overall, AWKUM maintains a positive academic reputation and continues to deliver good educational and extracurricular experiences for its students.', 'AWKUM, established in 2009, is the second-largest university of the province and stands 2nd in the last HEC ranking for Khyber Pakhtunkhwa. In the recent past, HEC awarded and ranked it 1st in the province and 15th in the country for research awards under National Research Program for Universities. The university has 32 academic departments where 95 undergraduate and graduate programs are offered to 12000 students. Five of its campuses have been upgraded as independent universities i.e. Women University Mardan, Bacha Khan University Charsadda, University of Swabi, the University of Chitral, and University of Bunir. This unique and immense contribution of the university within such a short period is unparalleled in the country’s history. With a qualified faculty of 450 (including half being PhDs), qualified administration, and a vibrant community of 12000 students, the university has tremendous potential for further contribution and becoming a leading educational institution.
Arslan Khan, Student Department of Pharmacy, AWKUM.', 'AWKUM, established in 2009, is the second-largest university of the province and stands 2nd in the last HEC ranking for Khyber Pakhtunkhwa. In the recent past, HEC awarded and ranked it 1st in the province and 15th in the country for research awards under National Research Program for Universities. The university has 32 academic departments where 95 undergraduate and graduate programs are offered to 12000 students. Five of its campuses have been upgraded as independent universities i.e. Women University Mardan, Bacha Khan University Charsadda, University of Swabi, the University of Chitral, and University of Bunir. This unique and immense contribution of the university within such a short period is unparalleled in the country’s history. With a qualified faculty of 450 (including half being PhDs), qualified administration, and a vibrant community of 12000 students, the university has tremendous potential for further contribution and becoming a leading educational institution.
Arslan Khan, Student Department of Pharmacy, AWKUM.', "Abdul Wali Khan University, Mardan (AWKUM)\xa0 is a\xa0public university\xa0located in\xa0Mardan,\xa0Khyber-Pakhtunkhwa,\xa0Pakistan.Established in April 2009, the university is named after\xa0Abdul Wali Khan, a prominent personality of Pashtunkhwa and has forty nine institutions affiliated to it.The university started in an already-built postgraduate college of Mardan,\xa0has nine campuses and more than ten thousand students enrolled in thirty one disciplines as of 2015.\xa0The campus at\xa0Swabi\xa0was opened in October 2010 in the Anbar elementary college building and has since then become the\xa0University of Swabi\xa0and the Palosa campus is home to\xa0Bacha Khan University.Currently the university has five faculties and three constituent colleges. The faculties are Faculty of Arts & Humanities, Faculty of Chemical and Life Sciences, Faculty of Physical & Numerical Sciences, Faculty of Economics & Business, Faculty of Social Sciences. name: Abdus Salam international centre for theoretical physics
reviews: ['ICTP is a world renowned international research center. There is also a training program for students. It is also a development center, for training workshops and other knowledge gatherings. It is located a little outside the city of Trieste in an exceptional setting by the Adriatic Sea. On the terrace of its Café - Restaurant, you can admire the magnificent marina or contemplate the magnificent sunsets. It is therefore an extremely pleasant place of exceptional natural beauty.', 'Opened in 1968, this building serves as the central hub for lectures, research, and administrative activities of the International Centre for Theoretical Physics (ICTP). Founded on the belief that scientific advancement should benefit all regions, ICTP provides education and research opportunities to scientists from the Global South. This building stands at the heart of that mission, enabling collaboration and knowledge exchange among researchers from around the world.', 'Visited this place twice to attend two workshops. The best place is the library, so many collection of books, just amazing. The  budinich lecture hall is also great. There is a cafeteria and a food court in the building. A must visit place for academic purposes.', 'Perfect place, large lectures hall with amazing design with a capacity of 250 - 300 persons, and it is equipped with the most recent presentation devices, recently there is a webinar broadcasting for the events and lectures inside ICTP ... There is also a large hall surrounding the main hall and it is used for the poster presentations with magnetic boards on both sides of the hall.\nIt is an amazing place ... Hope to visit this place regularly to attend all the scientific events related to my research interest ...', 'A perfect institute with diverse international people visiting from all over the world....beautiful location, nice people, fully resourced with academic facilities, peaceful, and rich environment for research 💕']

name: Aberystwyth University
reviews: ['Just walked through so not a review of the uni. Lovely grounds well integrated into the natural setting of the area. Lots of seeing and grassed areas for chilling out', 'We had a great open day at this university! The staff and lecturers seem lovely and knowledgeable. Accommodation looks pretty good too. Students are very helpful and polite - thank you', "Awesome University.  Very serene and peaceful place. The folks are exceptionally hospitable and friendly.  One of the safest cities in the UK. Decent accommodations that are very affordable in comparison to other cities. Things are also relatively cheaper also. It is a country side, a sea town, with just a straight street that also doubles as the business/ market area. Don't so great when one is searching for a part time job. There are jobs, but not just within the cities. It is a great resort point. During the summer. Ah, I also forgot,  the weather,  it is the most unpredictable thing you are ever thing of. It might be smiling one minutes and the next it with be cries of rain. Cold, quite cold. Wonderful surroundings,  great sets of Elizabethan buildings, some receiving renovations.  If you want to enjoy a peaceful and serene country town, then Aber is you point of call.", 'One would think that Medrus, their hospitality food hall, knowing that a conference of 70 people would turn up for lunch there on a Saturday would have more than soggy sandwiches and some pies, all available in paper bags. Tea and coffee in paper cups because not staff to wash porcellain. Penbryn Hall, the intended accommodation, was too filthy to stay in. Poor show.', 'I am an International student and I love everything about Aberystwyth University. The teaching quality, the facilities, the beautiful costal environment, and the hospitality of the people of Aber. I will always recommend this school to anyone looking to study in the UK.']

name: Abilene Christian University
reviews:
['🙋🏿‍♀️This year, I went for a tour of the beautiful ACU campus. It’s a small private university. I walked the Lunsford trail which circles around the perimeter of the campus in support of an event too. I was able to capture some of the campus monuments because I was amazed at all the detailed architecture, structures/statues. I ate a nice meal at the food court and also picked up some items from the campus store located near the food court. I was enamored by the campus’s serene and peaceful atmosphere.  It felt like the ideal learning and studying environment everywhere, no matter where I went. The architecture of the buildings and monuments are notably immaculate. I grabbed some admissions information from the welcome center and captured as much beauty as I could see. There are plenty of food options available.  The library is a big and spacious building offering group study rooms, independent study rooms, a resource and learning center, plenty of accessible learning and research resources, a Starbucks, late operating  hours, multiple stories/levels of study space, etc. It truly is ideal for studying, focusing and learning.  ACU has an art museum which displays beautiful art, some even did sell, state of the art fitness center, science center, sports arena, chaplain center, church hall, dormitories, and a plethora of facilities on campus.  I’m super glad that I took the time out to tour the campus and grab admissions information from the visitors center located adjacent to beautiful pound and Lunsford trail.

I capture the campus’ beauty with several photos, but it appears that reviews with photos are hidden from public view which is too bad. 🙏🏿🤷🏾‍♀️😇
Love, Light, Peace, Blessings , Community, Respect, Peace, serenity', 'When Christian Education Falls Short: Public Awareness of Hidden Bias in Grading

As a student of color navigating higher education, I have experienced firsthand how some professors’ unresolved personal biases can show up in their grading practices. These hidden prejudices often lead to disproportionately harsh evaluations of minority students, creating barriers that are difficult to challenge or even recognize.

This harsh grading is not merely about academic standards—it reflects deeper internal biases that have not been addressed or overcome. Unfortunately, relying solely on a professor’s academic qualifications, such as holding a PhD, is not enough to guarantee fair or compassionate treatment of all students.

Institutions must do more than hire based on degrees; they need to implement comprehensive plans of action to protect all students, particularly minorities who face disproportionate challenges. This includes:

- Ongoing training on bias awareness and cultural competency
- Transparent and consistent grading policies
- Accessible channels for student grievances and support
- Active accountability measures for faculty conduct

Students like me, who depend on financial aid to pursue education, deserve an environment where justice and equity are practiced, not just preached. Public awareness of these hidden grading practices is essential to help prospective students make informed decisions and to encourage institutions to take meaningful steps toward true inclusivity.

Christian higher education calls for justice and compassion to be at its core—not just in mission statements, but in everyday classroom practices. Awareness is the first step to fostering accountability and healing.', "Strong sense of God's presence. Community is emphasized and followed through with. Knowledgeable and helpful staff that go above and beyond typical expectations. They care about their students and staff.", "The ACU leadership camps are great and very meaningful. I don't like though how they always keep you guessing but I guess its a part of it though... They never let you know whats happening to you, where your going in a white van and pulling over and stuff makes you scared... I guess it contributed to the vibe though!", "Great school with good profs and good degree programs. A delightful and winsome Christian environment. I did my BA and MA there. My only real complaint is with the financial aid department. I ended up with $80,000 in student loan debt (after a half-tuition scholarship) because no one bothered to explain how the process worked. I don't believe 18 year olds should be making those kinds of financial decisions."]

name: Abraham Baldwin Agricultural College
reviews:
['Enjoyed Stallion Day 2026. Nice campus and even nicer faculty/staff and ambassadors. Enjoyed learning about their values, programs, and student life.', 'Great school. took my son to come visit it. Located in a nice town. Tour guides and school were very nice and friendly.', "Great college, great instructors, just a bit difficult when it comes to parking or easiness to use the study rooms at the library when you have exams and need to do study groups. Be aware there is a rule, students need to reserve the rooms 24 hours in advance and via e mail to the library. Even if it is on a weekend and they are closed please e mail them, they will read it when they get in on Monday and you will be able to use the room the very next day.
I hope this information helps other students succeed with their education's plans. Thank you!", 'I attended ABAC my entire College career and I enjoyed it! The Atmosphere is great, I enjoyed that my classes were within walking distance and the professors knew you by name and not just a number, the staff is friendly, great places to hang out or take pictures on campus and everyone loves fried chicken thursdays! Great campus! Gee Haw! 💚💛', "The school is overall a 3 star institution for sure, the administration is absolutely positively dreadful. From admissions to financial aid you will have to chase people all the time to get anything done, and sometimes that isn't even enough.
There is one saving grace to this place, it is cheap, at least cheaper than some of the larger institutions in the state, that does matter when you are a college student, but that isn't why I'm giving this review an extra star.
The extra star on my review is for the the teachers, they weren't all perfect but let me tell you, some of them were good hard working people who really had a positive impact on me in the early years of my academic career, so this 4th star goes out to them."]

Founded in 2005 through Royal decree of His Highness Sheikh Khalifa Bin Zayed Al Nahyan, President of the United Arba Emirates, Ruler of Abu Dhabi.']

name: Abu Dhabi University
reviews:
['Even one star is me being generous, best part was me graduating from ADU and even the graduation ceremony in the auditorium, and after one year of graduating !which is insane because this is a          “ reputable university and top university “ or so they say. Tuition fee expensive for the education we are getting. Professors? Oh please don’t get me started, do they even know the curriculum? I better keep my opinions on professor to myself ( saving jobs here ). Student facilities non existence, no place for students to breathe ! Or even sit together as friends and just hang around . The cafeteria on the other hand it’s like you’re stuck in 1978, so smalllll limited restaurants for students to eat at, not enough chairs and tables!!! Where do I eat on the floor during break hours?

Admission, finance , HR, registration team not enough staff, and no patience with students/ parents, some unnecessary comment were said to me by the staff ! where can I complain ??? And if I do complaint are you going to take my complain seriously? Ofc not. The university is getting loaded with students, it’s almost impossible to fit alll of these new students in ADU campus. Not to mention only top and smart students get all the attention support and glory, what about students who need help and support ? Why is no one encouraging them and giving them support, give them a chance to shine?

Next step graduating, no job till this day, hello ADU why don’t you hire your own graduates? Many universities ONLY hire their students, or does my GPA have to be 3.8 - 4 so you hire me or help me find a job. Even applying for a job at ADU takes forever I have to record a video every time I apply for a job?

Me personally, ADU never again.', 'I would like to give special appreciation to Noor Al Hashemi. She is always supportive, professional, and genuinely helpful. She consistently goes above and beyond to assist us, especially through the student association and the complaints department at Grandiose. She is truly one of the best staff members, and her dedication makes a big difference for students.', 'This university sucks the teacher are rude this is my third year and they don’t give make up exams like normal university they just want money and if you need a scholarship don’t join this university. The workers are the most lazy people I have ever met in my whole life i have never wanted to drop out of studying in my whole life and they also mixed up my classes and didn’t inform me who does that and guess what I failed a exam because of them and all the teacher said was it’s your fault like what go to any other university for your own health and keep your money. Make the prices cheaper this uni isn’t even known outside of uae', 'The tuition here is quite high, and I expected stronger academic support. Discounts are usually limited to new students, so continuing students don’t get the same benefits. Teaching styles vary, some professors don’t explain clearly or provide strong revision guidance, which made it difficult to succeed despite my effort. I even failed a course mainly because of the teaching / exams style, not due to lack of commitment. When I raised concerns, there was no support from management.', 'Abu Dhabi University is dedicated to empowering outstanding students and inspiring them to pursue their educational objectives.']

reviews: ['According to the Royal Decree creating the academy, the mission of the General Basic Academy of Non-Commissioned Officers of the Air Force and Space, with the name of Basic Air and Space Academy (ABA), is to train its students in the constitutional principles and characteristics of the Armed Forces, as well as to train them professionally and enable them to permanently adapt their knowledge to the development of science and technology in order to fulfill the purposes assigned to the Air Force and Space.', "The troop kitchen could have colacao or Nesquik or lactose-free butter for people who have intolerances or don't like coffee...", 'The bar is wonderful, with good prices and good portions. The facilities are a bit outdated; they should invest more in improving everything for our future non-commissioned officers.', 'Emblematic. A source of pride for the Spanish Army and a source of the best military personnel and professionals.']

name: Academia de Artillería de Alcalá de Henares
reviews: ['It is one of the best buildings I explored in Segovia, this is not only a military base, but also a museum containing historical war pieces.', 'An incredible building with a rich history and a lovely military museum. They had an open house for Columbus Day. Beautiful interior courtyard and a spacious museum with a large library. Well worth a visit.', "My father did his military service there 50 years ago, and he was especially thrilled to return. The same goes for the family who accompanied him.\nFrom the very first moment, the service was impeccable. The military officer who explained everything to us did so in great detail, showing us all the rooms and involving the children, who were absolutely delighted.\nThe visit is a piece of Spanish history, and it's a source of pride for the people of Segovia to own such a heritage site. Highly recommended, as I know it's not very well known. The library is a real treasure.", "This academy is located right in the heart of Segovia. It's quite large, stretching from San Francisco Street to San Antón Street. Furthermore, the Artillery Academy is very beautiful and a must-see, especially the library.", "I ran out of battery in Segovia to take more photos. The city is incredibly beautiful and offers everything for a great tourist experience, and it's a UNESCO World Heritage City."]

name: Academia de Caballería de Valladolid
reviews:
['cute place to take pictures and walk around this site.', 'The exterior is much prettier than the interior, but I still give it 5 stars…
An iconic building in Valladolid, large and beautiful…
It only opens to the public on special occasions.', 'While exploring the city in Campo Grande Park, we saw this building with its Neo-Plateresque architecture, which also houses a museum dedicated to the Cavalry. Since it was closed, we could only admire this iconic building from the outside. The photos accompany it.', "A stunning and spectacular building. On par with Westminster Abbey or Les Invalides in Paris.
It's impossible to describe until you're standing right in front of its entrance. Located in the heart of Valladolid, it's also very close to the city's shopping district. There's an underground parking garage nearby, and the city park makes for a complete visit.", 'The Cavalry Academy building is a historic landmark located in Plaza Zorrilla in Valladolid.
It was designed by architect Antonio Arévalo Martínez and built in the early 20th century.']

name: Academia Militar de Ávila
reviews:
['I enjoyed the place. Visited on a Saturday and the gentleman at the gate was very welcoming, very hospitable.
Small museum but very well planned.
I like the exhibits.

Best of all: FREE ADMISSION!!
But do note: Has opening hours, not always open.
See my pictures for details, or google before going to be sure.', "We arrived one minute before closing time, and when I said I wasn't from there and didn't know I'd have the chance to see it again, they let us take a quick look around. Although I couldn't appreciate it fully, it was very interesting.", "An exceptional and unexpected experience. The AGM Ávila is an extraordinary museum that deserves far more recognition and visitors. From the moment I entered, I was captivated by the architectural beauty of the historic building that houses it.

The museum boasts impressive collections of art and archaeology spanning from antiquity to more recent times. The galleries are thoughtfully arranged to provide an educational and enjoyable experience. Each section tells a fascinating story about the Ávila region and its historical significance.

What impressed me most was the staff's dedication to maintaining the collections in pristine condition. The pieces are beautifully preserved and presented, allowing for a full appreciation of their historical and artistic value. The context provided greatly enriches the visitor experience.

The museum's atmosphere is calm and contemplative. It doesn't feel crowded or chaotic like some other museums. This significantly enhances the experience.

I highly recommend this museum to anyone interested in history, art, or archaeology. It's definitely a must-see destination for any cultural visitor to the region. A hidden gem that deserves five stars!", "We visited the Ávila Military Academy one Sunday morning and it was a truly wonderful experience. Entry was free, and we were able to visit the museum and admire the antiques. Everything was very well explained and displayed, and there weren't many people around. A must-see if you're in the area.", 'The Archive contains documents and files from 1936 to the present day.
It has a Research Room and an Exhibition Hall with a small selection of written documents, maps, newspapers, and photographs of great historical value and interest to the general public.']

name: Academia Politécnica e Escola Medico Cirúrgica (Porto)
reviews:
['Atendimento telefónico lastimável. No período de arranque do ano letivo, o serviço da secretaria não se encontra preparado para atender às dúvidas dos estudantes. O serviço apenas dispõe de 1 hora por dia para o atendimento telefónico, e mesmo assim não consegue dar resposta.', 'Ontem (segunda-feira, 12 de maio de 2025) visitei com um amigo meu a vossa faculdade e simplesmente gostamos mesmo muito.

A arquitectura da vossa faculdade é qualquer coisa de espectacular, cinematográfica e cenográfica, tem encanto em cada recanto.

Gostei porque é uma faculdade prática, desconstruída, com uma planta muito intuitiva para toda a comunidade escolar, de uma limpeza incrível e fora de série mesmo.

Outra coisa que gostei foi: em qualquer parte da vossa faculdade tinha-se vistas e perspetivas muito interessantes, particulares, bem como, as escalas entre o interior/exterior, privado/público, grande/pequeno entre outras relações que parecem opostas, mas de opostas não têm nada, gostei mesmo.

Obrigado por terem uma boa faculdade.', 'Uma das melhores escolas de saúde de Portugal! Tem imensos cursos, pós-graduacões e mestrados em várias áreas de atuação de um técnico de diagnóstico e terapêutica!  É constituída por uma cantina um pouco pequena mas tem pratos de alta qualidade, com várias escolhas.', 'Lamentável nunca atendem os telefones, e nem respondem aos e-mails, serviço de secretariado funciona muito mal... Lamentável
Lamentável ainda os serviços académicos serem tao fracos, terem tao pouco profissionalismo, não darem respostas na fase de candidaturas, não são prestáveis, não são coerentes, são muito muito fracos.', 'Lamentável quê serviços académicos funcionem tão tão mal que os profissionais não ajudem nem sabem responder às questões.... Lamentável que exista apenas um balcão de atendimento lamentável que no arranque de aulas secretaria apenas funcione dás 9.30 as 11.30 lamentável ...']

name: Academia Técnica Militar Bolivariana
reviews: ['I joined in its inaugural year (2009) and it was one of the best experiences of my life; I am left with only beautiful memories and the pride of having worn that uniform.', 'The Institute for the Training of Technical Officers of the Bolivarian National Armed Forces operates in what was the National Barracks, inaugurated in 1921 to commemorate the Centennial of the Battle of Carabobo. Three years later, this barracks was renamed Sucre Barracks in honor of the hero Antonio José de Sucre, as 1924 marked the Centennial of the Battle of Ayacucho. It was designed by engineer Ricardo Razetti, a specialist in military buildings, and was declared a National Heritage Site according to Official Gazette of Venezuela No. 35,441, dated April 15, 1994.', 'It has interesting ancient architecture☺️✍️', 'It is one of the best experiences and I have learned a lot and many disciplines. I recommend it.', 'Excellent institution. Very organized and safe, beautiful decor.']

name: Academic medical center at State University of New York at Stony Brook
reviews:
['If I could give more stars to the three women working in the 8th floor south Pulmonary lab! I was so impressed by the compassionate treatment they gave us. The receptionist i believe, at the desk answering calls and the two ladies that gave the test. All three are angels and that hospital is lucky to have them. A very grateful patient.', "I want to apologize to Stonybrook for the threats and the trauma that I had caused because I was angry, scared, and in pain. I deeply regret the things that I said due to the frustration of people not believing me. I should have taken the appropriate channels to communicate to people about any concerns that I had instead of lashing out. I'm so sorry and I do live in deep regret all the time and I genuinely am heartbroken for things that I said to my to you all. I was hurting and in pain and I shouldn't have taken that out on you guys. I'm still recovering and battling through mental health issues but Im slowly getting better and fighting through my battles. It was deeply wrong and disturbing. I am learning to break away from old habits and trying to communicate better when it comes to my feelings. I am trying to break from what I had been taught. I was just so hurt and I didn't understand why no one believed or why others were hurting me in long island. Im so so sorry and I will do what I can to resolve the misdeamnor and hold myself accountable for what I did and the damage I cost. Please forgive me and please understand that I live in such deep regret, remorse, and embarassement. It was wrong and I should I have taken a more appropriate and respectful route. I have lost so much because of the pain and my behavior. Im trying to grow and be a better person. I for some reason cant remember all my doctors names but I deeply apologize for being so volatile and cruel. I used to a very nice girl and I let people and my pain corrupt me. Im not an angry black woman just a real sad one. I was truthful about what had occurred but for me to lash out and harass and threaten was really wrong. Im learning to forgive and to forgive myself . I am so sorry Stonybrook. I was wrong for what I did and everything still weighs on me very heavily and I hope you know that i loved Stonybrook so much and Iong island I just didn't understand why no one loved me back. I love you Stonybrook and long island I really do. I just wanted to be loved so bad and the pain to go away. Please forgive me Stony and please have grace for me. Im so so sorry. I want beat myself a lot for what I did and who I became because of pain.But hurting others does no good at all. I was wrong and im so sorry for being a monster. Just because others were hurting me doesn't give me the right to take it out on innocent people. I was really wrong and Im so sorry.", 'I don’t know why people choose to come to this hospital as their first choice. They show no compassion whatsoever ever to patients and also family members who are worried about their loved ones. The nurses I have come across with show no sympathy and I get it, you are just trying to do your job and move along but at least show that you care, that you understand how we are feeling, and not make us feel little.', 'I wish i could give this a 5 but the doctor knowledge was 50/50. Except dr sultana and my podiatry dr mulwani and Sweeney the pt supervisor. And the training lead James. who was great.

The food was not great. How you burn a hcheeseburger to the point it smells a whole room and send that up?! I highly suggest getting on the cbord patient app to order your food as i could make this kitchen and dining experience a whole lot better if i was in charge.

The social worker dept was horrible.

HERE IS THE 5 star review tho!!!! The true heroes!!

THE NURSES ON 14 SOUTH.

Jessica, Nikoli, Amie, Bianca, Colin, Chris, Alex, Christine.
these nurses are the absolute best in the world. I need to know they are appreciated and applauded for their outstanding knowledge training ability patience and bed side manner. You will always be in my heart for my time there. The world and the WHOLE hospital needs to know them.

And these aides Greg, Tess, Mari, Franci, and even tho it was her third day Gabrielle. You ladies made sure I were clean daily. I was less embarrassed. Always checking on me and we kept eachother smiling. You as well need to be appreciated for you being so amazing.

Stony Brook Hospital these Nurses and Aides that  i named seriously need to be acknowledged from the rooftops.

From the bottom of my heart. JC', 'I recently gave birth at SBUH and had a great experience. Although it was not an easy delivery, every medical staff I’ve encountered made it less stressful. They were extremely trustworthy and compassionate. I felt in control of every decision made. Suites are all private and they provided excellent postpartum care. I highly recommend.']

name: Academic University
reviews: ["I was rather interested to see the completion of this new building owned by New York City College of Technology. The other building's exterior appear outdated and not taken care of... It's clean and has wide open space.\nThe Academic Complex holds programs in the sciences and health-related field including Biological Sciences, Chemical Technology, Restorative Dentistry, Nursing, Dental Hygiene, Human Services, Health Services Administration, and Vision Care Technology.\nThere's also a Community Service Center that isn't available yet.", 'Waste of money, "state of the art" facility designed with flash and cash but no thought given to students and faculty. name: Academy of Christian Humanism University
reviews:
["Unfortunately, I live behind the Condell campus of the University; I don't know if there's another one. About two weeks ago, construction workers started working right behind the University, the side facing my building. It's summer, we're on vacation, and we want to relax. Is it legal for their workers (construction workers) to start making such loud noises at 7:00 AM every day? Cutting materials, blowing, shouting, moving and dragging materials, cutting, drilling, and I don't even know what else. It's a terrible experience for the neighbors in the area. We're fed up!", "Excellent place to study. It's a university that gives space to artistic careers. I recommend the degree program (PEL), which welcomes established artists (with a proven track record) who have completed prior studies. The professors are also artists and musicians. You can tell they are professionals well-prepared for the subjects they teach. ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️", "It's a good university overall. Personally, I think strikes can happen for specific issues, not because they're intentional. The infrastructure is good; it has a library on campus, which is a cultural heritage site. It has a printing room that can also be used as a computer lab. There are also computer labs in the Alaska building, which is underground and feels a bit cold. The cafeteria staff tends to be very rotated since it's rented. On the other hand, the professors in my program, which is a partnership, are generally good, except for the department head—I don't know why he's still there 💀. I hope this helps if it's your university choice ☝️", 'From the windows, the neighbors have a view of a graffiti that says "Piteate un coco" (hit a cop), and they haven't removed it. It's ugly. I added a photo. They've already been warned, and they don't care.', "I've been at UAHC (pedagogy) for four years, and if you're looking for a university with modern facilities, then this is the place for you.

However, if you're looking to develop academically and intellectually, this is the university for you.

It promotes critical and reflective thinking among its students and has a strong commitment to and connection with the community."]

There is so much talent and enthusiasm :-)', "I went to class during the summer of 2022, I took a lot of optatives including my favorite: Writing with the amazing actor and writer David Dean Bottrell, I enrolled myself in Acting for Film and Television and Musical Theater,  I loved both programs, my professors, my classes and my classmates. The only thing that I would recommend is to relax a bit with all the covid restrictions, we have to move on and get going, also we are actors we need to see our eyes and faces :).
Another observation: at the time I was 30 and I was one of the oldest, the average age of people going to AADA is early 20's, except for the acting for film and television program, everyone was a bit more and around my age.  I love the American Academy Of Dramatic Arts, it's an historic building, an historic Academy, great location, great neighborhood, it was an honor and an a dream for me going there. I'm very grateful for the experience.

Shot out to: Nurit Monacelli a great human being, actress and teacher and  to my professor and music director Christopher Mcgovern with whom all my singing for the actor classes were like an episode of glee.", "Went to a month long summer program which involved taking classes and meeting the students. The only professor I really liked was the guy who ran the improv class, the others weren't anything special."]

name: Academy of Foreign Intelligence
reviews: ["I recently attended a Microsoft Copilot training session led by Steve Savage, and I found it quite valuable. The practical tips and clear guidance made it easy to understand how to leverage Copilot across various Microsoft applications. Although my organization isn't fully utilizing OneDrive yet, I now recognize its importance for maximizing Copilot’s potential in MS Excel. The information generated by Copilot was largely accurate, and I appreciated the hands-on approach to learning. As a result, I feel much more confident using these tools in my day-to-day tasks. name: Academy of Humanities and Economics in Lodz
reviews:
['As a foreign student in Poland, my experience at the Academy of Humanities and Economics (AHE) in Łódź was extremely disappointing. From the beginning, it was clear that international students were treated as second-class. My classmates and I in the international group were often ignored or dismissed. The reception staff either didn’t speak English or outright refused to communicate with us in English, despite this being an international program.

The administration was chaotic. Professors and staff members frequently quit, and we were rarely informed about who replaced them. We often had no idea who to contact for basic needs or guidance. Professors were not kept updated about the class schedules, which led to constant cancellations — sometimes several lectures in a single week. These were not rescheduled or replaced; we simply lost valuable instruction time.

The quality of teaching was also shockingly poor. Many professors lacked expertise, and when the COVID-19 pandemic began, we were left completely in the dark. Communication shifted to email, but responses took weeks and were typically limited to one-line replies that failed to address our questions or concerns.

One professor, responsible for photography, didn’t even speak English and had to rely on a fellow student with basic Polish skills to serve as an impromptu translator. This lack of preparedness and professionalism was widespread.

During my thesis presentation, the reviewers were browsing their phones, barely paying attention. One of them — the photography professor with little to no English proficiency or understanding of 3D modeling (my thesis topic) — asked questions that revealed a complete lack of basic knowledge in the field. Her questions were so misinformed that even a first-semester student would have known better. Her presence on the panel made a mockery of the work I had spent a year developing.

This institution is disorganized, unprofessional, and utterly unprepared to support its students. My experience, and that of my classmates, suggests that AHE is more interested in collecting tuition than providing a real education.

This place is a scam. Do not fall for it.', 'The worst university ever. All they want is your money. Do not recommend. There are far more better universities. They will charge you everything and call you to court...', "Iwould not recommend this university for anyone, as their service is so weird and  all of this university policy's is depending on collecting money.
Normal this school is doing business. I don't even think that it's education center. Everyone should take care.
As far as you paid money everything is possible. After you paid addmission fee 200£, tution fee +2800$ and after you signed contract. also there is probability that they will tell you, they will not open currently semester and they will refund your tuition fee after euro exchange deduction and they will give you reasons that it happened coz bank take commission 🤭.", 'Horrible place, they lit me on fire as soon as i walked in', 'Terrible university, terrible stuff']

name: Academy of Performing Arts, Film and TV Fakulty
reviews:
['FAMU is a wonderful school located in the center of Prague. It has great history and interesting, experiences teachers. I am currently studying cinematography in the One Year Intensive Course in the international department. I am learning intensely, shooting projects on film (several 16mm and one 35mm) and getting connections all over the world.

If you are curious and proactive, the teachers and administration will make a great effort to make your ideas happen!

I am very happy for studying at FAMU.

4,5/5', "I loved my short experience there as an international student. I was part of a summer filmmaking workshop. Teachers were very experienced and helpful. I learned a lot while there. By the end of the workshop I learned that filmmaking was definitely what I wanted to do. Not just that but I wish I would have been able to return to FAMU and do the International Masters Directing program. I recommend this school to anyone. It's in a beautiful city and in a beautiful location!", "This is my first year pursuing a master's in Photography at FAMU, and I’m thrilled to be studying at such a prestigious institution. The school provides excellent facilities, professional studios, and
top-tier scanning equipment. I highly recommend this university to students, as it is one of the most renowned institutions for film.", 'A fantastic film school located in the heart of a truly inspiring and cinematic city. Surrounded by experienced film instructors—both Czech and international—who are active professionals in the industry, I am forever grateful for my time studying there. The opportunity to create a short film that was later selected for festivals made the experience even more rewarding.

I highly recommend applying to their programs!', 'I had a great Erasmus at this school, that is located perfectly in beautiful Praha.  With doing Production Design for multiple Shortfilms and extending my international network I can look back on the four month with a big smile.']

name: Academy of Public Administration of Belarus
reviews: ["The best educational institution in the country! I'm so glad I graduated from the Academy of Management. It has the best faculty and staff, and it was so interesting to not only study but also be within the walls of my beloved Alma Mater! It was fun, engaging, and educational! I'd happily go back to my student years; the time flew by. And the classrooms and offices are top-notch. Modern, stylish, and youthful.", "The best university in the country, the name speaks for itself! The best place to get a second or postgraduate degree! A great place to get a management education and build a successful career in the future! The only thing is that the interior is a bit dated; it could use some updating, but it doesn't affect the learning process!!!! ♥️🇧🇾✨", "It's a good place to get an education. I can't comment on the quality of the distance learning programs because I was a full-time student at UIR. Fortunately, this specialty isn't available through distance learning. There are always conflicting opinions, but if you try hard instead of complaining, everything is generally good. In what other specialty besides UIR have almost half of the fourth-year students successfully found jobs themselves?!", 'One of the best universities in the country - the Academy of Management, I congratulate you on your 30th anniversary!\nThe age is young for a university and I would like to hope that our descendants will celebrate both 130 and 230 years of the Academy. Special congratulations to the Department of Personnel Policy and Psychology from the 2011 Personnel Management group. To the staff of the department, optimism, professionalism, forward movement and peace of mind.\nHappy holiday.', "My colleagues and I go to the academy cafeteria—the prices are reasonable and the food is decent, especially compared to other cafeterias in the area (I've been to 3). The women at the cash register and bar are wonderful and very polite. A special star goes to the branded tableware—it's very atmospheric 😊"]

name: Academy of Saumur
reviews:
['Beautiful chateau, so much to see. Amazing architecture, displays of artifacts and information boards. Staff friendly and helpful. Toilets were rank, someone had made a right mess..
Very interesting reading about the history of the chateau.
Highly recommend.', 'We had a memorable visit, especially because of the powerful and beautiful fashion exhibit. This was such an unexpected surprise. The range of ceramics on view was memorable and, as a decorative arts historian, fascinating. Displays of objects associated with the long historical importance of equestrian activities associated with Saumur were also interesting. If you find yourself in Saumur, this is worth a visit. Be warned that you can take a steep hike up to the Château but there are easier ways to walk so take that into consideration.', 'Beautiful setting, even if Google did not tell us about a parking facility near the entrance which would have saved us a big climb. Impressive chateau, the Loire is famous for them', 'Spectacularly beautiful Chateau - worth visiting just to see the outside. Not really much to see inside though - a couple of rooms and a lot of ceramics and old horsey stuff ( strong equine tradition in Saumur). As is usual in France the information boards are only in French but there are guide pamphlets in other languages. If you can’t access the interior due to mobility issues you haven’t missed much but worth seeing just for the outside. Really is beautiful.', '9 euros each for entry.
Vaguely interesting to visit inside but I was surprised at some of the displays - Persian ladies clothing and Chinese plates seemed incongruous. A couple of boards were available recounting the history of the Chateau but otherwise not so interesting. The city views were enchanting but much more could be made of the site really.
Beware steep steps up and down. Perhaps a guided tour would be a good idea?']

If you’re a goal-oriented person who’s serious about achieving results, Speed Coach Harry is the coach you want in your corner.', 'in just three short private training sessions, coach mark has improved our daughter’s speed, and agility and technique! Just amazing!']

name: Academy of the Ministry of Internal Affairs of the Republic of Belarus
reviews:
['Modern training center for torturers and murderers.', 'Impressive. We went to the open day. We took our 9-year-old son with us, and he was also delighted.', "I studied here from '95-'99. I have only the best memories. I don't know what it's like now, but in '95, the competition was insane, about 25 people for every spot. I got into the police department, where the passing score was a little lower. Our first year was spent at the SEF, on Bogration Street. In our second year, we were transferred to the center. My recollections are that the BPF guys were constantly up to no good and kept us on high alert on weekends. It was lucky if they showed a film in the lecture hall, otherwise we spent the whole day on buses in the courtyards. It's a lot to remember...", 'Hello! I want to thank the teachers who raise real MEN! Today, my family and I had dinner in our forest, near the academy (no alcohol allowed). A patrol came over, checked our documents, and wished us a good time! They were very nice and polite! They even wore masks when interacting with us! We felt they cared about us and were protecting us! Thank you!', 'A great atmosphere! The cadets are very interesting and talkative.

For the country, the Academy of the Ministry of Internal Affairs of the Republic of Belarus is a strategically important facility for training personnel aimed at ensuring public safety.

SERVE THE LAW, THE PEOPLE, AND THE FATHERLAND!']

reviews: ["A stunning campus with an old European style buildings. This beautiful campus sits in the heart of a Wolfville, a quaint town that boast it's natural beauty. Is that what you think of women Acadia?', "The old stomping grounds. Timeless campus.\n\nGreat experience studying here. Can't recommend enough", 'If you\'re thinking about attending this university, run while you still can. It was easily the worst 8 months of my life. I was sleep deprived all year from hearing my neighbors punch holes in the wall at 3 AM and break my section\'s furniture (which everybody was charged for). Some of the professors weren\'t terrible, and the classes were bearable. The student life, on the other hand, was atrocious. The parties are trashy and disgusting, the whole campus smells foul, the dining hall food isn\'t edible, there\'s nothing to do, nobody has manners, most buildings appear unkept, the only gym is unusable, and the medical services are a joke. I could go on, but I believe I have made my point. No employer will ever take you seriously with a degree from this "university," so if you care about your future, I\'d highly suggest looking elsewhere and if you just want parties, find some place less trashy.']

name: Académie d'Orléans-Tours
reviews:
['This public body is unreachable. I’m surprised how unprofessional and lazy the people in this town are.', "I sent an email in early July to the regional education authority and to my son's school following a problem with a teacher.

No response, so I followed up two days ago and still nothing…
They don't even bother to reply or take the matter seriously. The principal, for her part, is no longer at the school, but she conveniently neglected to inform me of this when I came to see her in early July. She advised me to send the email and copy everyone, and that she could also escalate the issue. She did absolutely nothing because she knew she wouldn't be there anymore, so she doesn't care. Meanwhile, my son is forced to continue attending this school, and of course, he still has this infamous teacher. So, at the slightest problem, it will be a direct complaint to the police station. Too bad.", "There is no organization or competent staff. Every person you speak to is unable to answer your questions.

The receptionist was lovely, as was Ms. P., who took the time to explain things and answer my questions. These two ladies exuded unparalleled kindness and professionalism.

reviews:

Dommage qu'il faille mettre au moins une étoile pour pouvoir écrire un commentaire, c'est déjà trop cher payé. I called to ask for information, and they told me they'd connect me to the right person... And then I heard a message saying they'd be on vacation starting December 21st, accompanied by cheerful Christmas music, and then the call dropped.
It would have been much more sensible to tell me the offices opened at 1:30 PM so they wouldn't hang up on me after that message or answer at 1:00 PM, LOL.

Come on, to err is human, at least update your message if you're away or closed ;)", 'Excellent contact with the DEC, thank you for your clear and spontaneous clarifications on an examination result.', "It's a fight, a battle (3 weeks) when you want to get something that's rightfully yours! The reception is okay at the entrance, but as soon as you go upstairs, it's a stressed-out team and they think you're bothering them... zero kindness, curt answers, in short, you're annoying them (to put it politely!!) but I think that's their job!"]

name: Académie de Bordeaux
reviews:
['DAVA || GIP FCIP || VAE
Validated experience regarding the administrative and financial management of my DSCG VAE support.

While the support package funded by my CPF (Personal Training Account) was for 20 hours, I only received 12.5 hours of actual support (as evidenced by the Yousign attendance records) before taking the exam. This discrepancy is absolutely not my fault: it is due to the organization's poor management and deficiencies in the support provided.

Despite this failure on their part, the organization declared the full 20 hours to the Caisse des Dépôts (French public sector financial institution), categorically refusing to credit the remaining 7.5 hours to my CPF account.

After contacting the Caisse des Dépôts et Consignations (MonCompteFormation), they officially invited me to submit a report of suspected fraud against this institution for failing to comply with the mandatory "service rendered" rule.

A word of advice to future trainees: closely monitor your attendance sheets and the debiting of your training entitlements. Passing the exam does not in any way authorize the overbilling of public funds at the expense of your future entitlements.', "Hello, I am a teaching assistant for students with disabilities in preschool and elementary school. The Ministry of National Education pays me €942 per month, which is below the poverty line. How am I supposed to live with dignity? I believed that the values ​​of the Republic were equality and fraternity, but I don't feel equal at all compared to other French people, and I also wonder where fraternity is when people are kept in poverty? The State has been abandoning the poor for decades, even centuries (we see this with farmers, for example).

Every day I tell myself I'm fed up, I want to quit this job. The students will manage without me, and so will the teachers, since they're quite happy to have another adult in their classroom to support them in case of an emergency...

Yet the teachers aren't striking to support us; it's every man for himself... which makes me want to let everything fall apart even more, run away, and work for myself, because working for yourself is much more lucrative.

Yet there's a shortage of 50,000 teaching assistants in France.", "As it is normal to report problems, it seemed only right to share positive experiences. I wanted to thank Mr. Labadie for his responsiveness, efficiency, and courtesy. For a long time, the Rectorate's services suffered from much criticism, but that is now a thing of the past. Thank you to all these public servants who, despite often deteriorating working conditions, demonstrate true professionalism.
GL", "The good thing is they never reply, so for a director like Mr. Harwel, I find that very poor. I'm not going to tell you how to do your job, but in order to initiate an appeal, there's a deadline to respect, which can only be met when you provide me with the information requested in my last 17 emails. I understand that you don't read everything, which is rather ironic. I'm still waiting for a response.", 'I just spoke with the university administration. My contacts were quick, efficient, and accommodating. They were polite, helpful, and understanding. I thank them warmly for their welcome and professionalism.']

name: Académie de Créteil
reviews:
['Good 👍', "My experience with this administration has revealed serious shortcomings in its respect for students' rights and in its support for families.

Despite precise, repeated, and thoroughly documented reports, no effective and appropriate response was provided to my child's situation, even though their needs were clearly recognized.
The relevant departments responded with silence, inaction, or decisions completely detached from reality, leaving a struggling student without any real protection.
I was forced to take my case to the administrative court, which ruled in my favor in its initial decision, thus confirming that our rights had not been respected.
This institutional dysfunction has had serious human consequences: a vulnerable child and a mother forced to fight alone against an administration that should have protected them.
This situation should never have existed in an institution tasked with guaranteeing the best interests of the child. I'm forced to give it one star, which it doesn't deserve; it should normally be zero stars!", 'As a teacher, I was pleasantly surprised by the quality of the service I received. From the very first contact, the staff were available, attentive, and efficient in handling my request. A special mention goes to the administrator who responded with clarity and kindness, which is invaluable in what is often a stressful situation for contract teachers. Thank you for this practical and human support with our administrative procedures! 👩‍🏫📚', "You have to give it one star, otherwise there won't be a review.
No empathy from reception, secretaries, and even the rector (Head of the Public Education Service!).
No help, no support for students in the Val-de-Marne region who are left by the wayside, rejected by the public education service.
My son graduated this year and hasn't been assigned to any university to pursue a master's degree.
The rector must be unaware of the right to continue studies, which is completely flouted by the French administration.
Good to know: this academic year in September 2025, there are approximately 36,000 students who have not been assigned to a first or second master's degree in France.
A disgrace!", "This academy is putting me in a real bind. Dozens of calls and emails requesting my end-of-contract documents. In vain. It's October 7th, my contract ends August 31st, and still nothing. It's appalling. And they find it surprising that there is a shortage of teachers?! There are laws/rules of law that even state services must respect, and which they cannot ignore."]
