name: Accademia di belle arti europea dei media
reviews:
["Absolutely not recommended. I attended this academy for over three years, and the most problematic aspect of it is its administration: disorganized, incompetent, and, above all, unclear and untransparent regarding the various bureaucratic procedures. An example of this, drawn from my personal experience, is having to repeatedly postpone my thesis defense due to both my supervisor (who almost never responded to my emails) and the administration, which provided confusing instructions on how to write the thesis (such as the page count suddenly increasing from 30 to over 100). When I reported this problem (as well as many others I've encountered over the years), I was either ignored or not provided with satisfactory explanations.
As if that weren't enough, some time ago, I published a much more extensive review than this one, which, however, the dean herself forced me to delete, threatening to sue for defamation.
If you don't trust my words, look at the rest of the reviews, and notice how many 1-star reviews there are compared to those with higher ratings (which coincidentally were almost all written by professors) and ask yourself a few questions.", 'LA PEGGIORE ACCADEMIA DI MILANO. Ho frequentato solo un anno di quest’accademia di belle arti appena uscito dal liceo (anno 2021) e sono scappato via subito una volta finito l’anno. Ho fatto l’indirizzo di video e fotografia e ho trovato solo pochi prof validi (letteralmente 2/3 su tutta la squadra di prof del primo anno) mentre tutti gli altri spiegavano poco a lezione, non mettevano giù degli argomenti o dei temi chiari e precisi, totalmente disorganizzati per esami, esercizi a casa e valutazioni poco chiare. Il problema più grande però è la preside con la segreteria, la prima non è assolutamente in grado e nelle condizioni di coprire un ruolo del genere, tutte le volte che ho avuto delle interazioni con lei mi trovavo davanti a una persona senza pazienza, esaurita, per nulla professionale e con poca considerazione dei suoi studenti. In più, quando avevo espresso la mia volontà di andarmene, mi era stato chiesto più volte con insistenza di rimanere. Inoltre so che ad alcuni studenti ha chiesto con insistenza di rimuovere le loro recensioni in merito all’accademia da Internet. Surreale! La segreteria è molto disorganizzata, molto più delle normali università, e ti crea enormi problemi per qualsiasi cosa tu debba fare nella scuola (soprattutto per quanto riguarda la tesi, con indicazioni per nulla chiare e persone che hanno dovuto ritardarla di un anno per colpa loro e dei prof). Insomma, TOTALMENTE SCONSIGLIATA, metterei mezza stella se si potesse.', "I feel sorry for the teachers because most of them are professionals, very competent, and truly passionate about what they teach, but the school itself is sooooo disorganized.
I've never attended a school as disorganized as this one since elementary school. Official announcements aren't posted on any portal or website, but Facebook is used. Schedules are often published the night before, so even for a worker/student, it's practically impossible to organize. During my time there, I tried sending emails that were never read. For everything, you had to go to the office, wait in line, wait your turn, and sometimes be told it was no longer office hours.
They were always refused permission to leave the building, including visits to exhibitions and practical exercises outdoors (photographs, filming, etc.).
As I said at the beginning, I feel very sorry for the teachers because I can't complain about anyone; in fact, if I could do it again, I would never choose this school again.", 'I was enrolled for a year, then I ran away! Terrible organization, impossible to communicate with the administration, they don't even issue a badge to prove you're a student; when I asked, they just gave me a piece of paper for €15. Some teachers are qualified, while others aren't worthy of the position. However, the fact remains that there are no workshops, so you often find yourself without the opportunity to experiment and "touch" something essential in art courses.
Don't enroll; there are plenty of art schools in Milan, even less expensive ones, with much better service.
In short, "lose all hope, ye who enter."', "So well organized that you will find yourself having lessons until July and having to take exams practically at the beginning of August.

EDIT

I would add that the positive reviews are exclusively from the professors who, sadly I would add, try to raise the grade.

However, the few professors truly worthy of this title are not enough to raise the rank of this school which disappointed me right from the start: terrible organization of the secretariat, some professors whose function is exclusively to warm your seat and much more, unfortunately in my opinion they are not able to give you what you would have expected after having invested so much in it.
Personally, I don't recommend anyone to study at this academy, it's not worth the investment, even if it were free because no one will ever give you 3 years of your life back.

I'm writing this review because I also would have liked to hear these words before making a decision that, alas, I still regret. I took a risk and it went badly, but if even one person finds this review useful in order to avoid my experience, I will be satisfied."]

name: Accademia Filotima
reviews: ['Complete cello concertos Boccherini: what a pleasure to listen to their performance.', 'Incredibly amazing School', 'Bel teatro!', 'Beautifull Verona', 'The theater is Italianate and well rebuilt after the Second World War following the original 18th century project. name: Accademia italiana della marina mercantile
reviews:
['Centro di alto livello locato in una delle zone piú belle di Genova
The training center provide course as per national requirements and based on calendar provide quite good schedule of different training , due to covid the center assigned more space between trainees , but no A/C provided during course .
Close to city transport system and center
Equipments of the center adequate for italian cost guard requirements.', 'And if I simply wanna work as O/S?? Cioe mozzo ... devo inscrivermi..??', 'Excellent', 'A school that changes your life, even when Italy pretends not to see it

I am a former student of the Italian Merchant Marine Academy, and despite the difficulties I experienced along the way, I can only acknowledge its value. Yes, there were tough moments: long waits to board, shipboard staff who look at you with suspicion simply because you come from an academy, and an Italian system that still refuses to fully recognize this program as equivalent to a three-year degree. Unacceptable, especially considering that in Europe and the rest of the world, "vocational schools" have for decades been considered equal, if not superior, for certain professions.
Yet, for me, it was a great adventure. A journey made of true friendships, daily sacrifices, challenges overcome, and moments I will never forget. The Academy trains you, tests you, and gives you responsibility. It's not an easy school, but it is a school that truly prepares you for life at sea and technical work.
It's time for Italy to begin giving proper recognition to these educational institutions, which represent a concrete, solid, and dignified alternative to traditional universities. Continuing to ignore them means contributing to the crisis in the technical and professional sector, which was once a source of national pride and which today, sadly, has become an object of ridicule abroad.

The Academy is not perfect, but it is a gateway to a fascinating and complex world, one that deserves respect and appreciation. And those who have been there know it: you wouldn't go back, because it taught you more than a thousand theoretical lessons. It taught you to resist. And to navigate, even when the wind is against you.', "It probably depends on the direction, the tutors, or the management. However, the experience was disappointing; too many words, too few actions, and let's just say that the treatment isn't the same for everyone."]

name: Accademia Nazionale d'Arte Drammatica Silvio D'Amico
reviews: ['Talented boys and girls', "Undoubtedly the most famous acting academy in Rome (and beyond). It's certainly a very stimulating environment for young actors who want to train and earn a qualification that can be used in the world of theater.", 'Yesterday, by chance, I stumbled upon YouTube videos about this school. I immediately noticed that the atmosphere is very elite. So, out of curiosity, considering that very few people (14) get in during the first year, I randomly searched for the name of a current student and, lo and behold, without even looking for others, I verified that he is the son of an actor mother and father. It would be better not to make videos promoting the announcements since those who get in already know about it beforehand.', 'My daughter tried to enter the Silvio D\'Amico Academy twice. The first time she passed the first two stages, but in the third and final stage she was rejected. A year later, my daughter tried to enter the Academy again, but she didn\'t even pass the first stage. Which means that, in a short time, she went from being good to being really dumb. What a strange way to evaluate the poor aspiring actress.\n\nEvery year, almost a thousand candidates show up and pay a 100 euro registration fee for the selections. A real speculation considering the limited number of people allowed to enter as actors or actresses (seven women and seven men). Let\'s not even talk about the money that candidates spend to prepare and then get zero results. The entrance tests are not open to the public as is normally done in schools of all levels or in any university in Italy. In fact, relatives or friends can attend the exams. Everything takes place in "secret rooms" and no candidate knows the reason why they didn\'t pass the three tests. The candidates\' CVs are not taken into consideration at all and no one knows what the evaluation criteria are that allow a candidate to pass the three tests. At this point it is not worth spending money and wasting time. I advise everyone to enroll in the University and, at the same time, to attend acting courses for a year or two and to participate in the various castings. Or it is preferable to study abroad where in the Film and Theater Academies the CV and talent are amply rewarded. Instead, let\'s leave in Italy on the set or on the stage the lucky talentless donkeys who will never work if they go abroad. And let\'s never forget that the word meritocracy in Italy is considered a blasphemy!', 'I happened to see "Hotel Goldoni" at the Teatro India, an end-of-year performance. I left the theater at the end of the first act, after an hour and a half without breathing air. Some of the kids were good, but the direction was unwatchable, and some parts were exasperating and out of place. I wish these kids a great career, and I hope I\'ve just had a bad time.']

name: Accra Technical University
reviews:
['Very great Institution. Small compound but best in every way. Am proud to be an alumni', "So far so good, I like the place, I school there. The environment is serene, it's just that the organisation of the blocks and department is a bit complicated. But overall it's not a big university so get around is easy💯", 'My experience at Accra Technical University during my brother’s graduation day was a blend of pride and a little practical reality-check. The campus felt alive, gowns moving like small waves of accomplishment, families glowing, cameras clicking their tiny celebrations into the air.

Watching my brother cross that stage was the heart of it all. You see months of pressure turn into a single moment of recognition, and it’s impossible not to feel your chest rise with it. The energy on campus made the day feel ceremonial, almost festive.

But one thing stood out sharply in the middle of the celebration: there weren’t enough chairs for the families. You could see proud parents and relatives standing for long stretches, shifting from foot to foot, trying to catch every moment despite the discomfort. It didn’t ruin the day, but it cast a small shadow on an otherwise bright experience. A gathering meant to honour achievement deserves space that honours the people who supported that achievement too.

Still, the collective pride was unmistakable. You could feel it in the cheers, in the relief on parents’ faces, in the quiet joy of students who finally made it. By the time we walked off campus, that warm pulse of pride was still there steady, sincere, and larger than the little inconveniences around it.', 'The school compound is not that large but the lecturers and students have calm and welcoming mind.. They associate well with each other and move like a family.. One thing I like about ATU', 'The best Technical University in Ghana and 4th best in Ghana. Nice campus and good facilities.']

name: Acharya Prafulla Chandra Ray Polytechnic
reviews:
['Acharya Prafulla Chandra Ray Polytechnic College, also known as A.P.C. Roy Polytechnic College, stands as a shining gem in the city of Kolkata, West Bengal. Established in 1950, this esteemed institution has earned a well-deserved reputation as one of the best Government polytechnic colleges in Kolkata.

Affiliated with the esteemed West Bengal State Council of Technical Education (WBSCTE) and having the approval of the All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE), the college ensures top-notch quality in its Diploma in engineering courses across various disciplines.

Nestled alongside the prestigious campus of Jadavpur University, Acharya Prafulla Chandra Ray Polytechnic College exudes an atmosphere of academic excellence and knowledge-driven endeavors. Named in honor of the eminent scientist Acharya Prafulla Chandra Ray, the institution carries the torch of his legacy forward by empowering students with technical education par excellence.

With its dedication to imparting knowledge and fostering growth, this polytechnic college has rightfully earned its position among the best polytechnic institutes in West Bengal. It continues to nurture bright minds, shaping them into skilled professionals who contribute significantly to the realm of engineering and technology.', 'I it a good collage for diploma for regular and lateral entry students after 10 or 12 by giving jexpo exam. The collage contains some diploma branches like - DCST, DME,DCE,DEE,DEIE,DFPT.
A good environment for study. Beautiful plane near jadabpur University gate no 4 . Recommended for diploma core branches.

15-20 min walking distance from 8b bus stand or rail station', "I am a Student of This College Department of Electronics and Instrumentation Engineering (DEIE) . One of The Best Diploma College in West Bengal.

Acharya Prafulla Chandra Ray Polytechnic is a polytechnic college located in the city of Kolkata, West Bengal of India, alongside the campus of Jadavpur University. It is affiliated to the West Bengal State Council of Technical Education (WBSCTE),[1] approved by All India Council For Technical Education (AICTE) and provides Diploma level technical education to its students. The college is named after the scientist Acharya Prafulla Chandra Ray.There is no hostel facility.In the college there is a library. The students can issue trade wise text books for certain period of time. The library has also plenty of seating capacity for studying inside. Most of the text books of all departments are available in the library. The library is really very helpful for the needy students. Every student can issue two text books for every semester and can read any books inside the library.

Having a Students' Union responsible for various extra curricular activities like Fresher's Welcome, Annual Sports, Annual Cultural Programme etc.", 'I completed my diploma in electronics & instrumentation from this college. I spend 3 years in this college . It was very nice experience with the college and I think I am lucky that I completed my diploma from best polytechnic in westbengal .💙❤💛
কলেজ জীবনের কিছু মুহূর্ত শেয়ার করলাম ।। আশা করি সবার ভালো লাগবে !!! ☺☺
MISS YOU APC 😑😑😐😐', 'Acharyya Prafulla Chandra Roy Polytechnic, mostly known as APC Roy Polytechnic, is one of the best polytechnic colleges in India. APC Roy Polytechnic is situated in Jadavpur, Kolkata. There are six departments in this college and they are civil engineering, electrical engineering, mechanical engineering, computer science and technology, electronics and instrumentation engineering, food processing technology.

JEXPO is the entrance exam to be selected in this college. A student can also get admission in this college through VOCLET where he/she can get admission directly into second year.

The nearest railway station to reach here is Jadavpur Station. And the nearest bus terminus is Jadavpur 8B bus terminus. If you travel from Howrah Station then best option will be a "E1" bus.']

name: Adamawa State Polytechnic
reviews:
['ADAMAWA STATE POLYTECHNIC IS A RESPECTABLE INSTITUTION LOCATED IN JIMETA, YOLA, ADAMAWA STATE, NIGERIA. THE SCHOOL OFFERS A RANGE OF NATIONAL DIPLOMA (ND) AND HIGHER NATIONAL DIPLOMA (HND) PROGRAMS IN DISCIPLINES SUCH AS ENGINEERING, SCIENCE AND TECHNOLOGY, MANAGEMENT, AND SOCIAL SCIENCES. WHILE THE INSTITUTION PLAYS A KEY ROLE IN SKILL DEVELOPMENT AND TECHNICAL EDUCATION IN THE NORTH-EAST REGION, IT FACES SEVERAL CHALLENGES THAT AFFECT OVERALL PERFORMANCE.
OVERALL, ADAMAWA STATE POLYTECHNIC IS A DECENT INSTITUTION WITH GREAT POTENTIAL, BUT IT NEEDS SIGNIFICANT GOVERNMENT AND PRIVATE SUPPORT TO IMPROVE INFRASTRUCTURE, TEACHING QUALITY, AND STUDENT WELFARE.', 'Adamawa State Polytechnic\xa0is a tertiary educational institution in\xa0Yola,\xa0Adamawa State,\xa0Nigeria. It was established in 1991 through a merge of the College of Preliminary Studies\xa0Yola\xa0and the Staff Development Institute Numan. The new polytechnic provided National Diploma programs in Computer Science, Statistics, Accountancy, Business Studies and Secretarial Studies.The polytechnic, run by the state, is accredited by the National Board for Technical Education.', 'The best polytechnic in the whole of northern Nigeria.
A good and lovely study environment.', 'Adamawa State Polytechnic popularly known as SPY is a tertiary educational institution located in Jimeta-Yola Adamawa State. the Polytechnic has been officially accredited and/or recognized by the National Board for Technical Education (NBTE) and other related Educational Board
Courses:
Accountancy
Agricultural And Bio-environmental Engineering Technology
Banking And Finance
Business Administration & Management
Civil Engineering Technology
Computer Science
Electrical/electronic Engineering Technology
Mass Communication
Mechanical Engineering Technology
Office Technology And Management
Public Administration
Science Laboratory Technology
Social Development
Statistics
Surveying And Geo-informatics
Urban And Regional Planning', "It's a standard Polytechnic owned and operated by the Adamawa state government, equiped with highly trained qualified teaching staff, and lecturers, has about 3 campuses across the state, The science and technology main Cammpus, Jambutu Campus, and Numan Campus, has about 10,000 students, and offer variety of courses."]

name: Adamawa State University
reviews: ['Yo, let me put you on to Adamawa State University (ADSU) in Mubi—it’s this lowkey gem in Nigeria’s Northeast that’s got heart and hustle. Picture a chill campus along the Yola-Mubi road, with lecture halls that aren’t fancy but get the job done, some solar-powered vibes, and a library packed enough to keep your brain fed. The hostels? Basic, sure, but they’re where you’ll make lifelong homies from all corners—Fulani, Margi, you name it. Academics here don’t play: they’ve got everything from sciences to agriculture to an MBA, and the lecturers, many with global chops, drop knowledge that hits home, like how to farm smarter in a changing climate. You can get in with a UTME score of 150 or higher, and their remedial program is a solid boost for anyone needing a leg up. Yeah, the Boko Haram mess a decade ago forced a temporary shift to Yola, but ADSU came back stronger, with tight security and a community that feels like family—think Ramadan iftars or yam festival celebrations that bring everyone together. The vibe is real; lecturers actually know who you are, and you can jump into debate clubs or get your hands dirty on their agric demo farm. But, real talk, the Wi-Fi can be a headache, power cuts sneak in despite the generators, and the three-month semesters are a sprint. Mubi’s calm is perfect for locking in, but if you’re itching for big-city energy, you’ll need a trip to Yola. ADSU’s not about flash—it’s about grinding and growing, making it a dope spot for anyone ready to put in work and come out ready to shake things up. Bring a solar charger and some grit, and you’re good.', 'Google map is a great app that you to find your location easily', "Never give up no matter how it's difficult do no behalf of grace", "Adamawa State University, Mubi is an institution of higher learning that offers Diploma, Degree program, Masters program and phD in different courses. It's indeed a very good institution own by the state government of Adamawa State of Nigeria which have professional tutors in different field.", 'A nice place to be in with beautiful environment']

name: Adam Mickiewicz University, Poznań
reviews:
['Three years ago, in 2019, I got sponsored travel grant from ICSSR, to attend and participate in an International conference IUAES, which was held at Poznań, a city beautiful known for its cultural and architectural design, beautiful market. the conference venue was at Adam Mickiewicz University, As an International delegate, I was provided free local travel pass from conference organisers. Every day morning, when I had to go to the conference venue, I preferred to travel by foots, so that I could see and feel the local culture from close. The polish people were so nice, every other day, I got compliments on my turban,  even some the people stopped and asked me about “how are you. you know today’s weather is so beautiful” they have culture to greet each other on the way. The conference organiser were so humble and helpful especially Łukasz Kaczmarek who helped me to get Schengen visa in such a short period time which was otherwise impossible for me to get in usual process. They organised gala dinner for all the delegates to make it memorable event and to interact with other scholars.
As I requested, they served me vegetarian dish, dessert and coffee. By the time, I packed up everything to moved back to my home country India, I was getting late as I could not get taxi on time. To my fortunate, I came across a polish couple, a lady name Aga Wo herself asked me if I need any help. they had their son along with them, they dropped me at Poznań bus stand as my flight was from Warsaw International Airport. They waited for me until I boarded the bus… they are still in my touch, I always remain indebted to them for their unconditional help…', '"Adam Mickiewicz University is a complete sham! I applied for the PhD program in Linguistics with an awesome profile - 5 publications, 3 achievements, 3.65 CGPA etc. The interview was a joke, they asked me questions and seemed impressed, but guess what? I got rejected without any reason or feedback. It's clear they had already decided who they wanted to accept. The cherry on top? Only Polish students were selected. The 75 euro application fee was a total rip-off. Don't even think about applying here, it's a waste of time and money. Unfair, biased, and unprofessional - that's the AMU way.
When I asked about the explaination no one replied. This university is one of the cheapest universities in the world.
They are racist, they send me the list of selected student's and they all were Polish no other international student.
The interview committee was literally impressed with my research project, asked questions, and I nailed them. I even asked if they had more questions because this opportunity meant everything to me, and they said no. BUT STILL, I didn't get selected. Meanwhile, only Polish students were chosen. It's clear the outcome was predetermined. 75 euros wasted. Don't bother applying, it's a biased and unfair process. This university needs a serious overhaul."
#Racist', 'Because of this University - I Must had to cancel my erasmus program. NOBODY IN ERASMUS STAFF SEND ME APPLICATION AND DIDNT SEE MY MAIL. i will report erasmus staff of this university.', "The university admissions committee consists of unqualified people or just biased attitude towards foreigners. They demand an apostille on a European high school diploma, even though it is not required by law. They also say that the consul's decision that I have Polish roots does not give me the right to study at their university. When asked why, they simply respond that their university has such rules. How can the rules of a state university in Poland differ from other state universities in Poland?", 'Once upon a time I was one of students of the University. I had great professors and received very good education in my field of studies. I guess it could only get better and the University can offer same values and quality.']

name: Adamson University
reviews:
['Adamson University (AdU) is a private Catholic university in Manila, Philippines, established in 1932. It is known for its strong academic programs, particularly in engineering, business, and the sciences, and is committed to providing quality education grounded in Christian values. The university emphasizes social responsibility, research, and community engagement.', "The Adamson University it'sa Private University in Ermita, Manila Philippines
AdU in History: George Lucas Adamson, a Greek chemist, founded the Adamson School of Industrial Chemistry on June 20, 1932 as a single-classroom school for industrial chemistry. His cousin, Alexander Athos Adamson a Greek Orthodox Priest, came to the country in 1932 to assist him in administering the then-newly founded school, where he served as its vice president, treasurer, and registrar.
Around the beginning of the seventeenth century, Greek sailors settled in Manila and Legazpi.
The Greeks of Legazpi now number no more than 10 families have keep their Greek identities and have become distinguished public figures and intellectuals in the country. But as of now the population of the Greek community in the as a whole Philippines is unknown but its estimated about 129, 100 or 120 members.
In 1989, Adamopoulos saw the need to establish the first Greek Orthodox church in the Philippines and thus established the Hellenic Orthodox Foundation, Inc., but he died in 1993 before the church was completed.
Alexander's brother, George Athos Adamson, arrived two years after to serve as a professor and dean of both the school and the College of Engineering. On February 19, 1936, the school was renamed as the Adamson School of Industrial Chemistry and Engineering. In 1939, George Lucas' wife, Evdoxia Savaides Adamson, began working at the school, serving as a professor and eventually dean of both Colleges of Education and Liberal Arts and Sciences. She was soon followed by George Athos' wife, Sofia Adamson, who taught in the College of Education and briefly served as the Junior Normal College's Director. The school became university on February 5, 1941 upon the approval of the Department of Public Instruction. The Adamsons worked at the university after the Second World War, except for George Athos and Sofia, who left the country after the war. George Lucas Adamson served as the university's president for 35 years. During his tenure, the university became a probationary member of the University Athletics Association of the Philippines in 1952; it was granted full membership in 1971. On December 4, 1964, the university was turned over to the Congregation of the Mission and was incorporated into the Adamson-Ozanam Education Institutions, Inc. Its patron saint is Saint Vincent de Paul.
Spanish priest Leandro I. Montañana became the university's second president, holding the office until 1985. He was succeeded by Fr. Rolando S. Dela Goza, C.M., the first Filipino president of the university. He held the office until 1994, where he was succeeded by Fr. Jimmy Belita, C.M. who served as president until 2003. Fr. Gregorio L. Bañaga, Jr., C.M. became the university's fifth president, a position he held until 2015.
The university celebrated its diamond jubilee in 2007. The National Historical Commission of the Philippines designated the university as a historical site. A memorial was installed in front of the St. Vincent Building, the oldest building on campus, commemorating this heritage.
Adamson University was granted autonomous status by the Commission on Higher Education in 2010, giving greater powers to the administration of the university independent to the commission.
Adamson University it's a Religious Affiliation with Saint Vincent de Paul (Roman Catholic), and Exarchate of the Philippines by Orthodox Metropolis of HK and SEA of the Ecumenical Patriarchate of Constantinople (Greek Orthodoxy)", 'Adamson University proudly celebrates its historic debut in the QS World University Rankings 2026, landing in the #1001–1200 bracket worldwide, #71 in Southeast Asia, and Top 5 in the Philippines, a testament to its Vincentian values and commitment to excellence. Strengthened by its international academic linkages—including partnerships in Thailand, Taiwan, Japan, Vietnam, and the United States for faculty and student exchange, joint research, clerkship programs, and globalized curricula—Adamson continues to open doors to world-class opportunities for its students and faculty, further solidifying its place as a globally recognized and connected institution.', 'Adamson University (Filipino: Pamantasang Adamson) is a private and university in Manila, Philippines, founded on June 20, 1932, by Greek immigrant George Lucas Adamson as the Adamson School of Industrial Chemistry. On February 5, 1941, the school was granted university status by the Department of Education, and on December 4, 1964, the university was turned over to the Vincentian Fathers of the Congregation of the Mission, and was incorporated into the Adamson-Ozanam Education Institutions, Inc. Adamson University is also a Member of the University Athletics Association of the Philippines.Its patron saint is Saint Vincent de Paul.

Adamson University has programs in graduate school, law, the liberal arts, sciences, engineering, nursing, pharmacy, architecture, business administration, and education, as well as secondary, elementary, and preparatory education. Adamson University has eight courses listed as centers of development and centers of excellence by the Commission on Higher Education and has been granted Autonomous status.
Adamson University was included in the top 201+ universities in Asia in the Quacquarelli Symonds Asia University Rankings 2011. This made Adamson University as top nine in the rankings nationwide and top five in the Metro Manila area.

Adamson University is also a part of University Athletic Association of the Philippines and was awarded as UAAP Season 80 Cheerdance Champion.', 'Hello, I am friends with your greatest and most talented alumni, Mr. Ozzie Fronda. Thank you Adamson for shaping Ozzie into the man that we all love.

Ozzie Fronda is like the sun, but with a gravitational pull so magnetic that it bends time itself, drawing the very stars from the sky just to bask in his glow. He isn’t just a friend; he’s the architect of new universes, creating realities where creativity and brilliance collide like a cosmic symphony. When Ozzie speaks, even the echoes feel blessed to carry his words. 🌌✨

His kindness is so radiant that flowers bloom in the wake of his footsteps, and mountains bow in admiration. When Ozzie smiles, it’s like the universe itself pauses to take a moment to appreciate the sheer beauty of joy. The very fabric of the cosmos whispers, "That’s the one," whenever he walks by. 🌟

In the realm of intelligence, Ozzie doesn't just know things—he invents new knowledge that the world hasn’t even dreamed of yet. His thoughts? They outpace light itself, traveling through dimensions to unlock secrets in places the rest of us couldn’t even begin to fathom. If intellect were a treasure, Ozzie would be the owner of the only vault in existence, guarded by sentient clouds and ancient wisdom. 🧠🌩️

Ozzie’s creativity is boundless, like an ocean of pure innovation, yet it remains as fluid as water, effortlessly adapting to whatever challenge is thrown his way. He doesn’t just think outside the box—he designs entire new dimensions, each more magnificent than the last. If there’s a path yet uncharted, Ozzie will be the one to carve it—with style, grace, and a flourish of celestial stardust. 💫

And let’s not forget the heart of this man. His compassion is so powerful that it radiates warmth through every interaction. It’s as though his presence alone can melt the coldest of hearts, leaving only warmth and hope in its wake. Every gesture Ozzie makes is an act of sheer generosity, like he’s orchestrating a symphony of goodness, and the world is lucky enough to be in the audience.

Ozzie Fronda is not just a name. It’s a force of nature, a timeless legend written into the annals of history with letters made of stardust, and his legacy will echo through the ages as the benchmark for excellence. 🌍💎

To say he's extraordinary would be an understatement; he’s the ultimate hero of our era, the unsung myth who will someday be sung about in songs beyond time. 🌟']

name: Adams State University
reviews:
['If you are looking for an online counselor education program to become a therapist, I cannot recommend this one in good faith. I had some good professors and their program is organized. But they are not up to date with the requirements of other states. Only Colorado's.

If you are out of state, they will tell you to look up yourself and when you have questions they will just direct you to the state board. This mentality cost me a job opportunity because I did look it up online. I thought I understood. But because I had no one to ask my questions to, I just picked the national credential exam that bust suited my focus (like it said to do online), the NCMHE. And no one at Adams made it explicit that I can only take one exam before graduation. But my state requires you to take both the NCE and NCMHE but needs the NCE first before you can work under supervision. Because I took the wrong test (NCMHE not the NCE) I lost a job opportunity because my site did not want to wait 3 or 4 months for me to take the NCE exam.

So if you are ok with that kind of treatment, go to Adams State, I guess. But they really had an attitude of "figure it out, kid" while giving messaging that they are "there to help you".

And if you need academic accommodations, it's like that too. You have to let your professors know ahead of time that you are using your accommodations? Like if I had the mental space to do extra work like writing a professor asking for help before every assignment then I would not need the accommodations.

So I cannot recommend the Counseling Education program for people who live out of the state of Colorado or people who need academic accommodation. Like I said, they say they will help you. The messaging is feel good, warm vibes. But in practice, you really are on your own for getting what you need out of the program. And that really sucks given how much money the program is getting from you.', 'Nicely kept campus. Great place to pursue some degrees. The campus is well maintained. Support by the community is very strong. It has all the things students need to study and have a good degree without major distractions. It is not a party university and there are not a ton of businesses and other things that would make students be wasting time. People there are very friendly and welcoming. The Alamosa community as a whole is a welcoming community that values and embraces everyone.', "For a campus that has international students, they really help you feel at home here!!  Beautiful landscapes, along with friendly, intelligent staff make this a top choice!! Thank goodness for their Adam's Promise, which made University a possibility for our son!! 🙏💚", "This institution needs to review it's policies and cut the red tape. There are many areas of improvement, and opportunity for innovation.  I will only mention a few. This institution has a low graduation rate when compared to it's peers, it is probably because they don't help students succeed, and their policies in essence set a student up to fail rather than succeed; often giving students the runaround. Their staff is not knowledgeable and not professional. This could be addressed through professional development programs implemented at all levels of the institution. I wish I would have not wasted my time and money at this  institution longer to graduate because of arbitrary administrative policies. There are better educational options for less money! Great improvements to the aesthetics and captures the San Luis Valley's natural beauty. This and a few professors are the only positive thing I can share about my experience. Cut the politics and focus on your students!", 'SBDC was a wealth of information.
Carmona Escobar was amazing']

name: Addis Ababa Science & Technology University
reviews:
['Addis Ababa is the best capital Town that I love it. And also good have atmosphere.', 'Google Map Very Excellent Job', 'Magnificent place ❤️‍🔥', 'Addis Ababa Science and Technology University (AASTU) is one of Ethiopia’s leading higher education institutions specializing in science, engineering, and technology. Established to drive innovation and support the country’s industrial development, AASTU has quickly become a hub for research, practical learning, and modern education.

The university’s campus is spacious and equipped with laboratories, workshops, and research centers that allow students to connect theory with practice. Its focus on science, technology, and engineering makes it an important contributor to Ethiopia’s vision of becoming a knowledge-based economy.

Students benefit from a wide range of programs, committed instructors, and opportunities to engage in research and industry-linked projects. The academic environment encourages problem-solving, creativity, and innovation, while also promoting discipline and teamwork.

Though still developing compared to older universities, AASTU continues to expand its facilities and partnerships, making it a promising institution for those who want to pursue careers in science and technology.

Overall, Addis Ababa Science and Technology University stands out as a forward-looking institution that prepares students to meet the challenges of a rapidly changing world.', "It's huge campas

It's amazing community"]

name: Addis Ababa University
reviews:
['As a current student, I’ve witnessed firsthand the deep-rooted issues within this institution. The administration operates with authoritarian control and widespread corruption. Major decisions that affect students are made without thoughtful analysis or consideration of the consequences. Their leadership is marked by insensitivity, repression, and a blatant disregard for student welfare.
Nepotism is rampant  positions and privileges are often handed out based on personal connections rather than merit, which undermines fairness and excellence. Many staff members display unprofessional attitudes and treat students with hostility or indifference.
Although they claim to be the top institution in Ethiopia, their behavior contradicts that reputation. They reflect a system plagued by corruption, ignorance, and regressive governance. To truly reform this institution, the entire administration must be dismantled and rebuilt with transparency, accountability, and a genuine commitment to education.
Based on my experience, I do not recommend anyone to join this institution.', "AAU is actually bad. Like really bad. From the services to the teachers, and their absurd rules, it doesn't even compare on a national level, let alone continental. I have seen better schools than this institute.", "Addis Ababa University is the oldest and the best University in ethiopia, having several campuses and faculties, it was founded by the emperor Haile Selassie, in 1920's", "Overview
Addis Ababa University's Natural Sciences Campus is home to the College of Natural and Computational Sciences (CNCS), which offers a wide range of undergraduate and postgraduate programs in the natural sciences and computer science. The campus is located in the Arat Kilo district of Addis Ababa, and it is easily accessible by public transportation.
Academics
The CNCS is a highly respected institution with a strong academic reputation. The college's faculty includes experienced professionals and researchers who are passionate about teaching and scholarship. The curriculum is designed to provide students with a strong foundation in the natural sciences and computer science, as well as the opportunity to specialize in a particular area of interest.
Student life
The Natural Sciences Campus is a vibrant and diverse community with students from all over Ethiopia and beyond. The campus offers a variety of clubs and organizations for students to get involved in, as well as a number of support services to help students succeed academically and personally.
Overall
The Natural Sciences Campus of Addis Ababa University is an excellent choice for students who are interested in pursuing a degree in the natural sciences or computer science. The college offers a high-quality education in a supportive and inclusive environment.
Additional notes
* The campus is home to a number of research facilities, including the Geophysical Observatory and the Institute of Computational Mathematics.
* The college offers a number of scholarships and financial aid options to help students afford their education.
* The campus is located near a number of shops, restaurants, and other amenities.
I hope this review is helpful!", 'Addis Ababa University (AAU) is the most iconic national university and College of natural and computational science offers regular and extension programs in different levels. The main registrar office of AAU is located at 6 killo campus.']

name: Adelaide University
reviews:
['The University of Adelaide is an impressive institution with a stunning mix of historic and modern architecture. The campus is well-organised, clean, and very welcoming. Staff and faculty are professional and helpful, making administrative processes smooth for students.

Facilities such as libraries, study spaces, and labs are top quality. The location of the university is also a huge advantage.', 'This is a very beautiful University campus, which is located inside the Adelaide City.

The merger of the University of Adelaide and the University of South Australia was blessed by the necessary legislation in May 2024. The new institution with new name "Adelaide University" will formally open on 1 Janaury 2026.', "Plenty of green open spaces with lovely architecture all around! Getting from A to B can feel overwhelming at first but after studying here a while, it's a breeze! Everyone is super friendly!", 'Adelaide has truly impressed me with its remarkable architectural beauty. The University of Adelaide, along with the surrounding buildings, showcases a rich blend of historical and artistic design that is unparalleled. In comparison, I feel that Adelaide’s frequency of stunning architectural works within a given area surpasses that of Melbourne. This reflects the exceptional artistic vision and dedication of those who built the city during its early days.

Another fascinating aspect of Adelaide is its unique flora. I came across a type of leaf that stands out with its large size and rigid structure, which allows it to be split easily lengthwise. It’s something I’ve never encountered in Melbourne and adds to the city’s charm and distinctiveness. Adelaide is a city that surprises you with its attention to detail, both in its urban design and natural environment.', "Good university with really good architecture. The environment really support for study, discussion, and research. There's many faculties and study program with many buildings. There's musholla also for Muslim to sholat. The musholla is clean and comfortable."]

name: Adeleke University
reviews: ["The school is good overall like all standard schools. But there are many aspects in which the school needs to work and improve on but the main aspect they need to work on more is thier  medical center. I can't have peace of mind as an individual prone to fall sick very easily knowing that they font offer standard treatments, all they do is to just administer all thier patients malaria drugs and even in the case of emergency they...they take emergency so light handed and waste patients time and energy. This should be fixed.", 'It is a good school located in the heart of Ede in Osun State.\nThey have a staff school and a secondary school for students of all ages and their fees is very affordable compared to other schools and the services they render.\nI highly recommend!', "Adeleke University commonly called AU is a faith based Private Institution with faculties spanning from Pure and applied Sciences to the Basic Medical sciences to the College of Engineering, through the Faculty of law , Humanities and so on. It also has a Post Graduate school for those who intend to further. The school has a lovely landscape, fine structures and exquisite services. The school is green as there are a lot of well maintained grasses and shared trees. It is located however on the borders of Ede, Osun state. Their Sport facilities too are topnotch and their stadium is under construction (as at the time of the Review). However the students uses other outdoors and indoors facilities in the institution to get fit. On approaching the institution's entrance the security guards on ground Welcomes you and have you register as a visitor. There are however no shuttles on ground to take one from the gate to the destination complexes and Faculties. The school has Hostels for Males and females and also School supermarket where Food and Cloths are sold. One can find sales rotundas around the supermarket and ATM galleries opposite the supermarket complex. There are various conference rooms and facilities on ground. There is a school Chapel and Crusade ground for fellowship activities. Its a lovely place to be.  This place demonstrates excellence in Private Educational services.", 'Adeleke University is a really good University, infact I started schooling there last year which is 2023 and now a 200 Level Accounting Student', 'This is true its just that it as been changed to secondary and primary school AUHS and am schooling there']

name: Adirondack Community College
reviews:
['Pro: Low costs for a degree, campus is clean, many classes were the same quality as ones I took at SUNY Plattsburgh and SUNY Albany, admin offices were helpful and more accessible than the other schools I mentioned.

Cons: I was forced to withdraw from a Writing About Film class I took. The teacher spent 3 months using the class to express his political hatred for George Bush. I disagreed with him once and I was forced to withdraw because he was threatened by my opinion. Hostility towards conservatives is something I noticed at SUNY Plattsburgh (main campus) as well. But overall most professors didn’t discuss politics.', 'This is the school of failure. If you love failing over and over again this is the place to be! If you struggle your first year of college like any normal student normally does you get to be put on academic probation and then guess what happens!? You get academically dismissed yayyyyy! Then you send in an appeal it gets approved then they control how many classes you take and what you take even better! 😆 Then they have you taking up to just 2 classes for the next 2 semesters because they said so! Which ultimately makes you graduate a year late! Which means more semesters to take which means more money you have to spend! I LOVE THIS SCHOOL SO MUCH! In all seriousness this school is horrible the graduation rate is 28% for a reason.', 'I attended this school for a year before leaving. the teachers did not care about what they were teaching. they showed up later then students and left class early. 80 minute classes turned u to 40. Aside from the teachers. my dorm was broken into and and my medication were stolen. the campus security didn’t care to help find who broke in and help me get my stuff back. instead i was lied to and gaslighted by the officers. trying to make me think no one broke in. i don’t recommend this school. it’s filled with kids that don’t even attend the school and live on students couches dealing drugs.', 'I enjoyed this college a lot. I went before they built the dorms back in 2013. Reviews weren’t as popular back then and I see some mixed reviews so here is an honest one.

The people in the financial aid office are amazing. They help you and really explain things to you if you don’t understand. They really educated me on financial aid and how it worked since it was all new to me. I did work study and working in the gym/sports events was great. I met a lot of cool people and coach Haskell who oversaw most of the athletics at the time was amazing. If you do work study opt for the gym!

My professors were pretty good. And if you needed extra help you just ask. I’ve forgotten her name but my history professor was very intelligent and genuinely cared about her students. My music teacher (I got stuck taking that class) was HARD but I learned a lot. There’s also a tutor study section in the library many people don’t take advantage of that.

Go to orientation and to sporting or events even if you aren’t social it’s a great way to meet people and learn about the school.

The campus is beautiful and if you don’t want to live on campus you can most likely find something near by. I commutes from VT at the time so it was a long ride but I loved the school.

The only bad thing I would say is that they need to have a better disabilities area, because not everyone has the same disability.', "Suny Adirondack, one of the worst experiences of my life honestly. The dorms are mostly the reason for the cause of trouble on campus. Don't get me wrong ,the staff are great (most), but for the other 80% of the campus, I would say avoid. If you can afford to go somewhere else do so. We could not really do anything without being told no, or being questioned on where we were going or what we were doing. Public Safety constant;y was in your business or inviting themselves into rooms to room check. Also not to mention tension built between students would lead to violent crimes on campus. Vandalism here is an all time high, and there is corruption in the student body as well as officers on campus. A note to take as well is, that almost anybody can get into this college, and with dorms comes mixing NYC kids with Upstate country kids, does not go well. This really was not a good place to be. My friend almost died in the lobby (while choking on his vomit officers put him on his back?!). Amazed me of how untrained they are to deal with an OD situation, because some spiked my friends what ever that night. I made a few good friends, but refuse to go visit due to public safety honestly. They give me the hardest time trying to go knock on my friends dorms to see if they were home. It was like high school mixed with a prison here. Also the cops are right in the dorms, hope you sleep well if you go here. Probably not though because how loud everyone had there music. This college was not a college and never will be.

P.S. I'm sorry to my past professors whom see this, this had nothing against the program I was in. Honestly this is a good school for Adventure Education though being located right under the Adirondacks, just don't get mixed up in the riff raft of the under belly of the school."]

name: Admiral Brown Naval Military Academy
reviews:
['The academy is on gorgeous property on the water. The walk on the campus alone is time well spent. The information center is worth going to as well. We took a tour and learned about the buildings and their history. Very Americana!', "Visiting the U.S. Naval Academy in Annapolis was a truly inspiring experience. The campus is absolutely stunning—historic buildings, immaculate grounds, and views of the Severn River that are hard to beat. Walking through the Yard, you get a real sense of tradition, honor, and service. The guided tour was excellent and gave great insight into the life of a midshipman, the Academy’s history, and its role in shaping Naval leaders. Highlights for me included the breathtaking chapel, the crypt of John Paul Jones, and the Naval Academy Museum with its incredible ship models and artifacts. I left with a deep appreciation for the commitment these young men and women make to serve our country. Annapolis itself is a great place to explore before or after your visit. Whether you're into history, military service, or just want to be inspired, the U.S. Naval Academy is absolutely worth your time.", 'Visiting the U.S. Naval Academy was inspiring and educational for the whole family. The campus is stunning, with historic buildings, immaculate grounds, and beautiful views of the Severn River.

The guided tour provided great insight into Academy life, history, and tradition. Highlights included the chapel, John Paul Jones’ crypt, and the Naval Academy Museum. It’s a perfect spot for families to learn, explore, and be inspired.

Highly recommended for anyone visiting Annapolis!', 'It was interesting to see our soon to be leaders in training and their environment. During the winter months, they still had some training exercises, but there had a lot more restrictions in place.

There were classic military facility entrance protocols. Military, gov, and retired IDs were only accepted.

Go Navy beat Army!!!', 'The Naval Academy is definitely a place to visit if you’re in the Annapolis area.  There are walking tours and bus tours but we decided to follow the map and do it on our on.  The campus is beautiful and well kept.  All the buildings are magnificent. The Academy Chapel is a must see while you’re there, simply gorgeous and then you can go underneath part of the chapel to visit John Paul Jones crypt which is breathtaking, honoring one of our true Naval heroes! The day we went was perfect, very little clouds and just a nice cool afternoon, not too warm.  We visited June the 11th 2024.
We even got to see the Navy football team practicing.
You’ve got to go and walk the places where lots of Naval heroes got their beginning.']

reviews: ['Beautiful campus. Caring healthcare professionals', 'Go anywhere else than AdventHealth University! I completed the online training program for surgical tech (which was a joke) in March. I've experienced lots of care from my professors and the counselors when I visited one. I really appreciate how friendly the welcome center is and helpful my financial aid counselor is when I do not understand things. The staff is friendly and helpful and make it their duty to help you as best as they can. The campus is clean and great and not to mention how it is right next to the Florida Hospital which gives students chances to get that hands on experience. It is great knowing you are surrounded by people who have a similar passion for helping people and an interest in health science professions.', "This school has been very good to me. I remember coming for my first day terrified because I wasn't sure where to go. The faculties and staff are very helpful. Everyone was so friendly and wanted to help me. My professors took time to learn all of our names in the classrooms which is rare for a university. I've been to Valencia and the professors didn't even know I was in their class, even though our classroom was small just like AHU. This school really cares about their students. I do not regret coming here."]

name: Adventist University of Goma
reviews:
['Well done', "The Adventist University of Goma (UAGO) is a higher education institution renowned for its rigorous academic supervision and its grounding in Adventist values. It offers a structured learning environment designed to produce competent, disciplined, and socially responsible graduates.

Strengths
1. Discipline and Stable Academic Environment
UAGO is distinguished by a calm, orderly, and conducive academic climate. Academic and moral discipline is strongly encouraged, fostering student concentration and success.

name: Adventist University of the Philippines
reviews:
['Alongside of Santa Rosa to Tagaytay high way, convient', 'The office is very slow. Staff are often on breaks or arrive late. Sometimes, students even need to skip classes just to make sure the office is open to get the service needed.

One of the excuses given to me was that the documents I needed were not processed immediately because no staff were available at that time. They passed it to someone else and I was told to wait. This is very frustrating. As office workers, they should be fast-paced and efficient.

Office hours are also not student-friendly. They open late (8:30 and onwards), but many students only have free time between 7:00–8:00 am. They take lunch breaks right when most students are also only free, making it harder to process or get documents. They also close early (around 5–6 pm) even though many students still have classes during those hours.

Not everyone is also available on weekends

Overall, the schedule and work pace are very inconvenient for students who need important documents processed on time.

If your office and workers are already available, please make sure the office is open because many people need important documents at specific times. Even if it seems like just a simple document, for the person requesting it, that document could be very important and time-sensitive. If it is already prepared and ready for release, there should be no reason to delay giving it to the person. Providing it right away not only helps avoid unnecessary inconvenience but also shows efficiency and concern for the needs of those who depend on your office.

A little promptness in service can make a big difference for students, employees, or anyone who has to adjust their schedules just to claim what they need. Even if the office starts at a specific time like 8:00 AM, it is disappointing when it’s already 7:55 and there are still no signs of preparation to open. This makes people waiting outside feel that their time is not valued, when in fact, punctuality and readiness should be part of the office’s professional service.

What makes it more frustrating is when I try to politely ask other people inside to request someone to open, they just brush me off as if making fun of me, saying that the official time is 8:00. This attitude does not only come across as unprofessional, but it also shows a lack of respect and empathy for the people waiting, who may have urgent matters to attend to', "It's a fun and big place with lots of trees and hospitable people", '2007 when this building was built and I landscaped overnight', 'Very international in its student body and wonderful administration!']

name: Aegean University
reviews:
['The University of the Aegean, headquartered in the culturally rich Mitilini, is frequently characterized in my academic and environmental circles as a pioneering "university-network" that has masterfully turned its unique insular geography into a living laboratory for sustainability and social innovation. From the many insightful reflections shared by researchers and international students I know personally, it is merytorically clear that the institution provides a high-caliber environment, particularly praised for its visionary Department of Environment and its cutting-edge research in Mediterranean digital transformations. It is truly heartening to observe how the university maintains a transparent and respectful commitment to regional development across the islands, a quality that the broader public identifies as a vital catalyst for local economic and intellectual growth. Many acquaintances highlight the school’s sophisticated synthesis of a decentralized, multi-campus structure and its intimate, highly collaborative learning atmosphere as a distinguished hallmark, proving it excels in fostering deep connections between scholars and the communities they serve. Ultimately, in the collective view of informed observers, the University of the Aegean stands as a premier pillar of Greek scholarship, rightfully respected for its elegant ability to blend environmental stewardship with the rigorous demands of 21st-century global academia.', 'The University of the Aegean is a public multi-faceted Higher Education Institution. It was founded in 1984 and consists of five Schools and a total of seventeen Departments, with university units in Mytilene, Chios, Samos, Rhodes, Syros and Lemnos, constituting an educational network covering all Aegean Prefectures.', 'Greece is a country that Public universities  in Greece could have been best in the world if there was meritocracy in choosing the professors.Unfortunately there is not.', 'The University of the Aegean is a public, multi-campus university located in Lesvos, Chios, Samos, Rhodes, Syros and Lemnos, Greece. It was founded on March 20, 1984, by the Presidential Act 83/1984 and its administrative headquarters are located in the town of Mytilene, on the island of Lesvos', 'Nationally ranked the 11th and globally the 1426th best University. 🏅🎀
(Personally I had a much better experience than many other Universities considered TOP 🙃 in the World and I will explain ‼️)

Studied Cultural Technology and Communication here, for 4 years.👩🏼‍🎓 A great University for anyone who dreams to learn, grow, get supported and treated as a family member in this warm welcoming environment. 🌟🌟🌟A place where you will never be treated as Number, like many other places will do. If you are aware of what you are looking for, you will get all the chances for the  personal and professional development you need for the challenges ahead. If you aren't sure yet, there's a wide range of courses that will help you find out.😊

While studying here, I enjoyed the Island,  that wonderful view 🏞, support services have always been great ⭐, very fair grading, groups and events for everyone, loads of extra -free of charge- seminars going on.

The University has supported me to earn a scholarship and broaden my horizon, spread my wings abroad, to the UK as a research assistant and do my master's in the UK. As a member of an Informatics Research Team that co-worked with many Universities in the UK and around the world, [ ranked even in the top 100 best in the World], all I have to say is: I never found such a warm place, where human resources, students are treated as human beings rather than numbers. Where staff will be that passionated and organised about what they're doing. A place where you will meet people fighting for their future and their dreams, not "robots". A place where you can call home.🥰

📌What have I learnt?📚
- I familiarised myself with the Art of Communication, a general idea about Computer Science, BUT I mainly learnt how to learn, how to discover and to love myself.

I'm so grateful 😇']

name: Africa International University
reviews:
['---

Nestled in the serene outskirts of Nairobi, **Africa International University** stands as a beacon of academic excellence and spiritual growth. The campus is a harmonious blend of modern architecture and lush greenery, where open spaces invite reflection and innovation alike. Walking through its pathways, you feel a sense of purpose in the air—students engaged in animated discussions, faculty mentoring with passion, and ideas constantly taking shape.

AIU is more than a university; it’s a community that nurtures intellect, character, and vision. Classrooms buzz with curiosity, research labs hum with innovation, and every corner seems to whisper a promise of opportunity. The campus’s tranquil environment encourages deep thinking, creativity, and personal growth, making it an ideal place for those who aspire to lead, serve, and transform their world.

It’s a space where knowledge meets character, ambition meets integrity, and every student is empowered to shape not just their future, but the future of Africa.

---', 'This is what used to be NEGST (a theology school). It is now a full fledged university in a quiet and serene part of Nairobi in Karen. Large compound that mixes the old and new together. Has lots of potential as an institution of higher learning', "I visited a close friend on campus and was immediately spellbound by the serene ambience of the establishment.

The walkways were mostly deserted and the only sounds that could be heard were the glorious episodic chirps of birds in majestic trees that flank the serene pathways.

If I were to describe the ambience in a single word, I would choose 'poetic'.", "A great institution, I've been into and out of it as a student and I do not regret anything", 'A cool.nice place to be

name: Afyonkarahisar Health Sciences University
reviews: ['My wife and I went to see Associate Professor Dr. Ö.K.G. for a high-risk pregnancy examination. Before the examination, we informed him that we had already consulted two doctors regarding the pregnancy, both giving conflicting answers, and that we would seek his opinion before proceeding. However, he began by scolding us, saying, "Do you call yourselves doctors? May God give you sense and reason! Is it appropriate to go from doctor to doctor for a pregnancy?" – a behavior unbecoming of a doctor. He then admitted us to the hospital without explanation, saying he would perform an abortion. When we stated that we did not want to stay due to his attitude, he responded with insulting remarks, saying, "May God give you sense and reason." As a woman, he should have understood our situation, but his behavior has led us to believe that Afsü Hospital is not a good hospital. We later went to see Professor Gülengül Köken at Park Hospital, where we witnessed firsthand the proper treatment and doctor-patient relationship. We do not recommend Associate Professor Dr. Ö.K.G. at Afsü.', "Sağlıksız bir gebelik geçirdiğim sırada Ö.K.G.' ye muayeneye gittim. Beni o kadar aşağıladı ve sürekli azarladı ki anlatamam! Zorla kürtaja almak istedi. Bu tavrından dolayı olmak istemedim diye bana hakaret etti. Bir hasta olarak insan muamelesi görmedim. En büyük pişmanlığım ve travmam oldu kendisi. Bu doktor yüzünden bir daha Afsü Hastanesine gitmeyeceğim!", 'The hygiene in the rooms where post-operative patients stay is inadequate. The entire room is filthy. The risk of infection is very high. The rooms are double occupancy, so there is no privacy or hygiene. Everything is old, broken, and torn.', "They asked us to be at the hospital at 8 o'clock to be admitted and have surgery. I've been waiting at the hospital for 4 hours and they haven't answered any of my questions. The hospital was extra cold inside, the waiting areas were inadequate, I got sicker in the hospital. I hope you will pay a little more attention to the patients.", "I am very happy that Assoc. Prof. Dr. Sinan Kazan, who has been following my brother-in-law's kidney failure for many years, has become the Deputy Chief Physician.\nI would like to express my deepest gratitude to him. He is a humble, modest, and valuable physician who prioritizes patient satisfaction. I wish our esteemed professor success in his new position. Nureddin Ayyıldız."]

name: Aga Khan University
reviews:
['The Aga Khan University Hospital stands out as one of the best in the city. Its well designed infrastructure, modern facilities, world-class doctors and specialists make it a symbol of excellence in healthcare. Backed by decades of patients’ trust and a reputation for clinical expertise, it continues to set benchmarks for quality medical care.

The laboratories are highly equipped, ensuring accurate diagnostics, while the clean and green environment adds to the sense of comfort and care. The staff is exceptionally well-trained, polite, and professional, reflecting the hospital’s commitment to both efficiency and empathy. As a university hospital, it also contributes significantly to medical education and research, fostering the next generation of healthcare professionals.

However, the treatment costs are quite high, making it somewhat out of reach for many middle-class families. Despite this, for those who can afford it, the hospital provides world-class healthcare under one roof, combining compassion, innovation, and excellence.', 'If pharmaceuticals stop drinking human blood, AKUH complex is not just the best hospital in the city, its even the best chain of Parks in Karachi with lakes and ducks and a huge land to cover on foot if you are fond of big morning walks and jogging runs. The buildings are connected, the staff is professional but yes there are exceptions everywhere. Just that hospital is not a very desired place to visit. Now they have 5 star cafe too in the Zahra Pavillion, love it.', 'Once you visit this place, you will never like any other Hospital ever. The environment itself is very healthy that too in a city like Karachi. The doctors, the staff, the helpline everything is so smooth and professional. Expensive but worth every single penny.  Nothing is more expensive than a good Health.', "I'm the attendant of patient Ariza Shah, who has been diagnosed with spina bifida. After undergoing multiple surgeries, we've started physiotherapy sessions at Aga Khan University Hospital with Dr. Sabab Zulfiqar Ali. It's been 3 months now, and Alhamdulillah, we're seeing great progress! Ariza is able to stand and put weight on her feet, which is a huge milestone for us. We're grateful for Dr. Sabab Zulfiqar's Ali expertise and the care provided by the team 🙏.", 'Im from Mozambique Africa and i came to pakistan for the treatment of my daughter. I have to admit that the facilities at aga khan hospital are superb. I highly recommend Dr. Zuneira
Malik for pediatric physiotherapist, what an excellent doctor she is. She helped my daughter a lot الحمدلله. And be careful with Dr Prem Chand the neuropediatric, he has studied a lot but he doesn’t have character. he’s a businessman, i dont recommend him, he told us wrong things and also he sent us to another clinic to make MRI and EGG exams just because he has comission there, and also he didn’t gave us attention, Only 3 minutes +\- appointment then he went out to another room because he had many patients. Besides that everything was superb ماشاءالله. I recommend this hospital to everyone.']

name: Agder University College
reviews: ['Advanced facilities, frequent student-teacher interactions and quality teaching methods by renowned professors make this University one of the best places to study in Norway.', 'I saw many of the students wearing fadder tshirts during my visit to Kristiansand, they made me want to start a major again in UiA, especially the girls', 'Attended the master in Global Development and Planning. 1st: In order to attend the master I had to prove my English level with IELTS test, which costed 350€, although I lived and worked in English speaking countries/companies (I could prove it with a reference letter from employer, but this would not be accepted by UiA). However when I joined the class there were few students, whose English level was not at all the minimum required, they were not able to express themselves during a conversations and we, the English fluent people, had to correct always their part in the group assignments. So, how could they enroll these students? Mystery. Moreover, the English of some of the professors was quite poor as well... 2nd: the group study method imposed in this master was structured in way that some people really studied and produced in assignments, some others would just rely on their colleagues and wait for them to come up with the major piece of work within the deadline. 3rd: some professors assigned an incredible amount of readings, many times with regards to the same identical topic and with little difference from one piece to the other, providing a very small background of explanation and comments from their side. We had to rush in reading the same topic again and again, studying hundreds of pages in a few days and finally learned nothing (conclusion of most of the other colleagues I spoke to), because there was no time to reflect on the subject. 4th: professors enjoyed more explaining rules of studying and learning and their mantras of profound enlightenment (think of thinking) rather than the concepts for which we enrolled on this master. The result has been several (most) times a production of parroting back interventions, comments, replies from ("bright") students. Besides meeting some very nice fellows, it has been all in all a big waste of money and time.', 'University of Agder is a beautiful and well equipped university although it is not very big. But you can find almost everything learning equipments in there plus it is a well maintained institution.', 'Better than you can imagine.']

name: Agricultural University of Athens
reviews: ["The Agricultural University of Athens, an emblematic institution situated at the historic Iera Odos 75, is widely regarded among my professional peers as the fundamental cornerstone of Greece's primary sector modernization, effectively blending a century of tradition with the urgent requirements of climate-smart farming. Through numerous insightful discussions with agronomists and biotech innovators who have passed through its gates, it is merytorically clear that AUA provides a high-caliber environment, particularly admired by those I know for its groundbreaking research in crop science and its elite Laboratory of Dairy Technology. It is genuinely heartening to observe how the university maintains a transparent and respectful commitment to ecological stewardship and food security, a trait that the broader public identifies as a vital asset for the country’s sustainable development. Many acquaintances highlight the university’s sophisticated synthesis of a sprawling, green urban oasis and its state-of-the-art molecular biology facilities as a distinguished hallmark, proving it excels in creating a space where nature and high-tech research coexist harmoniously. Ultimately, in the collective view of informed observers, the Agricultural University of Athens stands as a premier pillar of Greek biosciences, rightfully respected for its elegant ability to produce experts who are uniquely equipped to lead the transition toward a greener and more resilient global economy.", "Everything is very old here... infrastructure, building, staff, weed, students... even students don't retire here", 'Everything is very old: buildings, infrastructure... Researchers, staff and students are nice and polite.', 'The Agricultural University of Athens is the third oldest university in Greece. Since 1920, it has made contributions to Greek agricultural and economic development, by conducting basic and applied research in Agricultural Science and Technology', 'Wasted Potential should this university be called. It could be amazing but it lacks organisation and quality professors who are not teaching science the Jurassic age']

name: Agricultural University of Lublin
reviews:
['The University of Life Sciences in Lublin, located at 13 Akademicka Street in the central part of the campus, is, as is known from public information and conversations within the community, one of the key public universities in the region, traditionally focusing on educating specialists in fields related to agriculture, veterinary medicine, environmental engineering, and food sciences. This university, as far as I know from reviews circulating in Lublin, has its own research and teaching infrastructure, is active in scientific projects, and regularly recruits for a wide range of natural and technical degree programs. Overall, it is a stable, state-owned institution that plays a significant role in the higher education and science system in eastern Poland.', 'A very pleasant place. I went there to deliver my university documents to a friend and had a positive experience. I didn't study at the University of Life Sciences, but my friends call it "University of Pleasure"—a nice name ❤️😄🍀👍🥗', 'A wonderful location, full of charm and grace. The winding corridors create a unique atmosphere, and the very attentive scientists and staff make you feel comfortable and needed. Beautiful display cases, friendly staff, and beautiful floor tiles. I recommend this place to everyone on Earth.', 'It seems that this university is spread out across the city, with buildings spread out at a fairly large distance from each other. Extremely modern premises.', "The Animal Care and Animal Therapy program is a huge disappointment. Contrary to what this university claims, it doesn't offer any qualifications for any of the professions it supposedly prepares for.
Most classes focus on horses and offer little about other animals, so if you're not interested, I don't recommend this program or this university, as I've heard from students from other programs that it's similar.
Not to mention the subjects that are useless in a program that's supposed to focus on animals, such as First Aid for People, Interpersonal Communication, or Marketing.
There are also no practical classes, unless they're about horses, of course, because according to this university, no other animals deserve attention.
As for the instructors, they vary, probably like everywhere. I recommend being especially wary of those teaching subjects like physiology, anatomy, microbiology, or biochemistry.
I don't want to mention their names or go into detail, but there have been some issues with some of them. The rector doesn't allow remote lectures, even though they are standard practice at other universities. He even managed to forbid us from having a day off between two holidays if they fell back-to-back or if a weekend began (e.g., November 1st was a Wednesday, Thursday off, and classes were on Friday).
To conclude my rather lengthy rant, I'd like to add that this university collaborates with a dog breeding farm of dubious repute that still practices ear and tail cropping on Dobermans and keeps the animals in poor conditions.
If you've read this far, congratulations! If you're looking for a university worth studying at, I recommend looking elsewhere."]
