name: Agriculture University, Kota
reviews:
['Agriculture University, Kota (AUK), located in Rajasthan, India, is a prominent institution dedicated to agricultural education, research, and extension. Established in 2013, the university aims to enhance agricultural productivity and sustainability through innovative teaching, research, and outreach programs. It plays a crucial role in addressing the agricultural challenges of Rajasthan, a state known for its arid and semi-arid regions.

AUK offers undergraduate, postgraduate, and doctoral programs in agriculture, horticulture and forestry through its 3 constituent and 13 affiliated colleges. The university is committed to equip students with modern agricultural knowledge and practical skills aligned to NEP 2020 to meet the evolving demands of the sector.

Research is a key focus at AUK which is accomplished by one ARS and two ARSS with faculty and students engaged in projects related to crop improvement, water conservation, organic farming, and climate-resilient agriculture. The university collaborates with 29 national and international organizations to promote sustainable farming practices. It also extends support to farmers through training programs, field demonstrations, and advisory services, helping them adopt innovative techniques for better yields and income.

The university has well-equipped laboratories, experimental farms, and modern infrastructure to facilitate high-quality education and research. Its emphasis on practical learning, along with strong industry linkages, makes it a significant contributor to agricultural development in the region. It’s one of the constituent college i.e. College of Horticulture and Forestry Jhalawar has emerged a leader in promoting protected horticulture including hydroponics in the state on commercial scale.

Overall, Agriculture University, Kota, is a vital institution for Rajasthan’s agricultural growth, fostering innovation and preparing skilled professionals to tackle the challenges of modern agriculture. Its commitment to education, research, and farmer welfare ensures a lasting impact on the state’s agrarian economy. The landscaping of administrative block of the university is very beautiful and impressive which increases the beauty of campus manyfold.', 'Hadoti area is high cultivation area in rajasthan state the agriculture University kota is most important role to increase agriculture production and research or development of new variety of the crops in the region based tech. Achievement of the university.', 'Good University for agriculture in Kota', 'Great research work in agriculture. 15-16 All India Coordinated Research Projects.  College of Agriculture, Kota is also here.', 'Rajasthan best university and training facility for food processing and value addition sector. Best facilities fir tranies.']

name: AgroSup Dijon
reviews:
["Excellent experience, many wonderful, enriching, and interesting encounters. A fantastic atmosphere fueled by a great sense of community.

Negative points: A largely incompetent administration that abuses its hierarchical position to destroy one of the school's only strengths: student life. In three years, it was a mere shadow of its former self, and the school now only uses the three events it authorizes to boast about supporting students. It's sad, and it doesn't look like things will improve with the school merger.

Ultimately, the engineering spirit is also instilled through student life, and they don't seem to understand that, which is a real shame...", "I completed a BTSA AP (Advanced Vocational Diploma in Applied Arts) remotely about two years ago (in 2022), and the support was minimal, with a very unintuitive interface.

- The courses lacked specific methodologies.

- When I encountered a problem during my internship, just two days before its completion, due to my supervisors' failure to adhere to the internship agreement, the director, while very friendly, advised me to apologize (and I wasn't paid).

- Most of the documents related to working in an applied arts company were outdated.

- Regarding the exams, several students were misled (for example, being told to create a slideshow when it was prohibited on the day of the economics exam).

Therefore, in my opinion, the remote learning system for this type of training was inadequate. And I don't know if it is now.", 'Some rooms are a bit old, but others have been completely renovated. Memories and interesting encounters from the training courses I attended.', 'An amazing school with an incredible student life!! 😀', 'A student association that is overwhelming due to its organization and lack of open-mindedness.']

Few signs

Bad roads
Unclear routes
Unplanned and unsupervised system', 'Jundishapur University was one of the oldest and most important scientific centers in the world in ancient times, located in the city of Jundishapur in the Khuzestan province of Iran. The university was founded during the Sassanid era and was known as a prominent scientific and cultural center in the world.
History and Significance:
Jundishapur University reached its peak during the Sassanid era, especially during the reign of Shapur II and Khosrow Anushirvan.
The university was a gathering center for scientists, physicians, and philosophers from all over the world.
Jundishapur University was active in various fields, including medicine, philosophy, astronomy, and mathematics, and had a significant impact on the advancement of science and knowledge in the world.
This university is known as one of the first organized educational centers in the world and played an important role in the transfer of knowledge from Greece, India, and Iran to the Islamic world.
Many of the medical findings and knowledge produced at this university were later transferred to Europe by Muslims.
Location and City of Jundishapur:
The city of Jundishapur was located near the present-day city of Dezful in Khuzestan Province.
The city was built by order of Shapur II and quickly became an important cultural and scientific center due to its convenient geographical location and access to trade routes.
Today, the remains of this ancient city can be seen near Dezful.
Summary:']

Today, the remains of this ancient city can be seen near Dezful.
Summary:']

Unplanned and unsupervised system', 'Jundishapur University was one of the oldest and most important scientific centers in the world in ancient times, located in the city of Jundishapur in the Khuzestan province of Iran. The university was founded during the Sassanid era and was known as a prominent scientific and cultural center in the world.
History and Significance:
Jundishapur University reached its peak during the Sassanid era, especially during the reign of Shapur II and Khosrow Anushirvan.
The university was a gathering center for scientists, physicians, and philosophers from all over the world.
Jundishapur University was active in various fields, including medicine, philosophy, astronomy, and mathematics, and had a significant impact on the advancement of science and knowledge in the world.
This university is known as one of the first organized educational centers in the world and played an important role in the transfer of knowledge from Greece, India, and Iran to the Islamic world.
Many of the medical findings and knowledge produced at this university were later transferred to Europe by Muslims.
Location and City of Jundishapur:
The city of Jundishapur was located near the present-day city of Dezful in Khuzestan Province.
The city was built by order of Shapur II and quickly became an important cultural and scientific center due to its convenient geographical location and access to trade routes.
Today, the remains of this ancient city can be seen near Dezful.
Summary:']

name: Aichi Bunkyo University
reviews:
["It was my first time at the university, but I think it was great to be surrounded by greenery and nature. I was able to make about 4-5 friends, which was great! What particularly interested me was when I found out that there were many classes related to my favorite subjects, history and English, and when I actually attended classes, I thought it was so fun! Not only is the scenery great, but the transportation is also convenient, with buses available for people coming from far away, which is very convenient, and I think the best thing about classes is that if you have any questions, you can easily ask the lecturers!
I'm no longer at this university, but the fact that the teachers were so cheerful and welcoming is proof of that.", 'I studied here for three years. As expected, more than half of the students were foreign students, so there were many cultural exchange activities. If you are studying, Aichi Bunkyo University is a good choice.', "It's an F-rank school.

The transportation system is poor, and the facilities aren't great.

The toilets are Japanese-style. Overall, it's aging.", 'There seems to be a high proportion of Chinese students.', "It's a good school."]

name: Aichi Bunkyo Women's College
reviews: ['I went and it was a failure', "That's wonderful.", 'The shuttle bus driver had a bad attitude.\n\nYou should avoid this university.']

name: Aichi Gakuin University
reviews:
["Beautiful campus. The staff are so nice. There's a big parking lot.", 'I accompanied my son to his entrance ceremony on April 1, 2025.
I remember how beautiful the cherry blossoms were.
The day was bustling with many new students.
I took this photo after the ceremony had finished without any problems.', 'I came here to check out the recommended entrance exam venue. It takes about 40 minutes directly from the Meitetsu Bus Center near JR Nagoya Station without changing buses. The fare is 560 yen one way. It may be easier for people coming from other regions to understand than changing to the Meitetsu bus from Fujigaoka Station on the Higashiyama Line of the subway.

The site is vast and it is quite a distance from getting off the bus to the exam venue.

If I pass the exam for the faculty I want, I will be attending the Meijo Park Campus in Nagoya City, so I thought it would be easier if I could take the exam at the campus where the faculty I want to go is located, rather than the Nisshin Campus.

2023.4.1
Entrance Ceremony', 'There is ample parking, so you can rest assured.', 'All of the students were kind and well-mannered. It was a wonderful campus, and I felt it was perfect for the young people who will lead the future.
I hope you will devote your most precious youth to your studies.
You are the ones who will carve out the future.
I hope you will make full use of our excellent facilities and push forward at the cutting edge of the world.
I believe that only a handful of students are able to use this campus.
To all of you well-mannered university students,
please do not rest on your laurels and study hard.
Your future is bright.

You cannot survive alone. You will lead the way for humanity, paving the way for a world where children and the elderly can live comfortably.

Enjoy a life that benefits all humanity, and become people who are appreciated by people all over the world.

You are a treasure of Nagoya and a treasure of Japan.

Please lead the Japanese people! 😊
Aichi Gakuin University students were all wonderful.']

name: Aichi Gakusen University
reviews: ["I used it as a TOEIC exam venue! \nThe campus is very clean, the atmosphere is great, and it's a comfortable university. The lounge in particular has a lovely atmosphere, making it a great place to relax. ✨In the winter, the heating makes it very warm and comfortable!!", "I used it at the TOEIC test center.\nThe TOEIC test guide said that coming by car was prohibited, and I wasn't sure if bicycles or motorcycles were allowed. I went there by motorcycle just in case, and found that bicycles and motorcycles were allowed.", 'Quiet environment and open campus', "Last week's Children's Festival was fun. As a future nursery teacher, it was full of fun handmade events that captured the hearts of children, and my daughter had a great time.", 'We will be holding a school festival on October 24th.']

name: Aichi Institute of Technology
reviews:
['The campus and buildings are very beautiful, and the location is lovely and surrounded by greenery.', 'I attended the school festival alone. I know several alumni from here.

① I'm the same age as the player who was drafted by the Chunichi Dragons and hit three home runs in one game at Nagoya Stadium. When I graduated from high school and started working in 1989 (Heisei 1), several members of the baseball team came to work the night shift at the Nonami factory for some reason. Even back in university, my colleagues would say, "Shio-chan, this guy is definitely going pro, you should befriend him." There was even a guy who was dating the daughter of former manager Takagi, whose parents were both former professional baseball players.

② Several members of the production technology department at my second company.

③ Another person in the technology department at a different company who holds a first-class molding technician certification.

I don't keep in touch with any of them now, but some people look down on them depending on the university. That's just the opinion of people who don't know the realities of the workplace. Back then, there were people who boasted about being able to use office computers, but now, computer-based desk work is so versatile that you don't need those skills.

Those who know the hardships of the workplace are truly considerate of others.


I went to the school festival alone, but next time I'd like to go with a group.', 'When you join the electrical engineering department, you will receive strict instruction on everything from how to write equal signs to how to write numbers and how to write tables correctly and neatly!', "I won't say what year I graduated, but I'm in the Electrical Engineering Department.
I would be honored if current students and those considering enrolling in the future read this.

Freshman year is a foundational course (mathematics and physics) designed to eliminate gaps in high school. However, Wednesday first and second periods of Programming and Electrical Circuits I are required, but they are hellish. Many people are killed. Make friends.
If you do full-time independent studies as a freshman, your senior year will be much easier. If you want to find a girlfriend, abandon your first, second, and third years and leave it to your senior year. Freshman year is all about making friends, getting a stable part-time job, and full-time independent studies. To make friends, there are classes that resemble physical education, so participate in those. The key is whether you can do it there or not.
The university festival executive committee will invite you. If you join because it looks fun and you think you might make friends, you'll be disappointed. Simply put, it's a part-time job that doesn't pay. Some people join for the sake of sharing their stories during their job hunt, but there's no need to go out of your way to join this vicious, black executive committee. Part-time work is plenty, so don't worry and work hard.

Second-year classes are an advanced version of the basic classes taught in first-year. You won't be able to advance to third-year unless you seriously study subjects like electricity and magnetism, analog circuits, and digital circuits.
Physics experiments are long and require multiple reports, but they're like practice for third-year student experiments. The professors are strict but also kind, so if you work hard, it's not a problem. Also, make friends.
Third-year is hell. You can still catch up on job hunting in the second half of your studies. However, student experiments begin, and there are three types of experiments (I, II, and III) with five themes each, for a total of 15 experiments, for a total of 30 experiments combined in the first and second semesters. The average number of submissions per year for people around me is around 230. Be prepared and put in the effort for a year. It's a good idea to have friends.

Fourth-year is easy for those who worked hard and earned credits in their first and second years, but it's tough for those who didn't. This is true at any university, but the biggest obstacle in fourth-year is choosing a lab. The credits are the same, but depending on the lab, you might only need to attend once or twice a week, or four times a week. Some require three or four hours, while others require around 12 hours.

The problem is that students aren't selected based on their grades, but rather on the equality of their grades across all labs. This often means you can't get into the lab of your choice. Improve your luck by visiting shrines, etc.

Job hunting depends on a person's potential, so make sure you have plenty of things to talk about with adults. Career centers are nice, but incompetent. They only talk about things like the Barnum effect. Don't rely on them.

PS
About the facilities
7-Eleven and Kinokuniya are very helpful.

However, one female employee at 7-Eleven has always had a bad attitude. Everyone I talk to agrees.

Kinokuniya offers 20% off stationery and 10% off books. While you don't get points or clear book covers, it's great to be able to buy things cheaply.

Also, even though it's summer, there are a ton of fruit flies all over campus.

Summary
If you share the same way of thinking as those around you, you'll make friends, there's no fighting, and no girls, making this a very comfortable university. Even if you're a bit of a nerd, you'll likely find it easier to get by with kind-hearted classmates.
I'll end this review by recommending that you spend your first year working part-time and making friends, your second year studying, your third year hellish student experiments, and your fourth year luckily finding a girlfriend. I wish you all the best in your student life.", 'This is a school full of memories. I studied there for four years. Looking at it again today brought back memories of those days. It brings back so many nostalgic memories!']

name: Aichi Kiwami College of Nursing
reviews:
['They have the best service and the Doctor’s there is really helpful and professional i would always recommend anybody to go to this hospital', 'Are there any professors or doctors at Aichi Medical University who have appeared on television?
Aichi Medical University has professors and doctors who have appeared on television programs. The following doctors have previously appeared on television programs:
⦿ Lecturer Yoko Inukai (Physiology Lecturer): Appeared on Nippon Television's "The World's Most Wanted Lesson" (July 2023)
⦿ Dr. Mamiko Mano (Sleep Medicine): Appeared on Nippon Television's "Learning with Kazulaser" (July 2023)
⦿ Professor Eizo Watanabe (Critical Care Medicine): Appeared on NHK's "Today's Health"
⦿ Assistant Professor Ryosuke Miyamoto (Pediatrics): Appeared on Fuji Television's "Mezamashi 8" on August 17, 2023.', 'I would like to thank my doctor for his excellent guidance. The male nurse was also very kind and I am very happy.', "In fact, the food at this hospital's cafeteria is better than that of Tokyo Medical University Hospital, Kyorin University Hospital, and Nara Medical University Hospital. Toshiba Hospital doesn't even have a cafeteria, so that's out of the question. I think the fact that it was rebuilt in 2019 is a big factor. With Dr. Heli, this is probably the general hospital approved by the Ministry of Health, Labor and Welfare with the most specialized facilities in the Tokai region.

For example, there are chairs spaced 5m apart in the hallways. No other hospital has gone to such lengths to provide a waiting area for when your feet get tired or something happens. As a result, I think the clinical, pathology, and technician staff all have high aspirations.", "You can get a Starbucks there! Plus, there's a dedicated parking lot! ☝️ Apparently, it has the best design of any Starbucks in the country! 🙌🙌

The design makes it perfect for capturing the afternoon sun, so it'd be great for a café on the terrace in the cooler months. 😉🍂

You can even see the doctor helicopter hangar from there. 🚁 Apparently, it's only 25 minutes away from anywhere in Aichi Prefecture. 😳"]

name: Aichi Konan College
reviews: ["It may not be flashy, but it collaborates with the local community, has opened a childcare support center on campus, and opens the student cafeteria to users of the childcare support center and open college programs. With a surprisingly spacious campus and a great student atmosphere, I think it's a really good school."]

name: Aichi Medical College for Physical and Occupational Therapy
reviews:
['Small private medical college. I attended a symposium here in 2024.', "I was a student here and it was the worst.
I'll tell you the secret behind their 100% national exam pass rate.
They screen out and eliminate students who are bad at remembering things without providing any support, leaving only the relatively talented students.
That's why it's easy to get in even if your score is low, but it's harder to graduate.", 'The students here have the worst manners. They run around on the train platform, cut in line, and make a lot of noise. 
Are they not taught common sense?', 'This is a junior college in Kiyosu City that also works hard to contribute to the local community.', "They have no intention of letting you graduate. If you join, you'll regret it."]

name: Aichi Prefectural University
reviews:
['You will get a high degree of education and experience', 'My child and I participated in the 88th Kids Park, which was organized in cooperation with the Faculty of Education and Welfare.

I am extremely grateful to the students who prepared and ran the event.

They prepared a haunted house for us on a hot day.

Two older women accompanied us, one in front and one behind, to make it comfortable for children to participate alone.

They encouraged and guided our child, who had become quiet from fear.

Even I, an adult, was a little surprised when the woman playing Sadako appeared at the end, but it was so cute to see her quickly disappear without our child noticing. I wanted to say, "Thank you, older women."

For my child, it was a valuable event as he doesn't often have the opportunity to talk to university students.

Thank you so much!✨', 'The buildings on campus, surrounded by nature, are beautiful and impressive.

However, access is inconvenient.

It's about a 10-minute walk from the Linimo's "Expo 2008 Memorial Park" station.

There are no convenience stores or anything along the way.

If that aspect were improved, I think it would be a much better environment for students...', "It's a nice, calm university, and the evening view is amazing.", 'When the underpass, which serves as an access route from the station, was under construction, many students ignored the traffic signals at pedestrian crossings.

Also, there were many people who appeared to be school staff smoking on the sidewalk outside the school gate, which was extremely annoying.']

name: Aichi Prefectural University of Fine Arts & Music
reviews:
['I stayed here for two moths as a Hungarian artist and I loved it so much. Professors & students are really talented, curious and hard-working. The staff are really helpful. Nice place to have a walk and watch an exhibition or a performance. Thank you all!', 'I like this university so much.', 'good', 'Visited during the school festival.
I was impressed by the various unique works of the students.

It seems like a good environment, nestled in the mountains.', "I visited for the first time the other day for the school festival.
The campus was spacious and had a relaxed atmosphere which was interesting.
At a university where music and art are both taught, I had a different type of student and vibe than at a purely arts school, which was a lot of fun.

I wish they would do something about the poor organization of the food stalls. It felt like you had to wait for minutes (or hours in some stores) to buy food, but I wonder if this relaxed atmosphere fosters free thinking.

The buildings are old, but I'd like to see them retain the modern feel of the time. I'd like to see something as wonderful as the Sogakudo Concert Hall, which is quite old but is well-used."]

name: Aichi Sangyo University
reviews:
['N', 'We used it as a venue for the Eiken exam. The school building is on top of a mountain, so we parked in a free parking lot halfway up the hill and took the exam in the "Classroom and Research Building," about a five-minute walk away. Parents waited outside. It was sunny, but since it was February, there was a bit of wind and it was cold.', "I'm a student in the Department of Architecture. I'm posting on a secondary account because I don't want to be found out.
To cut to the chase, there are two good points:
1) They're very caring towards motivated students and fully support the pursuits they want to pursue.
2) If you're particularly interested in architecture, you can easily contact architecture professors at any time, which can be useful for job hunting.
I think these are the two main points. The staff, including the Student Affairs Office, are also very helpful.
One drawback is that I often come to campus, but there are no daytime buses, so I have to hike, which I find very inconvenient. It's fine if you're just trying to strengthen your legs...
Also, regarding classes, they're basically university classes, so the difficulty varies depending on the subject. I recommend that new students and those planning to enroll at university visit an open campus and ask current students questions.
Finally, I personally think enrolling in the Department of Architecture was the right choice, so if you're having trouble choosing a major at Aichi Sangyo University, I think choosing Architecture will broaden your options. I sincerely hope that those taking the exam will pass.", "You'd be better off not enrolling.
There are too many assignments.
The cafeteria food is awful.
The classes are boring.
The professor just talks for 90 minutes, I want my tuition back.
The PC starts up too slowly.
There are a lot of presentations in the design department.
Don't enroll under any circumstances.
You'll regret it if you do.", "I am a new student who attended the entrance ceremony today. The signal is so poor that I can't use my smartphone at all."]

name: Aichi Shukutoku University
reviews:
['I visited the school festival because it was open to the public. There were stalls and stages set up by clubs and circles. There were also displays showing the progress of graduation projects by seminar students, which was interesting to see what they were studying at university. I also envied the student life on such a large campus.', "Professor Ishida, former Vice President of the Faculty of Cultural Studies, appears to be very active in political and social activities outside of the university. While academic freedom should be respected, the university's deep ties with specific organizations, carried out under the guise of the university, raise questions about its governance and risk management.

For students who wish to study in a purely academic environment and parents seeking a neutral educational environment, this background may seem a bit noisy and unsettling. We hope the university will provide a transparent explanation to ensure the quality and neutrality of its education.", 'It was delicious. I got a lot of free green onions and ate until I was full.', "1. Poor location
There's no train station nearby, so I have to take the bus.
2. The school bus is inconvenient.
There are too few buses. The only buses arrive at the beginning and end of classes, so if the train is delayed, I miss class. Even if class ends early, I have to wait for several minutes.
In the summer, the air conditioning isn't turned on until the bus is full, so it's very hot.
I have to walk several minutes from Akaike Station. Because of this, I often miss the train just in time. Rainy days are a real pain.", "It's a private university in the suburbs of Nagoya. It's spacious."]

name: Aichi University
reviews:
['Really...I want go there', 'Easy to locate', "It's a former army facility, and there are still buildings that retain its appearance in places. It was once home to a division, so it's spacious enough that you can enjoy cherry blossoms in early April. It's also close to the station, making it convenient for commuting. While universities are becoming more and more built up in urban areas, this is a truly university-like campus. It may be an old-fashioned way of thinking, but I like the campus, with buildings scattered across a large campus and plenty of greenery to enjoy.", 'This is the 49th university in Japan to be founded under the old system.
While the university's name is derived from the prefecture, the name isn't simply inspired by it; it's said to be derived from the word "Aichi" (wisdom), another name for "philosophy."
My first impression upon visiting the Toyohashi campus was that it was using its tradition and history as a cover to neglect investment. It's a shame. It seems like it's not much different from nearby high schools.
As a university with such a rich history and tradition, it should spend money to improve its status. It should follow the example of Chukyo and Meijo in terms of public relations activities and investment.

"Imparting Wisdom for a Globalized World."

Also, personally, I believe that in order to truly become a comprehensive university, it needs to establish a faculty of natural sciences. Because a university that prides itself on contributing to the community, I believe this is an essential subject for a manufacturing region like Aichi.', 'Those going to the Aichi University Toa Dobun Shoin University Memorial Center cannot enter from this entrance. 
The barrier at the parking lot entrance cannot be raised, so you will have to back out onto a busy road.']

name: Aichi University of Education
reviews: ['Studying at Aichi University of Education for half a year was an amazing experience, both academically and culturally. The university provided a supportive learning environment with knowledgeable professors and modern facilities, making it easy to engage in meaningful discussions and activities. The international support team was incredibly helpful, ensuring a smooth transition for exchange students. I also had the chance to connect with local and international peers, participate in cultural events, and explore the beautiful Aichi region. Overall, my time there was enriching and unforgettable, and I highly recommend it to anyone considering an exchange in Japan.', 'This university is a really nice university. The staff here are some of the best and brightest in their fields. In addition to that, the staff here are some of the kindest and warmest people you will ever meet that try to help you when you have a question. They have partnerships with universities across the globe and are constantly trying to do the best they can with what is given to them. It is a really nice university.', 'Quiet campus. Conducive for learning. There are several spaces for sports like swimming, athletics, baseball/softball, soccer, rugby, and tennis. There are also gyms for traditional martial arts. There is an arts center and a space for outdoor activities. The university also offers student housing. Library is being renovated. With two cafeterias and a cooperative. Several parking spaces. Accessible via Meitetsu bus from Chiryuu station, Kariya station, Nisshin station, and Aeon iMall Miyoshi.', 'Better education systems', "This is a National Category University that means it's a top level university."]

name: AIIMS Rishikesh
reviews:
['I am a visitor, attendant at AIIMS, Rishikesh. I wish to bring to your kind attention the extremely unhygienic and poorly maintained condition of the public toilets inside the campus. Despite being a premier government healthcare institute, the cleaning and maintenance standards expected from ground-level sanitation workers are not being met.

The toilets are suffering from:

Lack of regular cleaning by the assigned workers

Bad odor and unremoved waste

Blocked drainage and dirty floors

Absence of proper hygiene materials and supervision

This issue reflects a misuse and neglect of public infrastructure, which ultimately affects patients’ dignity, safety, and health. I believe the government has provided world-class facilities and infrastructure, but due to weak implementation and lack of accountability at the worker/supervision level, the benefits are not reaching the public in the intended form.

I would like to clarify that my complaint is not against the government or the institution, but against the failure in maintenance execution by responsible personnel, which is damaging public trust in government facilities and leading to wastage of taxpayer-funded resources.

I respectfully request that:

1. A strict monitoring and feedback system for sanitation workers be introduced

2. Regular inspections by supervisory staff be ensured

3. Immediate cleaning and long-term hygiene maintenance protocols be reinforced

4. Accountability measures be applied where negligence is found

This institution is a matter of national pride, and public infrastructure deserves respect and responsible upkeep from all associated staff members.

I hope my concern will be addressed at the earliest for the safety and dignity of patients and the general public.

Thanking you for your attention.', "Visited the hospital during an emergency for the toilet. I am from Delhi and was coming back from Rishikesh to Delhi when my father-in-law had to use the washroom. We were happy that we have gotten a hospital for that but when we saw the condition of the toilets and the property we were immediately regretting. There were no western commodes in the Men's toilet, not were there cleanliness. The toilet was stinking and was in bad condition.

When I saw the females toilet it was also a mess. Zero cleanliness zero basic things. The sinks were extremely dirty and the taps were running water. There were no pipes on the basins so when you wash hands your feet get soaked in water. The floor was like a pool as the water was everywhere.

Really poor and bad experience.", 'I had a very disappointing experience at AIIMS, especially in the emergency department. If a patient has been treated at another hospital, they hardly get the care they need here. The cardiology department, in particular, seems poorly managed. Doctors appear indifferent, and instead of offering proper treatment, they simply suggest oxygen support and send patients home directly from the emergency. This approach lacks empathy and professionalism, especially for critical cases. AIIMS needs to seriously improve its patient care, especially in emergencies.

AIIMS is supposed to be a lifeline for patients, especially those traveling from remote areas like the hills (Pahad), but the experience is far from acceptable. Patients rarely get the treatment they need unless they have a reference. The cardiology department is especially disappointing—senior doctors hardly ever show up in emergencies, leaving critical cases to junior doctors who seem inexperienced and unsure of what to do. This is unacceptable for such a sensitive department. AIIMS urgently needs to improve its emergency care, especially for those who rely on it as their last hope.', 'The emergency unit has the latest high tech facilities which operate 24 by 7. The emergency unit has designated ares of yellow and red for serious and very serious patients.

Teams of doctors work round the clock to provide relief to ailing patients.

A fleet of Ambulances equipped with life support systems for very serious patients are always ready for any eventuality.

The hospital buzzes with activity from dawn to dusk and when its dark a different set of staff takes charge.

There are some touts who offer to supply medicines that are not available in hospital pharmacy.

The canteen shops outside the emergency unit are a bit slow in responding to customers at night.', 'If you have to go to OPD then enter from Gate Number 3 and OPD is behind Trauma Centre.

Clean and maintained hospital 4⭐

Free Parking']

name: Aiken Technical College
reviews:
['Aiken Technical College is a place where education feels practical, supportive, and genuinely focused on student success. From my experience, this college is not just about completing courses or earning credits. It is about learning skills that actually matter and preparing students for real careers.
One of the strongest aspects of Aiken Technical College is the instructors. They are knowledgeable, approachable, and truly invested in their students. Classes are well-structured, and instructors make sure concepts are clear before moving forward. If a student needs extra help, they are always willing to explain again or guide you in the right direction. This personal attention makes a big difference, especially for students who are returning to education after a gap or adjusting to a new academic system.
The campus environment is calm, organized, and student-friendly. Administrative offices, academic advising, and student services are efficient and respectful of students’ time. Processes such as registration, advising appointments, and academic support feel straightforward rather than stressful. The college creates an atmosphere where students feel comfortable asking questions and seeking help when needed.
Another major strength of Aiken Technical College is its focus on affordability and accessibility. Tuition is reasonable, and the college offers scholarships, financial aid options, and flexible schedules that make education possible for working students and families. The programs are career-oriented, which means what you learn in class connects directly to the workplace.
Overall, Aiken Technical College provides a balanced educational experience that combines quality teaching, strong student support, and practical learning. It is an excellent choice for students who want a focused, affordable, and supportive path toward their academic and career goals.', 'I would not recommend Professor Colley’s class if you expect clear guidance or helpful feedback. Lectures are often confusing, quizzes are strict with spelling and full sentences required, and PowerPoints are not provided. Study advice is limited to notes, repeated questions get little help, and feedback is minimal. Overall, this class is stressful, frustrating, and provides almost no support.
Throughout the semester, I tried multiple strategies to improve after low quiz grades: rewatching lecture videos, reviewing notes thoroughly, using outside resources like YouTube for extra explanations, and reaching out to classmates on discussion boards. Despite these efforts, I continued to struggle to understand the material.
Quizzes are not thoroughly explained, making it hard to know what is expected. Requests for lecture PowerPoints were denied because they are “only outlines” and could be misleading without in-class explanation. Instead, students are directed to FAQs, discussion boards, or the Student Success Center. Feedback on assignments is limited, and concerns often feel dismissed, even with repeated attempts to ask for help.
Between confusing lectures, strict and poorly explained quizzes, denied materials, and minimal guidance, this class was far more stressful than it needed to be. If you value clear explanations, helpful feedback, and support that actually helps students succeed, this is probably not the class for you. Future students should carefully consider whether this teaching style fits their learning needs before enrolling.', "Aiken Tech is a wonderful place full of fascinating people from a variety of backgrounds and perspectives, and it's such a great atmosphere to be in that. I'm glad that I've had the chance to spend some time there and I hope that you also get a chance to visit Aiken Tech and see what I'm talking about. Take care!", 'Aiken Tech is the best school I have ever went to. Compared to Peru State(NE), & Suffolk Community College(NY).. The professors are great, very helpful. THEY want YOU to learn the material and pass. They are not there to TRY and flunk you.

The president is very friendly and kind as well as the staff members. They really want to help you achieve your goals. The library is great, very nice atmosphere, and very resourceful. The school has an academic success center where they provide FREE tutoring!! How awesome is that!

I also love how new the school looks compared to other schools. Brand new computers in the math lab.
PS. Great Security, and Officers that patrol the school. They are always around. I appreciate their hard work to try and keep the school safe.
-Shirley Schweitzer', "The thing about Aiken is there isn't enough employment for the programs. After graduation it's best to just move out of town or even better out of state. For example, LPNs have a much broader responsibility out west and up east and get paid an RNs salary in the south and north. Look up the he job market to see what's actually in demand and projected to grow then see what state is actually paying your worth. To me, if a job cannot compete with the cost of living they should not be doing any business. To live in most American states you need $40-$50/hr full-time. Depending upon which sector you work this wage works up east and down south and for 3 states out west as I've calculated. The point of working is to provide and sustain not to make rich people richer."]

name: Aims Community College
reviews:
['Up before the sun to catch the hot air balloons 🎈 at Aims Community College this morning! My friend might not be a morning person, but I, the quintessential early bird, took it all in stride. Just means more coffee for me while I waited! ☕ It was totally worth it, though. Seeing all the colorful balloons fill the sky was so cool!

#HotAirBalloon #EarlyBird #BalloonFestival #AimsCommunityCollege #ColoradoLife #ColorfulSkies #MorningAdventures #NotAMorningPerson #WorthTheWait #OptOutside', 'Aims Community College in Greeley, Colorado, is one of the top 100 community colleges in the United States, and for good reason. Its reputation for academic excellence is matched by its uniquely charming campus, featuring an array of small, eclectic buildings that create a welcoming and distinctive learning environment.

The engineering and chemistry departments are standouts, offering top-tier programs that rival those at larger institutions. During my time at Aims, I pursued a degree in pre-engineering and engineering drafting, creating detailed designs for CAD 101/102 and advanced 200-level design classes.

Equally impressive is the chemistry department, where the instructors’ passion for teaching and ability to simplify complex topics set them apart. In my class, which included about 23 pre-nursing students, the energy and dedication of the faculty were unmatched. The pre-nursing program’s seamless transition into the University of Northern Colorado is a testament to the program’s quality and preparation. The chemistry faculty were instrumental in helping students succeed, leaving a lasting impact with their supportive and inspiring teaching.

Aims Community College combines academic rigor, a supportive environment, and a beautiful campus, making it an outstanding choice for students pursuing their goals in engineering, nursing, or any other field. It’s a place where students can truly thrive, thanks to the dedication and expertise of its faculty.', "I was a recipient of their FREE TAX SERVICE'S  this last year....they madec2 paper copies of my financial papers and tax forms...they gave one copy to a STRANGER who also was there...they didn't apologize and left it up to me to Block my taxes and forms for future scams...
I expressed my disappointment,  anger for their lack of concern...it was like  OH WELL...please be very careful if you use their free tax services coming up.....", 'Aims put on such a great graduation yesterday. Great job AIMS! Congratulations to all the graduates 🎓!', 'At first, it can seem like a confusing campus, but the super friendly staff are more than willing to get you pointed in the right direction and to your destination! Combine that with amazing professors who care deeply about the subjects they teach and the success of each of their students and you got yourself an awesome recipe for success!']

name: Aimst University
reviews:
['My alma mater, graduated in 2017 with MBBS. Large compound with much greenery. Many facilities for students and staff.
Pros
- Beautiful landscape
- Many facilities available
- Dental hospital available
- Large cafeteria
- Beautiful sports centre with Olympic size swimming pool
Cons
- Nil
Overall, many memories and exciting times created here. Kipidap 👍', 'Kedah AIMST University is a **well-regarded middle-sized university** that offers a **balanced and student-centric learning environment**.

### **1. Personalized Learning Experience**
As a mid-sized university, AIMST provides a **more intimate academic setting** compared to large institutions. Students benefit from **smaller class sizes**, allowing for better interaction with professors and personalized attention.

### **2. Strong Focus on Health Sciences & Technology**
AIMST is particularly known for its **health sciences programs** (Medicine, Pharmacy, Dentistry) and **STEM fields**. The university has **modern labs, simulation centers, and hospital partnerships**, ensuring hands-on training.

### **3. Affordable Tuition with Quality Education**
Compared to larger private universities, AIMST offers **competitive tuition fees** while maintaining good academic standards, making it an attractive option for local and international students.

### **4. Multicultural & Supportive Environment**
With a mix of **Malaysian and international students**, the campus fosters **cultural diversity**. The university also provides **student support services**, including counseling and career guidance.

### **5. Strategic Location & Campus Facilities**
Located in **Kedah**, the campus offers a **serene yet well-connected environment**. Facilities include **libraries, sports complexes, and student accommodations**, contributing to a **comfortable student life**.

### **Areas for Improvement:**
- Could expand **research opportunities** and industry collaborations.
- More international exchange programs could enhance global exposure.

### **Final Verdict:**
AIMST University is an **excellent choice for students seeking a focused, supportive, and affordable education**, especially in health sciences and technology. Its **middle-sized nature ensures a close-knit academic community**, making it ideal for those who prefer a more personalized university experience.

Full scholarship available, let me know if u r interested. AIMST University do have student introducing scheme.', 'Update – 10 January 2026:
I am writing this update using another account, as only one review is allowed per account.
In my previous review, I mentioned that I had officially withdrawn from AIMST University, yet money was still deducted from me. Unfortunately, the issue does not end there. As of today, I am still receiving re-registration emails, despite having completed the withdrawal process.
This clearly shows poor internal coordination and a failure to properly update student records. It raises serious concerns about whether the university is even aware of students’ official status, or if the staff responsible simply did not carry out their duties properly.
Students should not be repeatedly disturbed after completing formal withdrawal procedures. This experience reflects inefficient administration, poor communication, and unprofessional handling of student records.
AIMST seriously needs to improve its internal processes, accountability, and administrative competence.', "AIMST University truly stands out as a top-notch institution. The vast and beautifully landscaped campus provides an ideal setting for learning. The hostels offer comfortable living spaces with essential amenities, creating a home away from home. The highly efficient and friendly faculty, coupled with state-of-the-art facilities, contributes to a positive learning environment. AIMST's commitment to a comprehensive education system, diverse academic programs, and research opportunities makes it one of the best universities I've ever visited. Additionally, a heartfelt thank you for providing the training that has helped us gain a wealth of knowledge. Highly recommended for those seeking a transformative educational experience.", 'Well maintained and well planned university buildings designed in very unique design, However after 20 overs we can see signs of wear and tear. After some maintenance it will be as good as new.
Very good environment for students to focus on their studies without any distractions.']

name: Air Force Academy
reviews: ["This is a very historic place. There are plenty of sites to see for a military retiree like myself. They have a full commissary and base exchange with many other shops and services. In passing some of the military members and cadets, they were all very, very polite. This is a very land expansive place as it's located in the prairies of Colorado, which makes it very beautiful.", 'When people think of the United States Air Force (USAF), they often picture fighter jets, huge cargo planes, and long runways stretching through the desert. But the Air Force also runs some massive bases outside the mainland United States.\n\nThese sites play a major role in America’s global military presence. Some are in Europe, some in Asia, and one is even near the Arctic Circle.', "Visited the United States Air Force Academy in early August and was simply awestruck by the history, mission, character and quality of people who first organized the institution, all the graduates over time, and the proud character of the individuals attending the academy. Beautiful campus nestled in the north part of Colorado Springs, with plenty of space and neat, air-themed buildings. The chapel was under renovation/construction when we visited so we could not view it, but there are plenty of images and models around to get an idea of how beautiful and majestic the building is. We don't have any family members in the military, but we greatly appreciated the valuable opportunity to learn about this wonderful institution and the amazing work of the people associated with it. Highly recommended if you're in the area.", 'Visiting the United States Air Force Academy (USAFA)is a unique experience that allows you to observe the training of future U.S. Air Force officers and explore its iconic architecture and aviation facilities.', "What an impressive place!  Not just the beauty and the organization of it, but the respect for the academy and the respect for the service is present.  The size of the grounds is immense, and we only stayed on the roads, not having time to go on any of the walking paths.  Even though the chapel is currently closed, we still learned a lot through the visitor's center, and the video they showed was informative as well.  Would have loved to have had more time here."]

name: Airlangga University
reviews:
['Nice education campus, clean and good arrangement. Easy to find park. The challenge is we had to pay parking fee when entering the campus', 'One of 4 best universities in Indonesia according to QS World University Rankings, Times Higher Education (THE), dan Webometrics.', 'Universitas Airlangga (UNAIR), a leading Indonesian university, known for academic excellence, strong research culture, global partnerships, and commitment to innovation, ethics, and community impact.', "I am writing a completely brief and true review to express my profound disappointment with the overall experience I have had as an international student at your institution.

First and foremost, the language barrier has been a significant obstacle for international students like myself. While I understand the importance of embracing a new culture, the insistence on forcing international students to study Bahasa Indonesia, even in fields such as veterinary studies that primarily use English, is perplexing and counterproductive. This hinders our ability to fully engage with the coursework and impacts the overall quality of our education.

Furthermore, the lack of support for international students is disheartening. The absence of specialized programs for students from other countries forces us to integrate into local programs conducted entirely in Indonesian, making it challenging to comprehend and complete assignments. A more international student-friendly approach would greatly enhance the educational experience for those coming from different cultural backgrounds.

Moreover, the university's emphasis on engaging students in seemingly meaningless and irrelevant activities for the sake of promoting the institution is concerning. As international students, we come to Airlangga University primarily to study and acquire knowledge in our respective fields. The insistence on diverting our time and resources to activities unrelated to our academic pursuits is both illogical and a waste of valuable educational resources.

I must also bring to your attention instances of unprofessional behavior by some tutors, lecturers, or senior staff members. For instance, interrupting a class to attend to personal matters, such as dropping off a child at school, is both unprofessional and disruptive to the learning environment. This incident, among others, raises questions about the commitment and professionalism of the academic staff.

In conclusion, based on my overall experience, I find it difficult to recommend Airlangga University as a suitable institution for pursuing higher education. The combination of language barriers, lack of support for international students, and questionable professional conduct by some staff members has led to a significant decline in the quality of education and overall\xa0satisfaction.", 'Bad weather because polutions and deficit clean air but this campus strong of journal publish.
Their impact is still not significant,
Their openness is not significant, faculties is not unite, and spread out like a island']

name: Air University
reviews:
['Visited Air University twice for my entry test. The first time was a quick in-and-out, but the second time we explored a bit. The library staff was kind enough to let us in, really sweet! The library had multiple floors and a great variety of books. We took some photos and enjoyed the atmosphere. We arrived early, so we walked around NDU, the greenery and calm, professional vibe was amazing. According to the looks and environment, it’s very impressive. Experience wise, I don’t know yet, but the first impression was really good.', 'An excellent ranking HEC recognized university. It offer admission in many education combination. It has a great faculty member who are ranked in top 2% of the world scientist. It is also offering a vastvrange of computing education. A high standard university in the area producing a quality graduats. Atmosphere is so good a very higenic environment for students. It also have a hostel for female students only.', 'Excellent educational institutions. We, Habib Construction, are proud builders of the underpass/intersection in front of the university.', 'Air University Islamabad is a premier public research university in Pakistan, established in 2002 under the Pakistan Air Force. It offers a diverse range of undergraduate, graduate, and doctoral programs in engineering, IT, business, social sciences, and more. Known for its high academic standards and modern campus, Air University promotes innovation, research, and leadership in various fields. The institution is recognized for its contributions to science and technology, preparing students to meet national and global challenges. With a focus on academic excellence, it fosters a learning environment that encourages critical thinking and practical problem-solving skills.', "Located in the heart of Islamabad, Air University is a premier institution that embodies academic excellence, innovative research, and exceptional student experiences. With a strong focus on STEM fields, Air University has established itself as a leading university in Pakistan.

Air University offers a wide range of undergraduate, graduate, and doctoral programs in disciplines such as aerospace engineering, electrical engineering, computer science, and business administration. The university's curriculum is designed to provide students with a comprehensive education that prepares them for successful careers.

Air University is committed to research and innovation, with a strong focus on solving real-world problems. The university's research centers and labs provide students with opportunities to engage in hands-on research and develop practical skills. From drone technology to cybersecurity, Air University's research initiatives are shaping the future of various industries.

Air University's faculty comprises renowned experts in their fields, providing students with high-quality instruction and mentorship. The university's campus is equipped with state-of-the-art facilities, including modern classrooms, labs, and sports facilities.

Air University has strong industry connections, providing students with valuable opportunities for internships, job placements, and career advancement. The university's alumni network is also a testament to its success, with graduates going on to work in top organizations globally.

In conclusion, Air University Islamabad is an exceptional institution that offers students a world-class education, innovative research opportunities, and exceptional student experiences. Whether you're a prospective student, researcher, or industry partner, Air University is definitely worth considering."]

name: AISECT University
reviews:
['*AISECT University*, Hazaribagh, established in 2016 in Jharkhand, is a private, UGC-recognized institution focusing on skill-based higher education. It offers various undergraduate, postgraduate, and diploma courses in fields like IT, Agriculture, Commerce, and Arts. The university emphasizes practical, industry-oriented learning, supported by modern infrastructure, to produce job-ready graduates.
Key Details About AISECT University, Hazaribagh:
✍️Establishment & Recognition: Founded on May 16, 2016, under the AISECT University Act, 2016 by the Government of Jharkhand. It is approved by UGC, AICTE, and BCI.
✍️Location: Located in the Korrah-Meru area of Hazaribagh, providing a 40-acre green campus with modern, well-designed, and secured infrastructure.
✍️Academic Focus: Known as a skill-based university, it integrates industry-specific skills into the curriculum, allowing students to choose two skill-based courses each semester.
✍️Academic Offerings: Offers diverse programs across faculties of Agriculture, Commerce, Management, Computer Science, Journalism, Arts, and Sciences.
✍️Placements & Facilities: The campus includes a digital library, labs, and a student-run community radio station. Reported placements include companies like TCS, with packages reaching up to 6 LPA.
✍️Vision: Aims to bridge the gap between skills and industry demand for rural and semi-urban youth, with a strong focus on research and employability.
✍️Popular Departments and Courses:
📌Agriculture: B.Sc. in Agriculture with a focus on farming, food processing, and horticulture.
📌Computer Science & IT: Courses on Information Technology and Computer Applications.
📌Management & Commerce: Programs in Commerce and Management.
📌Fine Arts & Performing Arts: Degrees in BFA, MFA, BPA, and MPA.
The university is part of the larger AISECT Group of Universities and aims to provide high-quality education to the region.', 'Worst management system. Never picks call and none of the team members are enough trained to answer any of query that we ask to the respected department. I would never recommend to take admission in such a college that ruins students carrer. Especially the examination department consits the worst staff members. An amazing and inclusive environment with diversified student crowd forms the core of this campus. Hostel, sports and canteen facilities are very good and make it one of the top private colleges in Jharkhand.']

name: Ajman University
reviews:
['As a current Psychology student at Ajman University, my experience has been genuinely rewarding. The campus environment is well-organized, student-centered, and designed to support both academic growth and personal development. The faculty members are knowledgeable, approachable, and truly invested in student success.

Administrative services have become more efficient over time, and the university continues to upgrade its facilities and digital services to enhance the overall student experience. The psychology program, in particular, offers strong academic grounding with practical exposure that adds real market value.

Overall, AU delivers a solid, future-focused educational experience. I’m proud to be part of a university that consistently aims higher and invests in its students.', "I recently took the IELTS exam at Ajman University, and it was an unexpectedly beautiful experience. From the moment I arrived on campus, I was struck by the serene and welcoming atmosphere. The campus itself was breathtaking—lush green spaces, modern architecture, and calm walkways that instantly put me at ease before the test.

The exam center was impeccably organized, with clear signage and friendly staff ready to assist at every step. The registration process was smooth, and the exam rooms were spacious, well-lit, and designed to minimize distractions. I appreciated the quiet environment and the comfortable seating, which allowed me to focus entirely on the task at hand.

What truly made the experience memorable was the genuine care shown by the exam administrators. They were professional yet warm, ensuring that every candidate felt supported and informed. Their kindness helped reduce the pre-exam jitters and created a positive, stress-free setting. Between the breaks, I even had the chance to relax in a pleasant lounge area, which was a thoughtful touch that made a significant difference.

Overall, my IELTS exam at Ajman University was more than just a test—it was a beautifully orchestrated experience that reflected the institution's commitment to excellence and care. I left feeling not only confident in my performance but also deeply grateful for the compassionate and efficient service I received. I would highly recommend Ajman University for anyone looking to take their IELTS exam in an environment that is as supportive as it is inspiring.", 'Ajman University has a modern and clean campus with a diverse student community. The learning environment feels welcoming, and students from different backgrounds can easily connect. Teaching is good overall, and most professors are supportive and explain topics in a clear, easy way, which makes learning less stressful.

The university uses digital platforms like Moodle, which is helpful for assignments, materials, and updates—something students really appreciate. Facilities like libraries and study spaces are well maintained. While some administrative processes can feel slow at times, the overall experience is positive.

Ajman University is a good choice for students looking for quality education, teaching, and a balanced student life in the UAE.', "Ajman University is a well-disciplined institution featuring state-of-the-art facilities. It's an excellent choice for your son or daughter to pursue their education and build a strong future. You can trust that they will receive top-quality education and support, ensuring their academic and personal growth.", 'I really enjoyed my visit to Ajman University! The campus was so clean and modern, and everyone was welcoming and friendly. I loved exploring the buildings and seeing how students learn and have fun. Even though it was just a short visit, it left a big impression on me—I would definitely love to go back again!
And carine was the best receptionist I have ever met']

name: Ajou University
reviews: ['I did my exchange semester there and I loved it! I would absolutely recommend it. The staff is willing to help you in any situation. The new international dormitory is also very modern, comfortable and clean. I also liked that the university is in a good location with a very big variety of options for food, cafés and shopping.', "Such a beautiful place. It is one of the most important universities in Suwon and one with most international students in South Korea. There are many exchange students each semester from all countries (like me Ecuador 😊) and international students for Master's degree. Everyone there is very kind and always help international students.", "If you're coming from Seoul, you can get here with one simple, short bus ride. Everything here costs cheaper than Seoul and thus it's a very nice place for students to live in. Such a great university with a convenient location, great nightlife, and friendly people.", 'Lots of Exchange program and Dual-degree program to USA, Great reputation of Engineering, Medical related and Law school in addition to high level students of other Majors, Convenient location near Seoul and wide campus area with beautiful flowers and trees, Central Library open for all year for studying, University leading Asia as its name. Anything is possible with the name of Ajou in Korea and the world.', "It's a big campus with ever busy people"]

name: Akademia Podlaska
reviews: ["I highly recommend it. However, I think there's a big difference in the study format. I'm in my fourth year of full-time studies and I really recommend most of the lecturers, who are easy to talk to and treat you equally, not as a student-worm-ass-lecturer situation. In my experience, the first two years are as if they didn't exist; a lot starts happening in the third year. A big plus is that the university emphasizes internships rather than regular classes. But you also know that studying is primarily about self-education, where not everything is handed to you on a silver platter.", "From a general perspective, the studies themselves weren't bad. But unfortunately, there are many older lecturers who discriminate, even though they are PSYCHOLOGISTS. For example, a lecturer in the psychology of marriage who claimed that a childless marriage isn't a family, essentially based her entire class on hate speech against those childless by choice and LGBT+ people, and on the test, she asked questions related to the Catholic religion. Another example is another lecturer who gave students a transgender book to read under the patronage of the Ordo Iuris sect. And at the end of the fifth year of psychology studies, a student is forced (because without it, the course fails) to discuss their private life with other students, after which their classmates are allowed to express their opinions about their problems and lives. Lecturers who conduct this also judge others' lives and difficulties in a harmful way. Such things are a topic for potential psychotherapy with a professional psychotherapist, and it's appalling that people who have been psychologists and therapists (I'm talking about lecturers) for years don't understand this. Furthermore, the studies are part-time, and in their fifth year, students are required to take a whole week off to attend classes every day. And no one, I emphasize—part-time—informed them about this when signing the study contract.", "I recommend it! If someone wants to learn, they will. There are many interesting subjects, and the professors are friendly and knowledgeable, and they demonstrate it in a fun way. The university is growing, and that's the most important thing.", "The standard of study is appallingly low. Online sessions, lectures are nonexistent, and there are tutorials, but they involve group presentations while the lecturer talks on the phone. Generally, the lecturers are rude, insulting students in front of the entire group (the great psychologist), and they're all master's students. Avoid this terrible tragedy.", "The university's standards are very low. The university charges high fees for students, while its equipment is from the 1990s. There's only one headset with a microphone for 30 students in the computer room. Not to mention the building's condition and the cost savings on heating. The schedule is constantly changing. Some classes are on John Paul II Street, some on Hetmańska Street, and only 10 minutes to transfer. I don't recommend it; it was the worst year and a half of my life."]

name: Akademi Farmasi Yarsis Pekanbaru
reviews:
["The service at the afternoon shift counter was around 12:00. The woman was overweight and wore a headscarf. She had no attitude and belittled BPJS patients. I queued from 6:20 AM until 12:00 PM. I waited and waited, but no one called me. When I asked at the counter, I was told there was no queue and was told to queue tomorrow. My house is far from the monument. Yesterday I went to the hospital and was told the queue was full and told to come tomorrow. Now I'm back, waiting in line for a long time, but no one takes care of me. I'm even told to come again tomorrow.", "One of Pontianak's proud nursing and pharmacy colleges, founded by the Islamic Hospital Foundation in Pontianak City.
The campus is quite large and spacious, and it has its own hospital located not far from the main campus building.
From the outside, it looks clean and tidy.
May it continue to grow.", "A clean, safe, comfortable, well-equipped, and Islamic campus. It's extraordinary.", "It's very enjoyable because I'm a student at Akfar Yarsi", 'Very good. Thank you.']

name: AKAD Hochschulen für Berufstätige, Fachhochschule Leipzig
reviews: ["Avoid this university at all costs. The academic advising service is very resistant to advice and doesn't respond to emails or calls.\n\nEspecially avoid the Faculty of Digital Transformation, as you sometimes have to wait years (!) for exam results because lecturers suddenly disappear for several months a year. Reaching them by email or phone is extremely difficult here, too.", "My girlfriend will be studying there soon and she's really looking forward to it. We'll see how it goes, but it looks pretty good so far. 10/10 👍", 'The Freshmen Program is truly the best. You can tell that the HTWK is making an effort to welcome freshmen in the best possible way. The Freshmen Coaches, in particular, have had a positive impact on my life and made it easier for me to start my studies.', "A very good university. There are many degree programs. The food in the cafeteria is affordable and delicious. There's a wide selection of university sports every semester. The library is also good. Very modern and has a large selection of books. The university is easily accessible by tram and not far from the city center.", "Unfortunately, zero stars isn't an option; complete disappointment."]

name: Akaki Tsereteli State University
reviews:
['There is a lot to write about. First of all, the university buildings and campuses are very old and all the classroom materials require to be changed and freshened. Also, having a church in front of the state university is a horrible fact of breaking a law about indoctrination.  I was a student of what they call "the best faculty in university" (English Language and Literature) which I didn't like at all. Teachers are old, rude and past oriented which doesn't make a learning a nice and joyful process. University has few exchange programs which often aren't fully granted. Another issue was that classrooms were never cleaned which explains why many people was sick all the time. I remember that the books were written by our lecturers and they were not better than other books out there, I am not saying anything about that I had to wait to get them for weeks and their material quality was just horrible. Unfortunately, if you want to study in this university and if you are let's say a "different" person, you will have to face very horrible homophobia, discrimination and stereotypical comments from lecturers and deans directly or indirectly towards you. I spent 4 years in ATSU and it was the worst experience I ever had.

In short, I would never, ever recommend this university to anyone. They may say that if you are eager to study, you can do that anywhere, but learning environment does matter and ATSU will provide you nothing but stressful, overly strict and old fashioned lecturers and environment!

Sincerely... :)', 'Beautiful old buildings, Many students are on the way. Need to map on information board. I like to see the library... but cant find.', "Not good quality of study. Education system sucks there... students study from Soviet Union books and they think it's an education. Lecturers are on the same level for many years and they don't think about progress.", 'A very nice place with big, calm and "green" courtyard, a 150 years old biulding. You can really feel yourself at the academia.', 'one of the best university i have ever seen. i was graduated last year. those five yearsi have spent there were great.. just go for it without second thoughts. 🤙🤙🤙🤙💦💦💦']

reviews:
['Akanu ibiam federal polytechnic is the best polytechnic in Nigeria as far as Nigeria is consen', 'This is an institution located in a remote community (Unwana) in Afikpo North of Ebonyi state.
It is a wonderful place for your educational career, it will open your mind and show you the real world of stressful educational background.
I did my Pre-ND, ND and HND all in this school. I love the experience through out my studies here. It was very stressful, fun and well enjoyed.
Poly Unwana, Aluta Continua

Ejesi m Poly!!! Jokwa plenty', 'Akanu Ibiam Federal Polytechnic Unwana is a better training ground for young people who wants to be educationally and morally balance. At Akanu Ibiam Federal Polytechnic Unwana, not only are qualified graduate produced but a well informed set of young people are made. Try Akanu Ibiam Federal Polytechnic Unwana for your first degree program and thank me later', "There are so many opportunities in this school, that you can honestly do anything you want to pursue your passion. The lecturers are always there for you, and are there to help you improve all the time. The academics are strong, and it's honestly so manageable.
Unwana Polytechnic truly have a competitive environment. Because the school is so small, you get to know everyone, and you realize the amount of competition that exists within every grade. Sometimes, you really just feel stupid in a class (I know I have). But I love this school so so much, and I have honestly met some of the best people in the whole world. Though it is competitive, everyone is welcoming, and once you feel a part of the Polytechnic Community, you feel like you can conquer the world. I am so happy I chose this school and I can't regret my decision. Long live Poly Unwana Community.", "I thank God for a successful defense in School of Business AIFPU in the profession of office Technology and Management 🧑‍💼

AIFPU is a tertiary institute that gives students the necessary requirement and experience for Future goals and achievements
