Both campus and city amenities are excellent!', 'The campus is beautiful and green, resembling a small city. I thoroughly enjoyed my stay!']

name: Akeno Flying School
reviews:
['Awesome', 'November 22, 2025

The Akeno Rainbow Team, a popular helicopter acrobatic flight team composed of instructors from the Akeno Air School at the Japan Ground Self-Defense Force's Camp Akeno, captivates audiences with their dynamic formation flight displays. They primarily use helicopters such as the TH-480, UH-2, AH-64D, and CH-47, and their spectacular performances have earned them the nickname "Japan's No. 1 Helicopter Flight Demonstration Team."
*The model names may be incorrect.

We were blessed with good weather, and many people attended.

There is no parking nearby, so you have to walk or bike from a parking lot a little further away.

There was a bicycle parking lot.', 'We participated in the annual Akeno Garrison Air Festival. The commemorative ceremony featured a vibrant musical performance by the band, and the flight review featured a dynamic formation flight, bringing the entire venue together in excitement. This year, a special wooden cart from the Omata Hohikidan was also on display, providing a rare opportunity to experience the fusion of traditional culture and a Self-Defense Force event. Many local residents and families attended, making this an event that can be enjoyed by people of all ages. This is a highly recommended festival where you can experience the power of aircraft and the charm of local culture at the same time.', "I went to the Akeno Air Show. It was my first time entering the base, and the Akeno Rainbow's action flight was incredibly impressive, something I thought I'd only be able to see if I came here. I stood the whole time, but it took a long time, including the wait, so I thought it would be more comfortable to bring a mat and sit down. I was lucky to be able to park my car at a friend's house right next to the base, but there is no public parking lot or anywhere nearby, so I had to take the train, which was a pain. There were many people illegally parking on nearby roads, but the narrow roads made it very inconvenient.", '航空祭は最高です!
2023年11月4日に行って来ました。
駐屯地正門向え隣りに臨時駐車場が
あります。
6時30分着いたら駐車場開門待ちの
乗用車が長蛇の列（約1km）でした。
天候もまずまずで久しぶりの制限なし
航空祭に気分も高まりました!
今年は木更津から陸自オスプレイも
来てくれ、盛り上がりました。
明野レインボーはじめ、浜松基地
岐阜基地の飛行展示、救難展示等
内容豊富で楽しませて頂きました。
自衛隊ならびに出店業者の皆様
お疲れ様でした。
ありがとうございました。
帰りに高速SA売店で赤福餅買いました。
今日の伊勢だよりは明野航空祭です。']

name: Akhbar El Yom Academy
reviews: ['Was a great graduation event', 'A distinguished university for study always seeks to develop its methods of education.', "As a educational place , it's good , but the essentials like food or transport are bad", '4 years of my life have been here, with 1 more year to go.', "It's not bad university."]

name: Akikusa Gakuen Junior College
reviews:
["I've been here for proficiency exams, and I have found the place to be very clean, organized, and maintained. Kudos to the maintenance team, crew, staff, and owners of this school. 👏", "The students at this school always walk side by side in groups, blocking the road, which is nothing but a nuisance.
Also, for some reason they shout loudly on their way to and from school, which is a nuisance to the neighbors.
This school should start by teaching its students common sense.
I often see children, who presumably live nearby, getting into trouble because the students have blocked their way.
I can't imagine that people like that will be able to get jobs in early childhood education or similar after graduating.
It's sometimes used as an exam venue, but why do people who come to take exams walk side by side in groups, blocking the road? I wish they would be more considerate of the inconvenience they cause to those around them.", "I visited this place to take the TOEIC exam. You must bring your own indoor shoes. If you don't have them, you can get shoe covers. The exam is held in the auditorium. The seats are fixed, so it's hard to sit for long periods of time, but the acoustics are excellent.", 'I went to the information session alone.
I tried so hard.

The teachers there told me:
"You won't make it," "Please don't come again." I researched you.
I can't read kanji, I can't sit still, I'm depressed, etc.
Please don't ask special needs children to become teachers or nursery teachers. It's disrespectful to children.

I quit the workshop multiple times, and they even said, "How can someone like that become a teacher?"
There's so much prejudice.
Don't be ridiculous.
Do they only accept able-bodied children?
I was hurt.
But my friend still goes there.
It's so painful.', 'This venue is sometimes used as a TOEIC testing center. 
Shoes are not permitted here, so please bring slippers.']

name: Akita International University
reviews:
["Incredible school with incredible opportunities. As an exchange students it's wonderful here and you get lots of chances to go out and explore the local and even further area. Video is an example of classrooms.", 'I would consider AIU a hidden gem. A wonderful small community with dedicated students and staff. Despite its isolated position relative to Goshono, it is able to effectively support the student life on campus.

It has various amenities on campus including the dining hall, a cafe, a school store, and Banafi shop.

There is a bus as well that goes from campus to AEON mall which allows you to transfer to other busses going through Akita city. Though it will be discontinued, there is another bus that goes to Wada Station, which will connect you to Akita station.

The staff speak both English and Japanese so do not entirely worry if your Japanese is not perfect. The professors are the same. Overall instruction is well thought out and cohesive, expect a lot of group work and collaboration in the classroom.

The student life here is better than most. It offers Kyūdō club, Kendo, Student Government, Model UN, Bike-share club, and various other clubs. It also hosts the AIU festival yearly which both foreign students and Japanese students can take part in.

The dorms are well done and I enjoyed Sakura Village during my stay in AIU. Bedsheets and such are provided but you always have the option to bring or buy your own.

AIU offers community outreach activities which differ from semester. There are opportunities to go to local elementary, middle, and high schools alongside universities.

Overall I would recommend to anyone who wants the experience of the countryside and a close-knit community. The only downside is the isolation of the university.', "I have studied here for 4 years. The students are more focused on study than average Japanese college students. Although it's located in the middle of mountains and it snows 2m high, most of the facilities are connected with each other, so you don't really have to go outside. They are clean and new. Local students are friendly to exchange students if you try to join any circles or communities.", "One of the best universities I've studied in. It's not typical Japanese university, it's hosts lots of international students that AIU hosts every semester. Prices of living exactly in this place is cheaper than in bigger cities in Japan. Since there aren't many attractions and transportation system isn't so frequent, you can save lots of money living and studying here. Study programs and schedules are very comfortable to use, all digital. Professors are mostly international, and each of them has his/her own way to teach. Being a small area it also offers lots of events for students and locals.", 'This university is just fascinating. The library is huge and containes a lot of useful books for your study exams. The university has so many cool features. Everything looks very beautiful. Very unique.']

name: Akita Municipal Junior College of Arts and Crafts
reviews: ["I visited for work. It's a new and beautiful building. It somehow reminded me of a European university. The professor who spoke to me said that he wanted to nurture talented people who will carry on Akita's traditional crafts, and I really liked that idea.", "Visited on April 18, 2024.\nAkita University of Art's philosophy is creation, challenge, development, nurturing, and coexistence and co-prosperity. The building is stately and has a sense of aesthetic beauty.", "I used it for my training. I'm really glad that it became a four-year university. Gallery Momosada, which was repurposed from a rice storehouse, was also very nice.", "The campus is spacious and open. The students' vibrant creativity can be felt in each and every display.", "I went there during the open campus. It was really interesting. I'm really glad I came. I felt that it was a place with more individuality than any other university, and where I could do my research freely."]

name: Akita Prefectural University
reviews: ['On my way home from fishing in Oga, I found out about the school festival and decided to check it out. It was fun, but the students were cheerful but didn\'t say hello. name: Akkon University for Human Sciences
reviews:
['The only university in Germany offering the very exciting "International Emergency and Disaster Relief" degree program. The option to study it in a hybrid format (both online and in person) is quite appealing, but unfortunately, it comes with numerous technical problems. Hardly a seminar goes by where the technology works completely smoothly. Furthermore, the video quality via "BigBlueButton" is very poor, resulting in pixelated images.

Missed seminars cannot be viewed later, as no recordings are made.

The content itself is very interesting, with many lecturers from the field. However, the technical implementation falls far short of my expectations, especially considering the high tuition fees.', "Generally, the degree programs are great. The content and lectures are very interesting. If you have other concerns (especially regarding the examination office), it's a disaster. You wait forever for a response, sometimes you don't get one at all, and you have to write a thousand emails or call repeatedly. Sometimes you can't even reach someone during office hours. I'm really disappointed in that regard, especially considering the high fees.", "Good class sizes and excellent lecturers! The content is practical, and the exam formats provide good preparation for further studies (e.g., at ASH Berlin). It's easily compatible with work, family, and other commitments. However, be aware that good self-organization is essential!

The location is easily accessible (subway/bus/bike), but there's a lack of a good student cafeteria nearby.

I enjoyed my studies and highly recommend it!", "Highly recommended!!!

I'm currently studying at Akkon myself and am very satisfied with everything.

The professors are all very dedicated, competent, and approachable. The study groups are a very comfortable size, allowing for effective and high-quality work.

There are solutions for all kinds of problems. Even as a working professional, a part-time degree program is easy to plan.

I'm very satisfied and can wholeheartedly recommend Akkon University!!!", "Plus: Good student-to-faculty ratio.
Bad: Lunch break is difficult. You only have from 1 p.m. to 2 p.m. There's no cafeteria anymore. Lockers are scarce. There are two snack bars with seating a 10-minute walk away. If many students go there, it gets very crowded."]

name: Akrofi-Christaller Institute of Theeology, Mission and Culture
reviews: ["The Akrofi-Christaller Institute of Theology, Mission and Culture is located in Akropong, Ghana.  It's roots date back to a Presbyterian Mission established in the 1800s by German speaking Swiss from Basle, Switzerland.  Amongst their achievements were a dictionary of the Twi and Fanti languages and translation of the Bible into the Twi and Ga languages.", 'Akrofi-Christaller Institute of Theology, Mission and Culture is a scholarly institution established to serve the wider Christian community in Ghana and Africa. The Institute is accredited by the National Accreditation Board of the Ministry of Education (Ghana) as a postgraduate research institution, with a Presidential Charter to award its own degrees.', 'Great institution, well experienced lecturers and theologians. Academic research is its hallmark and quality of education is very high.', 'Its a very nice place. My most interested features include the quietness of the place as well as the respect and tolerance that dwells among the members. And most of them are quite respectful of outsiders as well.\nAnd it is a great place to acquire more knowledge about the gospel of Christ Jesus and Christianity.', "Interesting place to be considering the weather condition. Most importantly the ancient structure over there is still in place and well maintained,in fact it's one of the institute's in Ghana that exhibits decency and respect to all."]

name: Aksaray University
reviews:
['It is very good university.', 'beautiful', 'Aksaray University (ASU) is a state university located in Aksaray, in the Central Anatolia Region of Turkey.

General Information
Year of Establishment: 2006
Affiliated Institution: Council of Higher Education (YÖK)

Type: State University
Campus: Central Campus (Located near Aksaray city center)

Academic Structure
Aksaray University includes:
Faculties (Engineering, Education, Economics and Administrative Sciences, Science and Literature, Islamic Studies, Sports Sciences, etc.)
Vocational Schools
Institutes (Postgraduate Education)
Higher Schools

Education and Student Life
Education is provided at undergraduate, associate degree and postgraduate levels.
Emphasis is placed on applied education.
Social facilities, sports fields, library and clubs are available for students.
The campus has a modern and development-oriented structure.

Location and Advantages
Aksaray has an advantage in terms of transportation due to its proximity to major cities such as Ankara, Konya, and Kayseri. Student living costs are more affordable compared to many large cities.', "The university is small, the trees are small, the canteen and buildings are generally clean, there's a market and a mosque. Aksaray has many historical places to visit, I recommend it if you like traveling. The number of student accommodations is limited and expensive, the minibus lines are insufficient. If you ask about social life for students; there are cafes in the city, but their prices are high and I think students are uncomfortable because of the different types of people there. Restaurants serve until 2 am, some until morning, but only a limited number; when it gets dark, there's nobody on the streets. Shopkeepers seem to have opened their businesses as a hobby. In general, the people of Aksaray don't like foreigners very much and look down on them, but they are good people. I spoke to a real estate agent the other day; he had enrolled his child in university in Niğde and said that house prices there were expensive. If you charge high prices to other people's children here, your child won't be spared elsewhere!!! I told him to his face, he froze 😇😇. What I have written here are my own observations and thoughts. Respectfully.", "Whether the campus is good or bad is debatable. I spent 6 years there, starting in 2014. The campus was a construction site; when we arrived, the engineering faculty was flooded, and the classrooms were insufficient. They later built a new faculty building. Getting from one place to another on campus required a 20-minute walk; even to the cafeteria, there were more foreign students than Turkish ones. The minibuses were always full. There were very few green areas and trees; I don't know what it's like now. It will probably become a proper university in another 20 years. Transportation is a problem in this tiny city; they need a tram line from the city center and the hospital to the university. I had problems finding accommodation; in my time, you couldn't get a place in the dorms, you'd get a place on the waiting list, but then it wouldn't come. Our best years were spent in misery and hardship. Construction never seemed to end inside the university. The municipality tried to do some things, like distributing food and soup, and they provided free buses. Once, the campus was flooded; I've included photos. It was always misery, I hope things have improved now."]

name: Akwa Ibom State University of Technology
Al-Ahliyya Amman University
reviews:
['A disgrace to higher education.
Amman Ahliyya University is an absolute failure as an academic institution. It operates like a profit-driven factory, not a university. Overcrowding is extreme, organization is nonexistent, and the academic environment is chaotic and embarrassing.
The teaching staff is one of the biggest problems. Many lecturers are shockingly incompetent, unprofessional, and clearly unfit to teach. From my own experience, **I witnessed open and shameless gender discrimination by a female lecturer** who consistently favored male students. High grades were handed to male students with ease, while female students were deliberately marked down regardless of their actual performance. This is not “bias” — it is outright academic injustice.
The administration is completely negligent. Complaints go nowhere, accountability does not exist, and students are treated like disposable sources of income. There is no transparency, no fairness, and no respect for students’ rights.
What is truly baffling is how this university manages to appear in any university rankings at all. Based on the reality inside the campus—poor teaching quality, discrimination, overcrowding, and weak administration—its ranking is completely disconnected from the actual student experience.
This university has failed in its most basic responsibilities: providing fair education, qualified instructors, and a safe academic environment. Anyone considering enrolling here should seriously reconsider, because once you are in, you are on your own in a broken, unfair, and deeply disappointing system.', 'My experience at Al-Ahliyya Amman University was shocking and unacceptable. I was subjected to blatant racist discrimination by a doctor who abused their position of power and intentionally gave me an unfair grade based on my identity, not my academic performance or effort.
The favoritism was obvious and undeniable. This doctor clearly favored students from the same ethnicity while marginalizing others, creating a hostile, biased, and deeply unfair academic environment. This is not subtle bias — it is open discrimination.
What is even more disturbing is that the university allows this behavior to exist. I fully blame the administration for enabling and tolerating racism by failing to enforce accountability, transparency, or basic academic integrity. A university that permits such conduct is complicit in it.
This experience has destroyed my trust in the institution. Students deserve equal treatment, not prejudice disguised as grading. I strongly warn prospective students and parents to seriously reconsider before enrolling in a university that allows discrimination and abuse of power to go unchecked', 'We heard about this University from numerous people and we decided to go check it out and register if possible when we arrived at the University I was impressed by the facilities and the organization then we went to the admission office we were welcomed warmly the procedure was easy everybody looked professional they helped us to finish the registration procedure then we visited the The faculty of information technology .it was a clean updated faculty with impressive classrooms.
Keep up the good work.', 'Fancy university high class clean the only thing you have to drive through high traffic to reach

name: Al-Azhar University
reviews:
["Al-Azhar University in Cairo, Egypt, stands as one of the world's oldest and most prestigious centers of Islamic learning. Founded in the 10th century, it has been a beacon of knowledge, scholarship, and religious enlightenment for over a millennium.
This is second oldest university in the world. This university is the Best university for islamic knowledge.
One of the most notable features of Al-Azhar University is its historic Al-Azhar Mosque, which is at the heart of the campus. The mosque's architecture is a marvel, and it remains a revered place of worship and pilgrimage for Muslims. It's also an architectural gem that reflects the rich history of Islamic art and design.
The university's impact on Egypt, the Arab world, and the broader Muslim community is immeasurable. It remains a source of enlightenment, a center for intellectual discourse, and a symbol of the enduring power of knowledge. Al-Azhar University continues to be a vital institution, both in Egypt and the global Islamic community, promoting education, tolerance, and the preservation of Islamic heritage.", 'One of the places must to visit if you ate coming to the market. Is ok for non moslem to enter in no prayer time just make sure to open the shoes and respect them.', "I'm proud of the students who have received the Al-Azhar scholarship in the Faculty of Islamic Sciences. A true center of knowledge and excellence.", 'This is al azhar university, which is established 1100 years ago. This is second oldest university in the world. This university is the Best university for islamic knowledge. There are 6lac students. Also 1lac foreign students.. this university is situated in cairo, egypt. 🇪🇬
The university is the top ranked university in the Egypt 🇪🇬 also world 🌎', 'Very nice.Al-Azhar University is\xa0ranked 701 in\xa0Academic Ranking of World Universities\xa0by\xa0Shanghai Jiao Tong University\xa0and has an overall score of 3.6 stars, according to student reviews on Studyportals, the best place to find out how students rate their study and living experience at universities from all over the world.']

name: Al-Hussein Bin Talal University
reviews:
['Professors are highly inadequate, with the majority not having the proper skills to teach or give lectures, and not being fluent in English either; which is the language of the material that they’re supposed to be lecturing in.

Facilities in faculties are (bad - average), with many that don’t even function, such as water fountains for drinking and numerous out-of-order bathroom stalls.
Some computers in the IT College are not well-maintained, tables in the Engineering Drawing hall are old with most being dirty and scarred (carved).

Not all lecture halls have curtains attached, making the sun an issue for lectures that depend on projectors, and some inconvenience for the students as well.

Transportation to-from the university is somewhat unreliable, the station is usually crowded simply because the means of transportation are not enough, and the people in charge are doing nothing about it.

As a student, your opinion does not really matter as the decision-making officials have no regard for the wellbeing of the university goers. Personally; I’d just wished that they would co-operate with the students more & value their thoughts more often.

I advise the ones responsible of this scientific edifice to immediately start taking action to ensure the delivery of fine education, as well as a decent experience for every student, and achieve the royal vision of King Abdallah II of a well-educated generation capable of combating today’s problems.

You can start with assigning supervisors that overlook lectures, ensuring that lecturers have a standard of providing eduction to those who attend, and co-operating with the students while satisfying their needs, in order to make this university a true landmark of Jordan.', 'These are some pictures of my beautiful university ♡ ^^', "it is wonderful and great university with unique location (in the desert of Ma'an)...environment friendly... fresh air.. wonderful students (they leave their hometowns to enjoy and stay in Ma'an) friendly and helpful staff...enjoy it", 'Very nice', 'Not recommended.

2. Academic Programs: The university offers a wide range of undergraduate, graduate, and postgraduate programs across various disciplines. It includes colleges of Medicine, College of Information Engineering*, Engineering, Science, Political Science, and Information Technology, among others.

Also, the University open the scholarship for the international students.

Al-Nahrain University continues to uphold its mission of excellence in education and research, striving to meet the evolving needs of society and contributing to the global academic community.

It was established in 2012 after the transfer of the Colleges of Agriculture and Veterinary Medicine from the University of Babylon to the new university. This was followed by the opening of the Colleges of Environmental Sciences, Food Sciences and Biotechnology, and Water Resources Engineering. The university's official name is Al-Qasim Green University, but it is sometimes informally known as the Green University of Al-Qasim. While the name Euphrates University of Agricultural Sciences was initially proposed, the official name was later settled on Al-Qasim Green University due to the location of the university's administration and other colleges in the city of Al-Qasim, south of Hilla, the capital of Babylon Governorate."]

Al-Quds University (AQU) is a national research university based in Jerusalem, Palestine. Established in the 70s as the only Arab university in the city of Jerusalem, AQU currently offers 105 undergraduate and graduate programs, taught through its fifteen degree-granting faculties, which include the faculties of Medicine, Dentistry, Pharmacy, Engineering, Law, Medical Professions, Arts and Humanities, Business and Economics, Science and Technology, Quran and Islamic Studies, Education, Public Health, Dual Studies, Bard-AQU, and the Faculty of Graduate Studies.

AQU currently enrolls 12,500 full-time students, with female students constituting more than 55% of its overall student body.

With approximately 860 full-time faculty, the student-faculty ratio at AQU is 15:1.

AQU comprises three main campuses in Jerusalem: Abu Dis Campus, Old City Campus, and Beit Hanina Campus, in addition to several educational centers in other Palestinian cities such as Gaza, Dura, and Zabadeh.

While AQU takes pride in founding in Palestine the first schools of medicine, dentistry, pharmacy, health professions, and law, AQU is equally proud of introducing the first liberal arts college in the entire Middle East region, which was established as part of an ongoing partnership with Bard College of New York. With support from the German government, AQU also founded a pilot academic program known as the "Dual Studies". Modeled on the German approach of merging academic learning with practical training, the Dual Studies program remains unique to the entire Arab world and currently enrolls more than 200 full-time undergraduate students.', 'I love Al Quds University. I studied there four years. I have so much memories at that place. ❤️', 'Best Palestinian University']

On Saturday morning, July 16, 2022, the university's administrative and teaching staff welcomed a delegation from the Syrian Historical Society, which had traveled from Homs Governorate, for a fact-finding visit. The tour began in the central auditorium with a short film introducing the history and establishment of the National Private University in Hama, highlighting its development in terms of construction, organization, and the architectural design of its buildings. I\'m guessing those were ambassadors in the front greeting guest. They were very welcoming and friendly. The ceremony started at 10 am and ended in 30 minutes. That\'s what I call "in and out"', 'I had Wendy Dees for PreCal online. The WORST experience I’ve ever had with a teacher and course. She did not respond to emails, the whole online course was terribly dis-organized, we never knew what was going to be on the exams and it just felt like one big joke. The exams were harder than anything I’ve ever taken. (coming from a full time PreMed student at Auburn University on the Dean’s List continually)I thought I was the only one struggling so I reached out to my classmates online and literally EVERYONE was failing her exams, and majority of these people had completed their homework, gone to tutoring and emailed her just like I did. We all were struggling so bad and Wendy Dees did NOT care one bit. I still have PTSD from this experience. Seriously, my online class started with around 23 people and there were only 9 people left when I dropped the course. TERRIBLE!!!! Why would anyone want to take a course or classes through this Teacher/community college when they truly do not care if you pass or fail the course? I met with her and the Dean to try to find a resolution to this, and there was none. Ms. Dees covered every reason I presented with a sorry excuse. The Dean, who is supposed to act as a NEUTRAL person to resolve the issue, completely and utterly 100% took Wendy Dees side. The WHOLE time. Incredibly unprofessional. I emailed Wendy Dees to let her know that I was most likely going to drop the class to see if she had any last pieces of advice and never heard back from her. The whole experience was completely awful, I can’t stress it enough. DO NOT TAKE WENDY DEES AND DO NOT TAKE ANY CLASSES AT COASTAL ALABAMA IN MONROEVILLE! You will thank me later. They are a complete joke! They make things WAY more complicated and unfair than it needs to be. It’s a community college. Not Harvard. Now relax and enjoy the few photos that I captured with my S10 device 😄', "As a student at Al Ain University's Al Ain Campus, I have had the opportunity to experience the institution firsthand. Overall, I have found my time at the university to be enriching and rewarding. Here is my review of Al Ain University - Al Ain Campus.

Academic Excellence: One of the standout aspects of Al Ain University is its commitment to academic excellence. The faculty members are knowledgeable, experienced, and dedicated to providing quality education. They go above and beyond to ensure that students receive a well-rounded education, combining theoretical knowledge with practical applications. The university offers a wide range of programs across various disciplines, allowing students to pursue their interests and develop their skills in their chosen fields.

State-of-the-Art Facilities: The campus itself is impressive, with modern and well-equipped facilities. The classrooms are spacious and conducive to learning, with advanced audiovisual aids that enhance the learning experience. The library is well-stocked with a vast collection of books, journals, and online resources, providing students with the necessary materials for their academic pursuits. Additionally, the campus has laboratories and workshops that cater to the practical needs of various programs.

Supportive Environment: Al Ain University - Al Ain Campus fosters a supportive and inclusive environment for its students. The staff and faculty are approachable and willing to assist students with their academic and personal needs. The university also offers counseling services and academic advising to ensure that students receive the guidance and support they require throughout their academic journey. There are also various clubs and extracurricular activities available, allowing students to engage in hobbies and develop their interpersonal skills.

Industry Connections: Al Ain University understands the importance of industry connections and strives to bridge the gap between academia and the professional world. The university collaborates with industry partners to offer internships, workshops, and guest lectures that expose students to real-world scenarios and provide them with valuable networking opportunities. These connections contribute to the overall employability of graduates, equipping them with the skills and knowledge needed to succeed in their chosen careers.

Room for Improvement: While Al Ain University has many positive aspects, there is always room for improvement. One area that could be enhanced is the availability of research opportunities for students. Encouraging and supporting research initiatives can further enhance the academic experience and contribute to the overall academic reputation of the university.

In conclusion, Al Ain University - Al Ain Campus is an institution that prioritizes academic excellence, provides state-of-the-art facilities, fosters a supportive environment, and connects students with industry professionals. It is an excellent choice for individuals seeking a well-rounded education in a vibrant and inclusive community. The IELTS center at Al ain University in my own experience is the best in computer delivered exam, if you are considering taking computer delivered exam, consider booking at Alain University. Most of the invigilators are local Indians,  so you will enjoy their company. Now relax and enjoy the few photos that I captured with my S10 device 😄', "As a student at Al Ain University's Al Ain Campus, I have had the opportunity to experience the institution firsthand. Overall, I have found my time at the university to be enriching and rewarding. Here is my review of Al Ain University - Al Ain Campus.

Academic Excellence: One of the standout aspects of Al Ain University is its commitment to academic excellence. The faculty members are knowledgeable, experienced, and dedicated to providing quality education. They go above and beyond to ensure that students receive a well-rounded education, combining theoretical knowledge with practical applications. The university offers a wide range of programs across various disciplines, allowing students to pursue their interests and develop their skills in their chosen fields.

State-of-the-Art Facilities: The campus itself is impressive, with modern and well-equipped facilities. The classrooms are spacious and conducive to learning, with advanced audiovisual aids that enhance the learning experience. The library is well-stocked with a vast collection of books, journals, and online resources, providing students with the necessary materials for their academic pursuits. Additionally, the campus has laboratories and workshops that cater to the practical needs of various programs.

Supportive Environment: Al Ain University - Al Ain Campus fosters a supportive and inclusive environment for its students. The staff and faculty are approachable and willing to assist students with their academic and personal needs. The university also offers counseling services and academic advising to ensure that students receive the guidance and support they require throughout their academic journey. There are also various clubs and extracurricular activities available, allowing students to engage in hobbies and develop their interpersonal skills.

Industry Connections: Al Ain University understands the importance of industry connections and strives to bridge the gap between academia and the professional world. The university collaborates with industry partners to offer internships, workshops, and guest lectures that expose students to real-world scenarios and provide them with valuable networking opportunities. These connections contribute to the overall employability of graduates, equipping them with the skills and knowledge needed to succeed in their chosen careers.

Room for Improvement: While Al Ain University has many positive aspects, there is always room for improvement. One area that could be enhanced is the availability of research opportunities for students. Encouraging and supporting research initiatives can further enhance the academic experience and contribute to the overall academic reputation of the university.

In conclusion, Al Ain University - Al Ain Campus is an institution that prioritizes academic excellence, provides state-of-the-art facilities, fosters a supportive environment, and connects students with industry professionals. It is an excellent choice for individuals seeking a well-rounded education in a vibrant and inclusive community. I am grateful for my time at Al Ain University and would highly recommend it to prospective students.", "A leading university. Has established its name for the community with flexible course  structure and suitable fees. Renovation has been done for not only building accreditation of programs is attained. Vital location in Al Ain City. Suitable fees and study hours. I'm proud that my 2 kids are students in there.", 'I went to ACCA exam their screens are very very small. Definitely you will fail  FR paper if you use their screen to write exam.']

reviews: ['Very trashy consulting and admin service, with the call receptionist girl knowing presque nothing and unbelievably slow handling of request(I didn’t leave my terminal conclusion until I was sure of the abnormality of the service I received). This is totally unfair and unacceptable because the baccalaureate results were only released on June 26th, a week after their refund deadline. This shows poor management and an unprofessional approach to handling student situations. The university should have been more transparent and flexible given the circumstances. Love you Morocco']

I have been trying to get into a class here for 3-4 months but due to me living over an hour away I can't just easily come in and talk to someone when I want to. NO ONE here answers their phones or even their emails. When I finally do get a hold of someone and try to work everything out they tell me that the class I want to attend is full and that they will put me on a WAITLIST and the class starts in A MONTH. this place is ridiculous and not worth your time unless you can talk to someone in person. I will never consider this school again."]

name: Alamo Colleges
reviews:
["Having been close to the campus for a while now, I've had the wonderful opportunity to witness firsthand just how fantastic San Antonio College (SAC) truly is. Through my fiancé's experience as an enrolled student, I've been consistently impressed by the quality of the facilities and the extensive resources made available to them.
What's more, the staff at SAC are incredibly supportive and genuinely invested in their students' success. It’s a campus where every element—from the learning environment to the people who make it run—works together to create a nurturing place for growth. Without a doubt, San Antonio College is a truly outstanding institution for anyone pursuing higher education.", "I have been a student at the Alamo Colleges for several years. Being a full-time student and a full-time employee is not for the weak. That being said, while there are some amazing people I have gotten to help me on this academic journey, the con's outweigh the pro's by far when it comes to SAC specifically.

My honest opinion- choose a different alamo college. I have had better encounters with SPC, NVC, and NLC. Specifically when it comes to needing certain classes to transfer out to a university. I had only one pre-requisite left to be able to transfer out for my BSN, SAC gave me a hard time over the CHEM lab requirement I was missing although I had taken the class and passed with a B. I have had overrides given to me for other courses for this very reason and had no issues in the past with other alamo colleges, but the chair for the biology department is obviously on a powertrip because how can you deny a pre-nursing student her final pre-requisite needed for nursing school? Luckily I got in contact with a helpful lady at NVC so I am registered for that final class needed (No hassle given sac, take notes!) and can finally move on from this place.

You will need to be your own advocate if you enroll here. They do not care, they neglect their students and if its an error on their end, good luck getting it fixed and don't mind the attitude they give you. Be diligent in what you are wanting and don't take no for an answer. I don't play about my education and neither should you!", 'I am interested in registering for a RE class, and needed information about what direction to take. Take the certificate at SAC or Continuing Education.  Anyhow, the rep I needed to speak to was not there and I was given some information which was very helpful. Thumbs up to those who helped from the beginning and directed me to the correct department.', 'Some good professors, but most of the school’s departments are extremely disorganized. Transcripts lost or not processed correctly, advisors who don’t help with degree planning, course materials from the 1980s. And if you complain, you’ll be told to transfer out :)', 'This place is the most disorganized, unprofessional, unhelpful group of individuals I’ve ever had to deal with.  My daughter is a transient student and it has been so exhausting getting any help from anyone at this organization. The advisors NEVER return emails or calls. This joke of a ‘college’ should be shut down  Did I mention they never let you into the Zoom meeting?  Ridiculous…. But I guess you get what you pay for.']

name: Alamo Colleges District
reviews:
['I don’t know much about this college but the architecture and location is very impressive. I’ll certainly be doing a tour with the kids to see if they want to go here', 'I’m extremely frustrated with Alamo Colleges (specifically St. Phillips College) and their registration process. This upcoming semester and the last have been nothing but headaches just to register for classes. Despite following my advisor’s recommendations, every course on my plan conflicts with the others because they’re all scheduled on Mondays and Wednesdays, leaving no options for Tuesday/Thursday classes. To make matters worse, I’m a history major, yet there are no history classes included on my plan at all. How is that even possible?

I can’t even book an appointment for help on their website because I’m not registered for classes. My advisor has been unreachable since Thursday because she’s on vacation, leaving me completely stuck.

The lack of foresight in scheduling, combined with the inability to get timely support, is unacceptable. Alamo Colleges needs to seriously improve their advising process, registration system, and accessibility for students who need help. This has been an incredibly frustrating and discouraging experience.', "Extremely slow with paperwork. I've missed deadlines because they can take months to make updates and they do not offer much help or guidance. Management, policies, protocols, employees, not sure which one needs to change.", "This place is a joke. We have received tons of cards and information about enrolling.They must spend a fortune. Probably could have paid for everyone to get at least their first year for free. Than you go to their website. Again. An overkill. And at the end of the day after following all their instructions and links. Nothing. U still can't enroll. And no one answers the phone.", "They need to manage the website better. You click apply, and it doesnt lead to an application page. Just a bunch of 'learn more' tabs. Why not make it simple. Like when clicking 'apply' having it actually lead to applying."]

name: Alaska Pacific University
reviews:
["APU and Alaska in general are really special places, and I would like to possibly do an MBA here.

I mean look at the other pictures of this place throughout the year, especially in the fall! So beautiful!

I've never been to Alaska as of 2024, but coming from Florida and doing undergrad in Colorado, I have always wanted to visit or even live in Alaska. It's definitely one of my favorite states! So I believe that doing grad school here will help me make that decision for myself.

Thank you!", 'I graduated from APU in 2014 with my Bachelor's in Earth Science. I was beneficial enough to "stumble" across the university during my university search shortly after high school. Coming from a small town in northwest Georgia I was worried about traveling across the country and attending a small liberal arts school in Alaska, but after arriving the residence life staff and the university were beyond welcoming. The school offers "active learning" courses that give students a perspective to learning that goes beyond the confines of a classroom. During my studies, I was able to study earth science first hand from the volcanoes on the big island of Hawaii or the gold mines of Alaska. I would highly recommend the university to any perspective student that wants an active learning experience and true faculty engagement.', 'APU is a World-Class University. It offers excellent course choices for new students locally and internationally. Exceptional instructors. I would highly recommend Alaska Pacific University to those that are serious about getting the best education that is tailored to their needs and professional goals. Keep Up To The Great Work APU', "Update: One positive thing about this University is that they read their ratings. About a month or two months after I posted this, APU finally added their school onto Army Ignite. I still wouldn't recommend it to anyone that's in the military due to their expensive tuition and the lack of experienced  VA workers.

If you’re in the military (especially in the Army) then save your time.

I heard great things about APU, hence why I applied to go here for the Spring semester of 2022. I’m in the Army and use Army Ignite to pay for my courses. The VA department knew that they needed to upload files to have APU available on Army Ignite and failed to so. Not to mention that this school charges around $4k a course, so you would have to be responsible to pay for some of it out of pocket. I was working on resolving this issue for A MONTH. Had I known it was going to be like this, I would’ve went to UAA.

In conclusion, save your time/money.", 'LOVE LOVE ! Extremely hygienic! Clean atmosphere, overall general hospitality and always an experience worth my patients!
Overall probably the best area for my obgyn to be stationed. Lots of medical clinics in this area.']

name: Al Azhar University
reviews:
["Al-Azhar University in Cairo, Egypt, stands as one of the world's oldest and most prestigious centers of Islamic learning. Founded in the 10th century, it has been a beacon of knowledge, scholarship, and religious enlightenment for over a millennium.
This is second oldest university in the world. This university is the Best university for islamic knowledge.
One of the most notable features of Al-Azhar University is its historic Al-Azhar Mosque, which is at the heart of the campus. The mosque's architecture is a marvel, and it remains a revered place of worship and pilgrimage for Muslims. It's also an architectural gem that reflects the rich history of Islamic art and design.
The university's impact on Egypt, the Arab world, and the broader Muslim community is immeasurable. It remains a source of enlightenment, a center for intellectual discourse, and a symbol of the enduring power of knowledge. Al-Azhar University continues to be a vital institution, both in Egypt and the global Islamic community, promoting education, tolerance, and the preservation of Islamic heritage.", 'One of the places must to visit if you ate coming to the market. Is ok for non moslem to enter in no prayer time just make sure to open the shoes and respect them.', "I'm proud of the students who have received the Al-Azhar scholarship in the Faculty of Islamic Sciences. A true center of knowledge and excellence.", 'This is al azhar university, which is established 1100 years ago. This is second oldest university in the world. This university is the Best university for islamic knowledge. There are 6lac students. Also 1lac foreign students.. this university is situated in cairo, egypt. 🇪🇬
The university is the top ranked university in the Egypt 🇪🇬 also world 🌎', 'Very nice.Al-Azhar University is\xa0ranked 701 in\xa0Academic Ranking of World Universities\xa0by\xa0Shanghai Jiao Tong University\xa0and has an overall score of 3.6 stars, according to student reviews on Studyportals, the best place to find out how students rate their study and living experience at universities from all over the world.']

name: Albany Technical College
reviews:
['They dont return phone calls. I work for a trucking company,, and I asked driver if he had his school transcripts, or diploma. This School finally reached back out to him after 3 weeks that it would take them 8-12 weeks for them to send the documents. What a joke. I refuse to take students from that school now.', "DO NOT WASTE YOUR TIME. This institution is not interested in your success, only your money and enrollment. It's poorly managed,   an over-reliance on policy without explanation, poor policy, sloppy problem-solving, and pure inattentiveness from administrative staff. NONE of the instructors look at the test they give you from the test bank they pull from and most have no idea what the curriculum even says (watch out for this in all core classes, its rampant). If you are attending for anything in the medical field they offer NO MEDICAL OR COMPUTATIONAL MATH courses. You just get to earn a credit in general college math and move on which will not help when taking the TEAS. Most everything on the website is not up to date and the IT/connectivity issues this school has is concerning.", 'I recently had a medical emergency and emailed my professors. They will not let me make up any of the assignments. Despite me having documentation, I wasn't allowed to turn in any late work because it wasn't emailed by the assignment "deadline" even though I sent emails before I received the documentation from the hospital. Very shameful.', 'This school has went down as far as customer service, they don’t answer the phone for you. They scheduled me to take the test and when I took off to take it at 1pm they told me late time was too late. They had me to take a number to be called but fail to mention that you had to sign in on a clip board to called to the back. The staff lazy won’t move or get off they cellphones to help you. They should wonder why people chose Albany State to attend.', "I am giving this school a 1 star..
The only thing I loved about this whole experience was my instructor Mrs. Trina with the nail tech program.. Albany tech materials needs to be up to par, schools usually be about their students but this one is mainly about the money. I had to talk with someone in the registrar office on why I didn't receive My official graduation email and they had no freaking idea as to why I didn't receive it. My GPA is a 4.0 and I was told by someone in the registrar office on grad fest that I was considered for honor grad and was redirected to go to the business office to pay for a honor sash and I did just that, I picked up my cap and gown and received my sash from MS. Hawkins. Fast forward to the day before graduation a former student who was in my summer classmate asked me why I didn't come to the honor's day program well for 1 didn't receive not one email or call or nothing so I reaches out to the registrar office to speak with ms Hawkins on why I haven't received an email about the honor's day program, so I sent her an email and asked her about it, she responded that only students with degrees or diploma is eligible for honors. So when I responded back to her email she has not responded back to me and when I called to speak with the president no one will return my call. Pictures are included..
You say because I am in a certificate program I am ineligible for honors if that's the case why did you tell me I qualified and sent me to buy this sash I am wearing in my picture that I am including.

Will I return to Albany tech for anything else?

Absolutely not"]

reviews: ['I’ve been a patient here for almost 9 years is very clean staff is respectful and the nurses and Doctors are amazing. Also they have the bold program or bold buddy for cancer patients. 5 Stars for me', 'Went to the Hutchinson dental center and I had a smooth experience there. Went to get a dental appointment for my self and my daughter. The receptionist was very pleasant and helped my thru the process.', 'This was the worst experience that I have ever had in the hospital. I am a sickle cell patient and I frequent the hospital very often. I came in with an open wound (severe and painful leg ulcer). First off they put me in a chair instead of a stretcher to lay down. My foot is supposed to be elevated instead they had my foot down. Secondly they gave me opioids for pain which makes me drowsy. They should have put me in a stretcher at that point but no..I was so uncomfortable and in excruciating pain. I have been asking to see the charge nurse and they have not gone to get them for me so I can speak to them. This is horrible and poor attention. The only thing I can say is that my PA Rafael was kind and listened to me when I asked him to use the machine to get my veins as I am an extremely hard stick but other than that they need to do better.', "The front desk receptionist on the date of 12/7/24 at approximately 11am. She is so rude asking me if I'm leaving.  When my aunt had a seizure and is in the emergency room.  I returned my pass and she gave me a sarcastic remark: oh hi you leaving bye.  What kind of receiving and comfort to worried family members is that. Smh", "Great hospital to go to if you're not feeling well. The staff r great and so are the doctors. They are patient polite and very respectful. I wouldn't choose no other hospital to go to. I had a severe asthma attack and panic attack at the same time. And they did everything in their power to make me feel comfortable and to help me get through it."]

name: Albertson College of Idaho
reviews:
['Not enough Parking. Street taken and all the spaces in the parking lot. Plan to park far away maybe a block and walk', "I basically had an entire minor where I paid to just do my own personal projects I was already doing. CompSci 0/10

Also they named a newer athletic building after a violent, homophobic, child abusing racist. When it was made clearly evident he simply had a conversation with HR and it was immediately forgotten. Despite repeated incidents the College seem to care more for his popularity with donors than their students' well-being.

Additionally they make you live on campus, buy their terrible meal plans, and good luck holding a full time job when nearly every professor's sending you hours of homework as a substitute for not teaching during actual class time. For how much it costs the non-athlete student with no parental support is at a distinct, almost insurmountable disadvantage.

Great place to send children if you want them to learn about the poor state of modern academia and drown them in expenses.", 'I was sitting at an outdoor table on campus while my wife was in class and a woman approached me and asked if I was a “town person”. She introduced herself as a school staff member and questioned me about why I was there. I felt very unwelcome and she seemed very skeptical that my wife was a graduate student on campus.', "If there were do overs in life I'd go back to school here in a heart beat.  Some of the most memorable times with some of the nicest people you could ever meet in life.  In a matter of a few weeks you got to know most everyone including the professors. Her teachers know her personally and are actively participating in her future.  She's received a broad education from a school that is nationally recognized with a superior staff. name: Alborz University of Medical Sciences
reviews:
['good', 'Very bad response, I went up and down the floors ten times for an answer, they sent me to another floor on each floor, and in the end, my work was not done.', 'Hello
I brought my 3.5-year-old child to Imam Ali Hospital in Karaj due to a mild seizure. After hospitalization, they transferred him to the ICU, and due to a wrong injection, half of the child's upper body became red.
Which resolved with temporary follow-up.
The ward nurse was so negligent in transferring blood to the laboratory that the blood clotted and the blood draw had to be repeated.
The child had a high body temperature and the doctor was not available for nearly 12 hours, and they were telling us to get him checked.
The child had been left with an empty serum in his hand for more than 15 hours, while the blood had returned to his head tube. The nurse connected him to the same head tube after calling the attending doctor. Whatever I said, this blood had been in the tube for 15 hours, and it had become infected and was a carrier of the disease.
He said it had nothing to do with me, we have orders
to change these tubes every three days
The child's blood infection had reached 70, while in a complete test a few days before the child was admitted to this hospital, there was no slightest infection.
Now that I want to transfer the child to another center due to the lack of care, they not only give me a copy of his medical record, but also say, "Give me 3 million Tomans on account along with your insurance book, let's calculate it later." Instead of me complaining, they did a kind of respectful hostage-taking. Now I demand care. If the slightest problem occurs to my child, who is responsible? I didn't want to be responsible. What will happen to my child's life? All the photos and documents are available moment by moment. If that center doesn't take care of it, I will send it to all Iranian and foreign media. If I am not Iranian, order me to go to Israel. There I will lose my religion, not my child. Thanks to Mahmoud Sharafi. Yasmin Sharafi's father, Bed 7, Children's Ward 2, Imam Ali Hospital', 'Stylish and beautiful building', 'پاسخگویی بسیار ضعیف']

name: Albstadt-Sigmaringen University
reviews:
['Best in the region ❤️❤️❤️❤️', "It's aight", 'Can anyone help an new international student in finding accomodation at this university . Please reachout or reply to this post. Thank you!', 'I absolutely love "wrestling" with my Sigmas in Sigmaringen 🤼‍♂️ Educationally, the school isn't all that great, but the printer produces undeniably high-quality prints if you give the German teacher, Ms. Afrorocker, two euros for permission to use it. Rumor has it that the printer is the school's main source of income, which isn't so bad. After all, the school has excellent research equipment, and I've lost count of how many poison gases have been created in the school toilets. In my opinion, it's simply a terrible school 🍲', 'I started my studies in the winter semester of 2020/21 and have had the following experiences since then:

University buildings & grounds:

+ Library within easy reach (directly adjacent to the university building)

+ PCs can be used freely (slow performance)

+ Printing facilities (not free)
+ Free and good Wi-Fi for students
+ Table football
+ Free water dispenser
+ Snack & beverage vending machine (at least the prices aren't completely exorbitant)
+ Coffee machine (large selection of coffees with milk, sugar, and stir sticks, plus it's served at the perfect temperature)
+ Ample parking

- No lockers
- Lab equipment isn't particularly modern, but perfectly adequate for educational purposes

- Campus is relatively empty (A few benches, nothing more)

- Electric entrance doors (must be opened with a switch) -> sometimes simply break down, then the entrance is blocked

Lecturers and teaching methods:

+ Personal relationships
+ Open to direct communication
+ Content largely accessible through self-study from home (online learning videos, digital lecture materials)
+ Many Internships

+ Numerous guest lectures

+ Annual trips to trade fairs and other events (no extra costs)

+ Lecturers are very flexible and accommodating to students' needs (e.g., deadlines, etc.)

+ Many past exams available

- Some students lack digital skills

- There are no make-up exams (if prevented from taking an exam due to public transport or illness/quarantine, the exam can only be retaken at the earliest in the following semester)

- Grading is sometimes based on a Gaussian distribution (the best student receives a 1.0 and is the benchmark for all others; however, the passing grade of 4.0 is fixed in these cases and does not depend on the distribution)

- Terrible timetable: it is not uncommon to have lectures from 5:30 p.m. to 7:00 p.m., even though there is a lunch break/free period starting at 2:00 p.m.

- Some practical courses don't include lectures, but there are graded tests. (Preparation for these tests relies on independent internet research (!!!) using only a few keywords. The content covered is very in-depth and far exceeds the suggested preparation keywords.)

Cafeteria:

+ Inexpensive meals with sides and dessert
+ Dishes are of decent quality
+ Daily: 1x Vegetarian menu
+ Sandwiches and other snacks for breakfast

- Breakfast is unfortunately only available from 9:30 a.m.

- Sometimes very crowded during peak hours

Public transport connections: (significant shortcomings)

- Absolutely below average for a "university town" and a complete planning failure:

- No direct bus connection between the train station and the university

- Buses always arrive and depart 5 minutes after the start and 10 minutes before the end of the lecture period

- The last bus leaves before the end of the last period (I think around 6:40 p.m.)

- You have to go to Schlossplatz to catch a bus from there to get a direct connection to the university

Student life and university offerings:

(Note: I can't comment on the student residence, so it's not included here)

+ University sports (e.g., inline basketball or skate and ice skating -> highly recommended)

+ After the exam period, the traditional "Game Over" party takes place. Very well attended, a real party atmosphere.

- Student "bar" with appallingly awful taste in music; I don't recommend visiting with a blood alcohol level below 0.2%.

- A festival was planned for 2022 but was canceled due to insufficient demand.

- Limited options, even in the city itself: The only place to go is probably Alfons (regular student events, located at the train station: note the exceptionally good bus connections).

+/- (or Gabi's Bar)

This review will be continuously updated.

Best regards, Mais']

name: Alecu Russo State University of Bălți
reviews: ['Enjoyed speaking with staff', 'High level University.', 'The "Alecu Russo" State University of Balti (USARB) is a higher education institution, a scientific, training and cultural center in the north of the Republic of Moldova, which has acquired through its achievements, in over 60 years of activity, a prominent reputation, both nationally and internationally, training qualified specialists who work in various fields of education, science and the national economy. The university campus is placed in a compact manner (study blocks, dormitories, services) in the center of Balti.', "Very disappointed with some teachers from the Department of Educational Sciences, Psychology and Arts! I graduated from the College of Music and Pedagogy with a straight A! I came to college and learned absolutely nothing new! Worse than that, the piano teacher can also teach cymbal, saxophone, clarinet and other classes (Arts Education Department) and you know why because they are not qualified specialists! A teacher who has no idea about folk singing teaches it to those who come to do singing! I am really not ashamed to give the names of teachers who think they have grabbed God by the legs! And who allow themselves to talk behind the students' backs. So let's call them by their names? Or maybe I will stay like that! And what can I say, when they take your documents, they can judge you: like there are no specialists anywhere else besides the university! And more than that, how can a teacher who has no idea about harmony, music theory and solfeggio teach these subjects!", 'The majority of working-age youth do not attend vocational schools, which leads to a decline in the professional level of the workforce. Moldova lags significantly behind many countries in terms of overall youth access to and attainment of vocational education. I can also add that after graduating from lyceums, students from Russian-language schools cannot receive a high-quality education at universities because there are virtually no Russian groups; instead, there are so-called mixed groups, where, naturally, teachers practically ignore the Russian language. It\'s safe to say that the teachers there are ardent nationalists, and this is not just empty talk. I can give an example from my own experience: when you answer a question at the blackboard, no one prompts you, and if they do, it\'s only in Romanian, even though the teachers clearly see that you don\'t understand Romanian well. Another example: there\'s a test, and naturally, all the assignments are in Romanian. Even though all Russian-speaking students are assured that there will be tests in both versions when they apply, this is actually a lie. Furthermore, if a classmate speaks Romanian and asks a question, they\'ll answer and help them right away. But if you\'re a Russian speaker and you ask something, they\'ll either ignore you, answer you in Romanian, or say, "citeste teoria." I\'m not talking about all teachers, and I\'m definitely not lumping everyone together. But I simply believe that if teachers go to teach at a university, they should first and foremost understand that there are both Russian and Moldovan schools, although it seems to me more and more every day that a genocide of Russians is simply taking place in universities. And then everyone wonders where the highly qualified specialists go and why so few teachers are going to work in schools. Just imagine what it\'s like for Russian-speaking students to study in Romanian, with mathematical and scientific terminology, with each lesson introducing something new. At school, all math was taught in Russian, and of course, no one thinks about it. I\'m not even mentioning the official university website, where all information is presented only in Romanian. Now, just think for yourself: is the quality of education in Moldova good or not? And what kind of future can we even talk about?']

name: Aleksander Zelwerowicz State Theatre Academy
reviews: ['very swag', 'Since 1955, the Aleksander Zelwerowicz Theatre Academy has been housed in the former Collegium Nobilium, a school founded in 1740 by Stanisław Konarski. Run by the Piarist order, the Collegium was intended for the sons of magnates and wealthy nobility, intended to educate the future elites of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth. The school operated until 1832.', "A beautiful building designed by royal architect Jakub Fontana, built between 1743 and 1750. The Collegium Nobilium Theatre's actors are students of the Acting Department of the Aleksander Zelwerowicz National Academy of Dramatic Art in Warsaw. The Collegium Nobilium Theatre presents graduation performances by fourth-year students, as well as workshop performances.", "A great school with great traditions 😀 Who among the great actors hasn't studied or lectured here? It's worth coming here, the true history of Polish theater and cinema!!! It's a pity that it's too late for me to become a student of this esteemed university... it's really a pity. Warm greetings to all lecturers and students!", 'The Piarist Collegium Nobilium was a school with the character of a university for the sons of magnates and wealthy nobility. The Collegium building was rebuilt several times. Originally Baroque, it was rebuilt in the style of Polish classicism in the early 19th century. Since 1955, the building has housed the Theatre Academy.']

name: Alfaisal University
reviews:
['For a university that’s supposed to be prestigious, there’s very little consideration for students’ well-being. The workload feels overwhelming and exhausting, with almost no flexibility or understanding, even during Ramadan. Students should feel supported, not constantly drained Academic standards are important but so is treating students with empathy and respect.', 'There is no doubt that Alfaisal University is one of the most prestigious universities in Saudi Arabia and it is characterized by the high quality of education. I visited it once and I admired its atmosphere. The main building is not yet complete', 'Alfaisal University, founded in 2002, in a very short period of time, has become one of the top non-profit university in the kingdom of Saudi Arabia.', 'Ranked between 251-300 internationally, Alfaisal University is one of the best Medical Universities in the Middle Eastern Region.  The infrastructure, campus, and facilities provided for the students is class above the rest in the Kingdom.  High profile Faculty and curricula matching international standards is what makes Alfaisal University the best when it comes to aspiring students of Medicine.', "I took the OET exam at Al Faisal University, which was a great experience. I arrived at the university at 6:30 am on February 8, 2025, due to traffic issues in Riyadh.

The exam center allows you to bring pencils, erasers, and sharpeners, and these materials are also provided by the exam organizers. Registration begins at 7:30 am, where your passport, passport-sized photo, and admit card are checked.

Before registration, you need to deposit your mobile, purse, and other electronic items in a locker room, where you receive a token. This is a great facility that keeps you stress-free during the exam.

After entering the exam hall, the staff provides clear instructions on how to fill in your roll number, name, etc. This is very useful for first-time exam takers.

During the exam, the listening section comes first, where you receive comfortable headsets with excellent sound quality.

This is followed by the reading and writing sections. After completing the writing section, you can exit the exam hall and collect your belongings.

For the speaking section, you need to register again, which takes place an hour before the scheduled time. My speaking exam was at 3 pm, and I registered at 2 pm. The speaking examiner was very cooperative and friendly.

In my opinion, Al Faisal University is a great place to take exams. The staff is very cooperative and friendly.

If you're planning to bring your own food, that's a good idea, as the McDonald's on campus is sometimes closed.

Thanx For Reading ♥️"]

name: Al Fashir University
reviews: ['Lovely interesting', 'Ok', 'The University of El Fasher is a Sudanese public university and a member of the Association of Sudanese, Arab and African Universities. It has cooperation agreements with regional and international universities. Its history dates back to November 1975, when former President Gaafar Mohamed Nimeiry issued Republican Decree No. 8, which provided for the establishment of a university in Darfur. However, this was not realized until 1990, when President Omar Hassan Ahmed Al-Bashir issued Republican Decree No. 142, establishing a university in Darfur under the name "Al-Fateh September University." It was officially opened in El Fasher, the capital of Darfur at the time, in February 1991, in buildings belonging to the Italian company (Reki) west of El Fasher Airport and south of the former El Fasher Secondary School, which was working on constructing some internal roads in the state.', "The two photos are from the university farm, managed by Al-Shafi'i and Walaa. The remaining photos show the activities of the Seed Sustainability Project in North Darfur.", 'House of knowledge']

name: Alfred University
reviews:
['Cool little town and beautiful campus. Check out the “castle” on campus. Museum of Ceramic Arts is small but very interesting. Well worth a visit.', 'This review is not about the school itself or the beautiful music center.  It is about the horrible parking situation during a big event at the music center.  There is not enough parking.  No shuttles, no one directing which way for additional parking, very long walk just to get to the facility.  Very disappointed.  But the building is great and all county was well attended and the kids did great.', 'Honestly speaking, the university has a nice scenery and many resources but the people here are just!!! The food also is mid, not good, not bad, I have gotten sick from the food more times then id like to admit. For the amount of money they charge and the fact that they keep increasing each semester payment by about $1000, you would think new things or better quality would be introduced.

They lure you in with a seemingly nice scholarship but then the semester payments make you have a mini heart attack. Even with loans and financial aid, they still have me paying about $4k for the semester payment. Absolutely ridiculous and not worth the money.

The financial aid office lacks accountability sometimes aswell, if something needs to be updated/done or if something was updated, they won’t even let you know, you have to find out for yourself and sometimes have to dig deep with them.

The counselors for therapy, come on, can we do better?

Again, not worth the money. If you come to this school, I advise you to join off of HEOP or know that you will be able to afford thousand dollar semester payments (2k - 5k+).

Alfred University, please do better. Your financial aid office and their process/effort.

School Rating: 4/10', "Best college I've ever attended!", 'Amazing school. I don’t go here but my girlfriend does and, minus the stress that obviously comes from school, she seems to love it here! Obviously pricey, but you’re really getting what you pay for.']

name: Algebra University College
