name: Al Ghurair University
reviews:
["Its a great university at an ok price, being in the middle of nowhere is bad but you make do over time!

Nothing about this university is perfect, everything is subpar but they don't charge 10k$ a semester so all is forgiven.

I paid an average of 4360$ a semester with 5 courses years ago, price may vary over time tho.", 'I studied at this university after wasting years of my life at a university in Syria, but due to the war, I was unable to complete my studies. Al Ghurair University was one of the first universities to receive me and give me a university scholarship on the condition that I maintain a high grade point average throughout my years of study. I went through many happy and sad times of study, and I finally obtained a university degree and had it certified by the Ministry of Education. I thank all the faculty members who passed by me, and honestly I learned a lot from you, and I thank the founder of the university because he is humane and merciful in a time of materialism. He gave most students the opportunity to study at low prices compared to other universities. I always remember Al Ghurair University and I was very sad because it was closed. I have many memories at this university and I am grateful that I studied and graduated from it.', "If I could describe the worst of the worst universities out there in UAE. Al Ghurair university would be at the top. Worst decision of my life to join. I regret it so much. I hope I could get my years in life back. Some professors were very rude and selfish. Some professors did not teach anything in class. Cheating in exams was also common. Exam papers were already with some students. No supervision of the staff at all. Also, no activities in the college at all. No clubs, no competitions, no sports nothing. Pretty dead place, would remind me of ZombieLand. Just a big box sitting in the middle of the desert in academic city. There are some professors who were so loyal with their work and so good, I can't forget them ever. Something positive here at least. Thank god, thanks to ministry of uae for shutting this university down. Like literally in the morning there was a entire french school who would come in the university to study and our timings would begin at 3 pm onward. I hope this university never opens up again. Everything from the bottom of my heart ❤️", 'Al Ghurair University is the most wonderful place i have been in 3 years to complete my degree in mechanical engineering , thanks for my AGU university .', 'THE UNIVERSITY STAFF IS VARY BAD THEY CANT DO SIMPLE  THING THEY CANT SEND 1 EMAIL LATER TO ANOTHER UNIVERSITY
NOW IT HAVE BEEN TWO WEEKS AND STILL WAITING FOR MY GRADUATION CERTIFICATE']

name: Algonquin College
reviews:
['Highly recommended! Algonquin College is an amazing place to learn! The campus is beautiful and very clean, making it a great environment for studying. The staff are incredibly friendly and always ready to help, which makes the college feel welcoming and supportive. I really love the positive energy around here!', 'I had a good experience studying Bachelor of Building Science / Civil Engineering at Algonquin College between 2014 and 2015.', 'The ac hpat exam at Algonquin College is honestly a joke. Your entire future in health programs is decided by one entry exam, and if you don’t do well, you’re blocked from retaking it for an entire academic year. That means you can’t apply to any health programs for that year, which is completely unreasonable and unnecessarily stressful

I ended up applying to Pre Health Sciences as a backup, and now that program might get suspended, leaving me with no clear pathway forward. It feels like I’m being punished instead of supported.

For a college that claims to care about student success, the system is unfair and poorly thought out. Very disappointing experience overall.', 'This has stuck with me for years, I don't know how many times I've used it as my answer to how was college.

I went a few years back when I was 28. I turned my life around, got clean and finished high school. Having lived so long one way I was unsure of whobibwas or what to do.

I was riding the bus when I saw ad that read "Be who you needed growing up". What I needed was someone to see, and just listen.

This is how I sum up my experience of hoping to make a change for myself and hopefully someone else some day.

Second day we had a teacher post part of the quote from the Toronto mayor. "..jay and Finch are gangsters and thugs..'(I don't have the exact quote, google it). She asked what racist about this?
Everyone started coming up with answer, raising their hand. I looked up the full quote first "the murderers and drug dealers on Jay and Finch are gangsters and thugs".

I raise my hand, stand up when called on, explain how I had friends who aspires to be gangsters and thugs that's not racist. She said something irrelevant.

I then said, do you think low income area is only black people? She said no, then I quickly said the full quote and said "so then you think all black people are murderers and gangster?;I'm just trying to understand how any of those things mean black people.

The class began adding their input, I was told I shouldn't be there,something about privilege. Honestly nothing after that related to what I said or the teacher asked and we just moved on.

On break a few black students came up and said they agreed, to which I said I don't really care what you think right now. In class was the time say anything. I don't care if anyone agrees with when I'm standing up for my views.

So thank college, I was treated the same as I always, but now I make my voice heard when i believe in something. This place will not teach you how to think. It will teach you to take one tiny piece of information, form an opinion and refuse to listen to anyone else if it's not the same. If they make a valid point, attack them make them feel unwanted or racist.

We are people all capable of what we work for. If you feel your a victim, all these things that happened are holdinge back, it's so much harder. Your dragging an anchor, slow down life and limits how far you can go. Things can get pretty tough in life, and stick with you. Reach out and get help for it, work on not carrying the weight. The world will make you feel invisible, like less then other. You are worth something, people do care about you. If something left you lost, maybe try and find a way to help a kid not go through what you did.', 'Their authenticator app consistently locks me out of my student accounts conveniently on days when I have assignments due. Pretty difficult to submit completed work when you can’t get into your own accounts.']

name: Alhosn University
reviews: ['Best ever university,  I liked all faculty members and which as students we really appreciated Dr. Taimour efforts and supports.', 'What is the official website of the University? Someone kindly share.', 'Obtained my ABET Accredited Bachelor Degree in Mechanical Engineering', 'This university is closed indefinitely until further notice.', 'Dr. Taimur is the best professor ever']

name: Al Hussein Bin Talal University
reviews:
['Professors are highly inadequate, with the majority not having the proper skills to teach or give lectures, and not being fluent in English either; which is the language of the material that they’re supposed to be lecturing in.

Facilities in faculties are (bad - average), with many that don’t even function, such as water fountains for drinking and numerous out-of-order bathroom stalls.
Some computers in the IT College are not well-maintained, tables in the Engineering Drawing hall are old with most being dirty and scarred (carved).

Not all lecture halls have curtains attached, making the sun an issue for lectures that depend on projectors, and some inconvenience for the students as well.

Transportation to-from the university is somewhat unreliable, the station is usually crowded simply because the means of transportation are not enough, and the people in charge are doing nothing about it.

As a student, your opinion does not really matter as the decision-making officials have no regard for the wellbeing of the university goers. Personally; I’d just wished that they would co-operate with the students more & value their thoughts more often.

I advise the ones responsible of this scientific edifice to immediately start taking action to ensure the delivery of fine education, as well as a decent experience for every student, and achieve the royal vision of King Abdallah II of a well-educated generation capable of combating today’s problems.

You can start with assigning supervisors that overlook lectures, ensuring that lecturers have a standard of providing eduction to those who attend, and co-operating with the students while satisfying their needs, in order to make this university a true landmark of Jordan.', 'These are some pictures of my beautiful university ♡ ^^', "it is wonderful and great university with unique location (in the desert of Ma'an)...environment friendly... fresh air.. wonderful students (they leave their hometowns to enjoy and stay in Ma'an) friendly and helpful staff...enjoy it", 'Very nice', 'Not recommended.
A uni that lacks EVERYTHING.']

name: Aliah University
reviews:
['Aliah University has a good campus with modern classrooms and labs, especially in the New Town campus. Fees are affordable and facilities like library and hostel are decent. However, placements are not very strong, and students mostly depend on off-campus opportunities. Overall, a good place for academics if you are self-motivated and focus on building skills yourself.', "Aliah University, located in Kolkata, is a prestigious institution that offers a diverse range of academic programs and a conducive environment for learning. Having experienced studying at Aliah University.

The campus infrastructure of Aliah University is not  well-maintained but equipped with modern facilities. The library is a treasure trove of resources, providing access to a wide range of books. The classrooms and laboratories are equipped, creating a conducive environment for practical learning.

The university also emphasizes co-curricular activities and encourages students to participate in sports, cultural events . This holistic approach helps in the overall development of students' personalities and fosters a sense of community within the campus.

Furthermore, Aliah University promotes a diverse and inclusive atmosphere. It celebrates the richness of different cultures and encourages interaction among students from various backgrounds. This diversity enhances the learning experience and prepares students for a globalized world.

In conclusion, Aliah University is a reputed institution that offers education, experienced faculty, modern infrastructure, and a supportive learning environment. It equips students with the necessary knowledge and skills to excel in their chosen fields and prepares them for future challenges.", 'State University in West Bengal. name: Alice-Salomon-Fachhochschule für Sozialarbeit und Sozialpädagogik Berlin
reviews:
['This college seems to produce skilled and caring people in the spirit of its namesake, Alice Salomon. The community outreach is also to be admired.', 'Terrible school for Erasmus students 👍', 'In 2025, the Paste Up exhibition was realized on the pillars of the ASH (Alexander University of Applied Sciences). The exhibition took place as part of the "Dotty Tool" project.

Students from the ASH and many other volunteers from the neighborhood helped with the project.

The project was supported by Elene Misbach (research assistant at the ASH / Campus Transferale),
the artist Anett Lau, Albrecht Fersch, and the neighborhood mothers.', "I'm studying social work here, and I have almost nothing but good things to say. At ASH, great emphasis is placed on discrimination-sensitive perspectives and a critical attitude toward power.
The fact that this doesn't sit well with some people who feel attracted to patriarchy or right-wing structures is evident in the recent recessions. Furthermore, the administration is more than committed to fulfilling its responsibilities. I felt very well looked after from the very beginning, and I find that the content and quality of the courses are of a high standard. When I talk to friends who studied at other universities about the content of my studies, I already notice a big difference.", 'I am studying social work here and I feel good because I can do my studies in peace alongside my other obligations.']

name: Aligarh Muslim University
reviews:
['Aligarh Muslim University is not just a university, it’s a heritage.
Spread across a vast and green campus, AMU is known for its Maulana Azad Library (one of Asia’s largest), beautiful Sir Syed architecture, and a vibrant student life filled with diversity. The environment is rich in culture, history, and knowledge.

A perfect place where tradition meets modern education. ✨', 'One of the best university in India. Aligarh Muslim University (AMU), located in Aligarh, Uttar Pradesh, is a prestigious institution with a rich legacy of academic excellence and cultural heritage. Established in 1920, evolving from the Mohammedan Anglo-Oriental College founded by Sir Syed Ahmad Khan, AMU stands as a beacon of inclusive education, welcoming students from diverse backgrounds across India and beyond.', 'Aligarh Muslim University (AMU) is a great place to study. It is affordable and has a lively and positive environment. The teachers are well-qualified and very helpful. The campus life is enjoyable with many fests, clubs, and student activities. The university has very good facilities, including big libraries and good infrastructure. AMU also focuses on the overall development of students, not just studies. Although placements in some core branches are not always the best, most students have a good experience here. Overall, AMU is known for its quality education, diversity, and long history, which makes it a very good university.', "The university's rich history and heritage are evident in its architecture, curriculum, and faculty. The campus is beautiful, with lush green lawns and historic buildings. The faculty is experienced and knowledgeable, and the academic programs are well-structured. The university's focus on Islamic values and culture is unique and adds a sense of community to the campus. The university also has a strong alumni network, which is beneficial for career growth. Overall, AMU is a great place to learn and grow.", 'Historical and architectural Central University that was founded in 1875 as a Madrasa
Strachy Hall']

reviews:
['Academic Support:- The lectures and guidance from faculty have strengthened my knowledge and inspired me to explore beyond the syllabus.

All the departments are well maintained and have a very clean atmosphere.", 'Very Good Institute/university it is. name: Allahabad Agricultural Institute, Deemed University
reviews:
['Located in the historic city of Prayagraj (formerly Allahabad), Uttar Pradesh, the Sam Higginbottom University of Agriculture, Technology and Sciences (SHUATS) stands as one of India's most prestigious and oldest institutions dedicated to agricultural education and rural development. Established in 1910, it was the first of its kind in India and has since evolved from a humble agricultural institute into a comprehensive, multi-disciplinary university.
Historical Legacy: The "Gospel and the Plough"
The university was founded by Dr. Sam Higginbottom, an American Presbyterian missionary who arrived in India with a vision to modernize agricultural practices and alleviate poverty. Guided by the motto "The Gospel and the Plough," the institution aimed to combine spiritual values with practical scientific training.
A major milestone in its history occurred in 1942 when it became the first institute in Asia to offer a degree in Agricultural Engineering. In recognition of its significant contributions to higher education and research, the Government of India granted it the status of a "Deemed-to-be University" in 2000.
Academic Infrastructure and Excellence
SHUATS is spread across a sprawling, lush green campus of approximately 1,020 acres. It is organized into several constituent colleges and schools, catering to over 10,000 students.
Key Academic Units:
Naini Agricultural Institute: The heart of the university, focusing on core agricultural sciences.
Vaugh Institute of Agricultural Engineering and Technology (VIAET): Renowned for its engineering programs.
Warner College of Dairy Technology: One of the premier centers for dairy education in India.
**Jacob Institute of Biotechnology and Bio-Engineering: Focusing on cutting-edge research in life sciences.
The university offers more than 300 academic programs, ranging from undergraduate to doctoral levels. While agriculture remains its flagship domain, SHUATS has diversified into Forensic Science, Theology, Business Studies, Health Sciences, and Humanities.
Accreditation and Rankings
The university maintains high standards of quality, evidenced by its accreditation and rankings:
NAAC: Accredited with an 'A' Grade.
ICAR: Its agricultural programs are accredited by the Indian Council of Agricultural Research.
NIRF: It consistently features in the National Institutional Ranking Framework, particularly excelling in the Pharmacy and Agriculture categories.
Campus Life and Facilities
The SHUATS campus is a self-sustained township featuring:
Hayes Memorial Mission Hospital: A fully functional hospital providing healthcare to students and the local community.
Research Centers: Numerous experimental farms and research stations where students gain hands-on experience in crop production and animal husbandry.
Hostels: Comprehensive residential facilities for both male and female students, including international hostels for foreign scholars.
The university is also known for its Career Placement Cell, which facilitates recruitment with leading organizations like Mahindra & Mahindra, ITC, Escorts, and various NGOs and government bodies.', 'Established in 1910\xa0as "Allahabad Agricultural Institute".\xa0In 1942, it became the first institute in India to offer a degree in\xa0Agricultural Engineering. In December 2016, UP State cabinet elevated the institution from the status of Deemed University to full-fledged University by passing the SHUATS Act operational from 29 December 2016, thus renaming it to SHUATS. University possess 1020 acres lush-green campus, including 400+ acres of research farms. It is divided into constituent schools and separate blocks for administration.University has 12 hostels on campus, 6 women's, 4 men's and 2 international hostels.
The campus has many facilities like an open-air theatre for cultural events. Several\xa0football, hockey and Cricket grounds; tennis, basketball, squash and volleyball courts. are built on campus. The campus houses a shopping complex, several cafeterias, restaurants, ATMs, post office, a centre of\xa0Krishi Vigyan Kendra\xa0and a branch of the\xa0State Bank of India.', "This was a good college experience. Academically, was not as expected. The college has a superb infrastructure. Has good facilities for practical and labs. It has all kinds of workshops and labs. However students get less help from the college administration to grow one's entrepreneur skills. The placement is very good for its related branches while for other core branches, it's not so good. Fee is high in terms of tuition and hostel fees but Food in the hostel is very good as compared to other colleges.", 'The first Agricultural Institution in India ,best known for BSc in Agricultural Sciences and Agricultural Engg.Though the Engg Faculty isnt that promising except for College of Agri ad College of Agri engg', "Best Agricultural State University in India and for all other courses especially allied sciences. 1st Institute to offer agriculture engineering degree in India. I completed my B.Tech Biotechnology in the year 2018 now I'm in Ireland completed my MS in Pharmaceutical Sciences. Teachers treat you like family not all of 'em. My Blessings, prayers and best wishes always with my Alma Mater. God Bless you abundantly. Highly recommend if you want learn about life to become a better human being not just for studies and getting a degree."]

name: Allahabad University
reviews:
["The University which given 3 Prime Ministers to India 'Allahabad University'; one of India's oldest and most prestigious institutions, boasts a rich history and academic excellence. Located in Prayagraj, it offers a wide range of courses across various disciplines. The university has a strong alumni network, including notable figures like Harivansh Rai Bachchan, Jagdish Chandra Mathur, and Narendra Mohan. Allahabad University is known for its research-oriented approach, fostering critical thinking and intellectual growth. Its sprawling campus and state-of-the-art facilities provide an ideal environment for students to thrive. With a legacy spanning over a century, the university continues to uphold its tradition of academic brilliance and innovation. Its alumni have made significant contributions in various fields, making it a revered institution in India.", 'University of Allahabad – My Alma Mater
The University of Allahabad, established in 1887, holds a truly special place in my heart. As my alma mater, it has been more than just an academic institution—it has been a transformative journey where I learned, grew, and discovered so much about life and myself.
The campus itself is a beautiful blend of architectural styles. From Hellenistic influences to majestic domes and intricately designed arches, the university stands as a testament to historical depth and cultural richness. Every building tells a story, creating an atmosphere that inspires learning and introspection.
One of the most remarkable aspects of my experience was the dedication of the professors. Each one poured their heart and soul into teaching, going beyond textbooks to ensure that students truly understood and connected with the material. Their passion was contagious and deeply motivating.
The senior students also played a vital role in shaping the university environment. They were kind, supportive, and always willing to guide juniors—not just in academics, but in navigating campus life as well. Their genuine care created a strong sense of community and camaraderie.
Reflecting back, my time at the University of Allahabad was a wonderful experience—rich in knowledge, friendships, and unforgettable memories. It’s a place that shaped who I am today, and I will always carry its legacy with pride.', 'Best university, The university takes a lot of time to do any work properly. The admission process here is very slow. One should work a little faster,but environment is good.', '"My experience at Allahabad University has been good. The teaching staff is supportive, and the campus has a unique heritage vibe. But some facilities need improvement like hostel and management."', "Allahabad University: A Legacy of Learning
A Pillar of Indian Education
The University of Allahabad, nestled in the historic city of Prayagraj (formerly Allahabad), is one of India's oldest and most prestigious institutions of higher education. Established in 1887, it has played a pivotal role in shaping India's intellectual landscape.
A Rich History
From its inception, Allahabad University has been a beacon of knowledge and a breeding ground for eminent scholars, politicians, and social reformers. Its illustrious alumni include numerous Nobel laureates, freedom fighters, and leaders who have made significant contributions to India and the world.
Academic Excellence
The university offers a diverse range of undergraduate and postgraduate programs across various disciplines, including arts, science, commerce, law, medicine, and engineering. It is renowned for its rigorous academic standards and dedicated faculty.
A Vibrant Campus
The sprawling campus of Allahabad University is a hub of intellectual activity, with state-of-the-art facilities, well-stocked libraries, and modern laboratories. The university's vibrant student community organizes a plethora of cultural, literary, and sports events throughout the year.
A Legacy to Cherish
As Allahabad University continues to evolve, it remains committed to its core values of academic excellence, social responsibility, and national service. It stands as a testament to the enduring spirit of learning and innovation, shaping the future of India."]

name: Allama Iqbal Open University
reviews: ['I did my thesis research with a professor in this university. Labs are up to date and have all the required softwares. The place is really neat and environment is good.', "It's a best institute for study from home. Teachers are very cooperative. Campus is also very good and situated in a best area of Islamabad. It's very near to metro bus station.", "The Allama Iqbal Open University (AIOU), located in H8/2 Islamabad, Pakistan on Prof. Ashfaq Ahmad road near Peshawar Morr. It is a prestigious university that offers educational opportunities not only to Pakistani students but also to students from other countries. It is the largest university in Pakistan, providing degrees from matric level to PhD level, allowing students to study from the comfort of their homes or offices.\nAIOU holds the distinction of being the world's fourth largest institution of higher learning, with an average annual student enrollment of over 1.2 million, with the majority being women. The university has 44 regional campuses and centers spread across Pakistan, creating a peaceful and conducive environment for education. It's truly a place to be cherished. It would be beneficial to have helpful staff members stationed at the gate to guide students regarding their queries and provide information about available facilities within the building. Since many students come from distant remote areas, it is crucial to accommodate them with proper guidance and ensure suitable seating and accessibility to cafeteria areas. AIOU offers a wide range of more than 2000 courses, making it a hub of diverse educational opportunities. Personally, I completed my B.Ed. and M.Phil in History from AIOU, and I am grateful for the enriching experience it provided to me.", 'It’s really disappointing that AIOU did not cancel or reschedule the paper held on Saturday, despite the fact that all main roads between Islamabad and Rawalpindi were closed. Many students couldn’t reach their exam centers due to road blockages, yet the university still continued the exam as if everything was normal. This is completely unfair to the students who worked hard and were unable to attend through no fault of their own. AIOU management should be ashamed and must take immediate action to provide another chance for the affected students.', "The Allama Iqbal Open University (AIOU) is a biggest  university in Islamabad, Pakistan. The university is the world's fourth largest institution of higher learning with average student enrolment of more than 1.2 million per year and the majority are women. The university has 44 regional campuses and centers throughout Pakistan. Worth mentioning is that more than 2000 courses are being offered.Peaceful place with educational envirnment. simply love this place. Anyhow, the behaviour of the secrurity staff is very inferior. There must be a helping staff at Gate that can guide student about their queries and other proceeding/ facilities available in building. Usually student came from distant remote areas so these students must be accommodated with proper guide. Anf should have suitable sitting and accessibility to cafeteria areas."]

name: Allameh Tabataba'i University
reviews:
['This place was the true meaning of this story from Saadi's Golestan:
"They asked Luqman: Where did you learn manners?"
He said: From the rude; I avoided doing whatever I found distasteful about them."

You see many evil creatures dressed as humans!

A place where you would like to learn goodness, righteousness, humanity, and honesty, but on the contrary, by seeing these strange creatures and monsters, you will understand that you cannot learn knowledge in a place called a university, and you may even distance yourself from being human for miles.

You will understand how wrong you are about some things and how awful a place can be!!
A psychology university that needs a hundred expert therapists to treat only its own staff, if only they could be treated!!', 'Facualty professors of industrial management & IT are among the bests in IRAN but general condition and educatinal welfare of students especially postgraduates is one of the worst universities in this country.
Because of these problems: Misbehavior of employees and lack of accountability, lack of metro station and poor access to transportation, dominance of religious ideology, misbehavior of security and guards, structural and procedural problems, and a lot of bureaucracy, Lack of the most basic facilities for graduate students, such as proper and full-time access to the largest search engines and scientific databases.', 'ATU comprises a diverse range of faculties, schools, and colleges, including:

Faculty of Communication Sciences
Faculty of Economics
Faculty of Law and Political Sciences
Faculty of Management and Accounting
Faculty of Mathematical and Computer Sciences
Faculty of Persian Literature and Foreign Languages
Faculty of Physical Education and Sports Sciences
Faculty of Psychology and Education
Faculty of Social Sciences
Faculty of Theology and Islamic Knowledge
ECO College of Insurance (ECOI)
School of Graduate Studies', "I spend ten months in Iran, completed language course at Persian language center Qazvin and recommended by the Education Ministry of Iran to Allamah Tabatabai University for September 2022. University administration waisted one and half month for registration process. Finally told me that I will receive interview letter but after two weeks I was informed by third person that my admission has been deferred to the next term 2023. Consequently I got back to my country. Iranian administration is accustomed of delaying work and humiliate students. Foreign student must not go to Iranian universities for studies.
Teaching staff may be fine but administration doesn't deserve even zero. Without star I was not able to comment, that is why I have selected a star.  مدیران تنبل و احمق", "I only give it two stars just because of some great professors whose efforts are going to waste in this university.

I sure had some problems with some profs (one of 'em failed me just because of her personal reasons and they made attend volleyball courses despite given documents which showed I have heart-related problems).

They treat students very badly.They've got this passive-aggressive attitude when it comes to answering questions and doing your never ending paper work.

The staff is pretty inefficient to the point of being rude and insulting.Once there was this huge crowd of students in front of the education building and I tried to get in just to ask some questions and this guy who does the cleaning work (long face , short stature) pushed me which reminded me of some old cartoons but I lost my hope in this place anyway."]

A place where you would like to learn goodness, righteousness, humanity, and honesty, but on the contrary, by seeing these strange creatures and monsters, you will understand that you cannot learn knowledge in a place called a university, and you may even distance yourself from being human for miles.

You will understand how wrong you are about some things and how awful a place can be!!
A psychology university that needs a hundred expert therapists to treat only its own staff, if only they could be treated!!', 'Facualty professors of industrial management & IT are among the bests in IRAN but general condition and educatinal welfare of students especially postgraduates is one of the worst universities in this country.
Because of these problems: Misbehavior of employees and lack of accountability, lack of metro station and poor access to transportation, dominance of religious ideology, misbehavior of security and guards, structural and procedural problems, and a lot of bureaucracy, Lack of the most basic facilities for graduate students, such as proper and full-time access to the largest search engines and scientific databases.', 'ATU comprises a diverse range of faculties, schools, and colleges, including:

Faculty of Communication Sciences
Faculty of Economics
Faculty of Law and Political Sciences
Faculty of Management and Accounting
Faculty of Mathematical and Computer Sciences
Faculty of Persian Literature and Foreign Languages
Faculty of Physical Education and Sports Sciences
Faculty of Psychology and Education
Faculty of Social Sciences
Faculty of Theology and Islamic Knowledge
ECO College of Insurance (ECOI)
School of Graduate Studies', "I spend ten months in Iran, completed language course at Persian language center Qazvin and recommended by the Education Ministry of Iran to Allamah Tabatabai University for September 2022. University administration waisted one and half month for registration process. Finally told me that I will receive interview letter but after two weeks I was informed by third person that my admission has been deferred to the next term 2023. Consequently I got back to my country. Iranian administration is accustomed of delaying work and humiliate students. Foreign student must not go to Iranian universities for studies.
Teaching staff may be fine but administration doesn't deserve even zero. Without star I was not able to comment, that is why I have selected a star.  مدیران تنبل و احمق", "I only give it two stars just because of some great professors whose efforts are going to waste in this university.

I sure had some problems with some profs (one of 'em failed me just because of her personal reasons and they made attend volleyball courses despite given documents which showed I have heart-related problems).

They treat students very badly.They've got this passive-aggressive attitude when it comes to answering questions and doing your never ending paper work.

The staff is pretty inefficient to the point of being rude and insulting.Once there was this huge crowd of students in front of the education building and I tried to get in just to ask some questions and this guy who does the cleaning work (long face , short stature) pushed me which reminded me of some old cartoons but I lost my hope in this place anyway."]

name: Allan Hancock College
reviews:
["Im halfway done with AHC and I have absolutely no regrets with choosing to go here. With all the programs offered and the helpful staff and environment it made being away from school for 10 years and 3 kids later not so hard. Im in the CARE program for parents and apart of the Learning Assistance Program for extra support and financially it has its benefits as well as a student. I feel cared for and I know where to go if I need help. You get the results that you put into your education. The only way out of poverty is through an education. The EOPS department has gone above and beyond for me and my family. I mainly do online classes, but there's only been one class I had to go to in person for my specific degree because of the lab needed. So glad I went to my local community college.", 'First year back in many moons. It was a learing curve for sure since everything is online. Took a min to learn the Canvas app and navigate thru the school website. The good thing is they do have very friendly staff that is very willing to help. Which made it a much more user friendly experience. The campus is easy to understand and navigate thru and the amenities are top notch.', "A bit late for a review, but better late than never. Started here Fall 2016 as a non-traditional student. I transferred to Cal State Channel Islands Fall 2019. In the 2.5 years I was at AHC, I met some wonderful, amazing people. The school has a reputation for being the college version of a feeder school. In other words, students can start here and get into places like Cal Poly, or indeed any of the other Cal State or UC Schools if they work hard and play their cards right. That definitely isn't the case elsewhere.There's tons of scholarships and support programs available with a good chance of getting accepted as well. Like most things in college, you get out of it what you put into it. The only downside is it's a bit of a commuter school. When classes let out, the place turns into a ghost town because most of the students commute in and have part time jobs along with studying. That being said, the teachers and counselors really do care about the students here.", "I just finished my first semester as a returning student.  I am very impressed with the improvements the college has implemented since I have been away.  Each department has implemented strategies which will allow students to have more effective communication channels and will allow them to have a higher success rate.  The student body Government is doing great and developing awesome new community leaders in the process.  People here are super accepting of people's diverse backgrounds and this is a great place to go to learn about our culture in a safe environment for people from all walks of life.

I am super proud to be a student at Allan Hancock College and I am looking forward to the next semester.  Hancock is now offering a program offered by them through Cal Poly San Luis Obispo called 2 + 2.  This allows Hancock to offer students the ability to get a four yearw degree in Sociology and Business!  I am a business major and I am way happy about this and will look forward to new four year degree offerings.

The Hancock bookstore is operated by Follett, who has a long and proud history providing text materials to education for years.  They are a great resource and I am so happy to have such a prestigious institution helping us get the materials we need to be successful.

Thank you Allan Hancock College for providing such a safe and awesome educational experience for anyone who wishes to enrich their educational experience with a tradition of excellence in providing the best instructors and classes that are available for any person who wants to better themselves with no exception!", 'I attended AHC and played on the soccer team.  This was a while back but my overall experience there was great.  Easy parking, not over crowded and the counselors were great in assisting me as I transferred to Cal Poly.  Save some cash and go to AHC!!!!!  Worth it.']

name: Allegany College of Maryland
reviews: ["To go to this college you have to submit to completely invasive digital surveillance inside your own home.  You will be required to take tests at home because professors are too lazy to proctor their own tests, so use AI to proctor tests (you're not allowed to use AI for anything, but your professor will use AI for everything, including assessing your work).  You are expected to use HonorLock, which requires you to submit your biometrics, a photo of a government issued license, and video of your home (yes, an actual video of your home; to make sure you're not cheating, but of course someone stores that video and now a recording of your private home is made public to the university).\n\nGovernment run schools are preparing to accept invasive digital surveillance inside your own personal, private spaces.  Never forget that a lot of what government run schools are is to prepare you for the dystopian plans they have to monitor you at all times for the purposes of control, while accepting those in authority are not subject to the same monitoring and control.", 'Had my teeth examined exrayed and cleaned. They are students so they are a little slower. They must ck in with their teachers. It may take more than 1 visit to get your wk completed. However, were very kind and worked to try to make the process comfortable. Great price! They charged $30 or $40. My Dentist wanted $400 after insurance.', 'The Allegany College of Maryland campus offers an exceptional academic environment and supportive community that empowers personal and intellectual growth. Its faculty is a notable asset, with professors who are both renowned experts in their fields and dedicated educators who prioritize the development of well-rounded individuals through comprehensive learning experiences that extend beyond the classroom.', 'I have visited so many colleges. This is one of the very few I would consider attending (and I currently am). I chose this school because the professors and staff actually help the students. The campus is smaller, but in no means lacking. I always feel so welcomed here. This college really is what you make of it, you can have the full college experience if you want tom', 'This school nursing program is racially profiled. All white board of nurses is laughable. Upload your picture to your profile for a grade… huh? Why? Your pictures are not on the website! Their nursing program is a joke failing student because of clinical skills, when they are literally passing academically. What a money scam. The nursing bag that is almost $100 bucks does not have enough supplies to complete a skill with. So, basically make believe all the way through. If you are black and dark complexion like me, don’t waste your money and time here. Go traditional!']

name: Allegheny College
reviews:
["Fantastic school hidden away in Crawford county PA.
They also have some of the best food in the area. Wether you're eating at the school or taking advantage of their catering Dept you can't go wrong!", 'It's a small liberal arts college that is listed in the "100 colleges that change lives" book and it does indeed change lives. The culture is that everyone smiles at each other walking around the beautiful natural campus & it only has around 1,000 students total so you get to know everyone. Students have easy access to their professors and a personal relationship with their advisor. Allegheny is the 32nd oldest college in the United States and the oldest college in continuous existence under the same name west of the Allegheny Mountains.

These photos show some of the rhododendrons in bloom in the ravine on campus & the view from inside the crowd while someone took a group alumni photo during the 200th anniversary reunion celebration in 2015.', 'Beautiful campus, very well respected  college in Pennsylvania and my alma mater.  Proud that it is an environmentally responsible school.', "Beautiful school, small campus, $hi+ whole town. Out of people that live close to school and work at the places students work and shop, most seem to lack integrity, and basic manners. From my experience. Some are wonderful beautiful beings. Unfortunately that place, seemingly, sucks that out of you the longer you are there. (NOTHING to do there...I mean NOTHING, unless you're into drugs bars and skills machines. 🙁🤷🏻‍♀️", 'Great campus and sights to see.']

reviews: ['Allegheny Wesleyan College is a Christ-centered Bible college that offers solid academics and a genuine Christian atmosphere. The staff and students are friendly and welcoming, and both classes and chapel services reflect AWC’s mission to put God first while preparing students for ministry and service wherever He leads.', 'All because the cutest guy attends there😉', 'Best college ever! These were some of the greatest days of my life. Quality Godly education!', 'It was great to fellowship with the good staff and wonderful students.', 'This is definitely one of my favourite Bible colleges 👍']

name: Allen College
reviews: ['I’m Happy my sis recommended me this school, everyone was nice they were all very friendly, professional. I had Gabriel omg she is an angel in disguise because she is loving well spoken well groomed and right there I knew I had a new younger  sister. I just can’t wait to get started. The speakers were well spoken as well well groomed they were nice and they were helpful. I would give them more than 5 stars if I could.', 'Good afternoon everyone, I hope this message send people encouragement and to continue to keep fighting, these past few days I haven’t really decided what career I want to pursue but I kept telling myself  to go for  for medical assistance since it always has been a dreamed to help others short story I was so close to being enrolled to further my career until they told me they don’t accept my (Penn Foster) high school diploma I was hurt because  I worked so hard to get this high school diploma, Ms Vanessa also told me that I should go get a GED and it broke me  because I’m just a young black female trying to make a career for myself i understand she was doing her job so it’s no disrespect to her but i just want to tell everyone and everybody  just keep thriving   Back than you need a high diploma to work etc now I need a actual high diploma to  build my career is sad but this was my experience you live and learn many opportunities are out there  just don’t give up', 'I had a Great experience speaking with Gabby. She definitely gave me more confidence applying for this school.', 'I love my advisor and everyone there. They brighten your day, there is never a full moment when with them and i cant wait to learn', "I am so disappointed with Allen School. This review is not to get back at any specific individual but I think people should know. My daughter went to Allen school, completed her modules, but she still owed the school money so she wasn't allowed to do her internship. The school called her telling her she can come in before the six month dead line to pay, so she can do her internship. She went in payed the money,  spoke with the financial office person who made it clear that my daughter didn't owe any more money. On the day my daughter was given her site information to start internship she received documents in an email to sign, she wasn't aware what she was signing in her head she's thinking it's documents to start her internship. That wasn't the case, suddenly the school started sending emails saying she is owing money which is alot more than her first agreement when she started. They enroll my daughter all over again. My daughter came in before the dead line. To my knowledge someone didn't process the paper work on time before the dead line. So my daughter now has to pay for someone messup. And their explanation is blamed on financial aid and the government funding. They told my daughter one thing and did something else behind her back without her knowledge and never discuss any new bill with her. They made my daughter do a dock sign and that can be transfer to any document. Now they are not giving her the certificate so she cannot get a job, and she is owing the school money. Together with what she had already paid it will total over three thousand dollars Just for a  medical assistant  certificate. Be careful with Allen School. Just to mention my daughter was eligible for fancial aid. And she also got a loan she received from the government to help with the tuition that she have to pay back. Sad.."]

reviews: ['Kids on college baseball team were very good ambassadors of Allen. When I asked about the coaching staff one said " coach will be at my wedding" and "i met my best friend here". I'm very honored to be a ACCC graduate. -Greg Watt", 'This is a great junior college. The professes are passionate about what they do & the student-to-teacher ratio is really nice. reviews:

reviews: ["The distance learning program is extremely flexible and can be perfectly combined with work. name: Allentown College of Saint Francis de Sales
reviews:
['Event in University Center: Excellent parking, very good food and table service.', "We had our 60th class reunion in the activities building. The venue and food we're very nice.", 'Awesome campus! Also convenient daily Mass at the chapel.', 'I love this school! Most especially because as a veteran, I feel like I belong, and Mrs. Wendy Badman, the Director of Veterans & Military Services, really cares about the veterans, military-affiliated students, and our families! She does so much for us and the community. I’m so happy and grateful to be a Student Veteran of DeSales University!!!', "2025 Edit: Do not go here if you want to be Catholic. That ship has sailed.

The Campus is clean, and well maintained. The food isn't too bad. But aside from these constants, you will have a different experience depending on your worldview going in. The theology and philosophy courses you will be required to take will be authentically Catholic, which will either encourage or enrage you depending on if you are a practicing Catholic or not. Other general ed's will be standard, but sufficient.

DeSales University is going to be a decidedly different experience depending on your major as well. If your major is in the arts and you are a practicing Catholic, you will have a tough time since most of the people in that major will be agnostic or atheist (and professors may be geared towards that). However-the actual theory and practical application of the discipline you are studying is going to be very good and useful.

The people you meets will likely be very nice, and you won't have a tough time finding friends if you try.

The faith life of campus is pretty good, with various to groups to encourage you. There's a small community of students who attend daily mass, which is always a help. The Mass itself however (Sunday and weekday), leaves a bit to be desired. The music especially will sound a bit 1970's, instead of having the timeless characteristic of a liturgically accurate mass. Gregorian Chant or Polyphony would sure help here, but the choir director is a protestant at the moment (for some reason) so that is unlikely.

All in all, whether you are a practicing Catholic or an unabashed pagan you will have some issues with DeSales, but the academics themselves are good and you will likely be prepared for your field upon graduation."]

name: Allen University
reviews:
['It is among the Nations Oldest and greatest institutions of higher learning.  An awesome HBCU. Your High School Graduates will be elated that they chose this place to further their education.', "My godfather, DR. Brian Newsome took me to a gray collegiate basketball game on President's day weekend so I can get time away from home, but getting a wonderful GCA fan experience so I can break my personal addiction of carrying my car brochures around everywhere I go. Overall, the bathrooms were pristinely cleaned and sanitized and it was awesome. Gray defeated Whale Branch by a good margin. #WAREAGLE", '⚠️ warning⚠️if you are trying to live on campus in the dorms YOU CANNOT HAVE VISITORS AT ALL !! The workers will watch the cameras 25/8 so even if you try to sneak someone in they will find out and you will get charged for a $75 dollar fee.

The dorms also cost $9,012 PER SEMESTER just to not be able to do anything. You can only have a microwave and a fridge. You can’t have any family come visit either or they will make your family leave their id at the desk. You also cannot have ANY kitchen supplies except for forks, knives, and spoons. Anything else they will take and charge a $100 fine for that as well.

They do not tell you any of this until you are already here and moved in. It is a setup so if you prefer freedom I would get off campus housing. Redpoint and The Rowans cost $600 a month (per person) but it comes with a bed already. $600x12= $7,200 versus paying $18,024 for no freedom.', 'This is school is by far one of the worst HBCU. It’s sad because it wasn’t like this when I was in college. The Administration is very unprofessional and rude. It’s sad that when Dr Spearin left and a few other good professors this schools went down. The shootings and robbery they keep quiet about. The professors who call themselves Dr are not any guidance it’s as if they’re there for a check not the betterment of the kids. Oh and why do they take forever and a day to disperse those kids refund checks like
It’s theirs. Just utter lack of taste. Trash…', 'Honestly I wish I could give a 0. I used to be proud to say I went to this HBCU. Especially since my grandfather was on the board and is the reason the two main dorms are there to house the current attending students.DO NOT COME TO THIS SCHOOL. After I graduated I tried calling and emailing for a year to obtain my transcripts and degree and when I finally reached somebody they said they couldn’t give me them because I have a $34,000 balance that I owe. I asked for my itemized statement and there were multiple discrepancies as well as a sum of about $8,000 that was taken from me for a semester that I didn’t attend. When I confronted them they stopped taking my calls and emails. I contacted FASFA they returned the money and continued to ignore my calls and emails. There are 167 other alumni that are also in this situation. Allen allows you to register doesn’t notify you of a balance and then tells you after they’ve accepted your money for senior dues and right before graduation. They have stolen financial aid funds and scholarships  from multiple students and I have the receipts to back up this claim. AVOID THIS SCHOOL at all cost unless you want your money stolen and your transcripts wrongfully withheld and to be screwed right before graduation. I’m in the process of taking legal action.']

name: Alliant International University
reviews:
['I do not recommend Alliant’s MA in Clinical Counseling program. There were serious issues with financial transparency during enrollment that left me misinformed about the true cost of the program. Despite raising concerns, the school offered no meaningful resolution or support.

Even more concerning is the lack of academic quality. Instruction is minimal to nonexistent. Most classes involve independent reading, discussion posts, and PowerPoints, with no real teaching or engagement. Feedback is rare, and several instructors were unresponsive for weeks, making it difficult to succeed or feel supported. The program feels like a degree mill, disorganized, impersonal, and poorly managed.

I deeply regret enrolling and would strongly advise others to consider more reputable programs with real structure, instruction, and transparency.', "Was a prospective student in the PsyD program. I was not impressed at their being very little information given as to why the Emeryville campus was no longer accepting students. I applied in October and had an interview schedules for early March. I was told two weeks before the interview, which was in-person and on-campus, and I had paid for a flight to get there, that there were no more admissions for the future, and to apply to a different program in California. It would have been prudent and professional to let people know before the end of 2025 at the very latest. This says a lot about the lack of professionalism garnered here. Additionally, I took the interviews, and was much less than satisfied at having to be addressed with and answer the following questions: Can you afford to move here? Do you have any financial concerns? Certainly not any of your business. If you don't want someone in your program, just tell them. Don't waste everyone's time with pointless and irrelevant, not to mention rude questions. And the general statement: You don't seem to have many friends. Not sure what that has to do with anything, let alone getting into a doctorate program, other than showing your serious lack of integrity, and inability to focus on what is actually happening. And to see one other program being ended as well, also without explanation, gives enough information to know that there are some serious ethical violations going on somewhere. I don't know if it's current clinical students, staff, or both. But they are certainly nothing short of red flags. You're credentialing board will be hearing about all this. And you've gotten nothing less than the stars that you deserve."]

name: All India Institute of Medical Sciences
reviews:
['The All India Institute of Medical Sciences (AIIMS), New Delhi is India’s premier medical institute, established in 1956 by an Act of Parliament as an institution of national importance. It was envisioned by India’s first Prime Minister, Jawaharlal Nehru, and Health Minister Rajkumari Amrit Kaur to serve as a center of excellence in medical education, research, and healthcare.

Key Highlights
- Location: New Delhi, India
- Campus Size: Spread across ~213 acres
- Motto: “Shariramadyam Khalu Dharmasadhanam” (The body is indeed the primary instrument of dharma)
- Purpose:
- Provide world-class undergraduate and postgraduate medical education
- Advance cutting-edge medical research
- Deliver specialized healthcare services to patients from across India and abroad
- Facilities:
- A large multi-specialty hospital with advanced diagnostic and treatment facilities
- Research centers and laboratories
- Training programs for doctors, nurses, and allied health professionals
- Reputation: Recognized globally for its high standards in medical training and patient care, AIIMS Delhi sets benchmarks for other medical institutions in India.

It stands as a symbol of India’s commitment to accessible, high-quality healthcare and continues to be a leader in medical innovation and service.', 'AIIMS Delhi is no doubt one of the best government hospitals in India when it comes to medical expertise. The biggest plus is that most services are extremely affordable compared to private hospitals, which is a huge relief.

However, the experience is not perfect. The crowd is overwhelming almost every day, and getting tests or appointments done can take a lot of time. You really need patience to navigate the long queues and multiple counters. Some staff members are helpful, but a few can be unresponsive or rude, which adds to the stress when you already have a patient to take care of.

Cleanliness is decent in some areas but definitely needs improvement in others. Parking is also a challenge.

Overall, AIIMS Delhi is a lifesaver for middle-class families because of the affordable and trustworthy treatment, but the management, waiting times, and basic services could be much better.', 'AIIMS New Delhi is one of India’s most prestigious medical institutions, known for excellent patient care, advanced treatment facilities, and experienced doctors.
Despite heavy crowds, services are systematic and affordable.
The hospital provides quality healthcare to all sections of society, making it a trusted and respected medical center nationwide.', '✅ In short:
AIIMS Delhi = excellent doctors + affordable care, but time-consuming, crowded, and bureaucratic experience.

🌟 Positive Experiences
World-class doctors – Many reviews highlight that AIIMS has some of the best specialists in India. People come from across the country (and abroad) for treatment.

Affordable care – Consultations and treatments are far cheaper than private hospitals, sometimes almost free for poorer patients.

Advanced facilities – Latest equipment, research focus, and availability of treatments that are not common elsewhere.

Life-saving reputation – Patients often say “AIIMS is the last hope” and share stories of successful treatment in critical cases.

⚠️ Challenging Experiences

Long waiting times – Patients often wait hours (sometimes days/weeks for appointments). Crowds are very large since AIIMS serves the entire country.

Overcrowding – Many reviews mention huge queues, full waiting rooms, and even people sitting on floors.

Staff behavior (non-medical) – Complaints about rude guards, clerks, or nurses. Doctors generally get praise, but support staff often get criticized.

Complex process – Registration, OPD slips, and appointment booking can feel confusing, especially for first-time visitors.', 'AIIMS Delhi (All India Institute of Medical Sciences) is widely recognized as the top medical institution in India—a place where ambition meets opportunity and the reality of campus life is both intense and incredibly rewarding.

Academics and Faculty
The academic rigor at AIIMS Delhi is extremely high, right from the very first year. Classes often run from 8 AM to 4 PM, combining lectures, practicals, and clinical postings. The curriculum is regularly updated and includes both traditional medical subjects and hands-on training with real patients. Most professors are highly experienced and approachable, though some niche courses may struggle with faculty or organization. Overall, students come out with strong clinical skills and a deep knowledge base, well-prepared for the medical field.

Infrastructure and Campus
The campus sprawls over 200 acres in Ansari Nagar—a green pocket in South Delhi. Facilities are world-class: modern lecture halls, advanced labs, a huge central library (BB Dikshit Library), libraries, sports complexes, swimming pool, gym, Wi-Fi throughout campus, and separate hostels for boys and girls. The hostels are generally allotted on a first-come, first-served basis, and the mess food is decent but can get monotonous. The entire campus is always buzzing with activity, from cultural fests to guest lectures.

Student Life and Activities
Life as a student is vibrant but also demanding. Many students speak about the significant workload and pressure, but strong peer networks, supportive seniors, and regular fests help with stress relief. Events like Pulse (the annual college fest) and other cultural, sports, and academic competitions are major highlights. There’s a healthy balance between intense academics and opportunities for relaxation and socializing. Many seniors guide juniors both academically and personally, contributing to a collaborative environment.

Placements and Careers
AIIMS Delhi offers unparalleled placement opportunities. Graduates regularly secure top positions in hospitals, research institutes, and even in international healthcare organizations. The reputation of AIIMS ensures career security and opens doors globally. The alumni network is very strong, and many graduates also appear for further studies abroad or for competitive exams in India.

Challenges
Workload: The academic pressure is real—the volume of study, assignments, and patient care can be overwhelming.

Bureaucracy and Management: Some courses (like Optometry) may face issues with management, transparency, and faculty allocation.

Infrastructure Upgrades: Despite world-class facilities, some buildings and systems still require modernization, and digitalization is ongoing.

Final Thoughts
For any ambitious medical aspirant, AIIMS Delhi is the dream destination. The intensity of academics and hospital work, combined with excellent facilities and a vibrant student life, prepares students for bright futures as doctors, researchers, or academicians. While the journey is hard, the rewards—in skills, friendships, and career opportunities—are well worth it.']

name: All Nations Bible College
reviews: ['A centre of excellence for "Word and Works" mission training. High quality training on the Whys, Ways, and Hows of international and domestic mission. The setting in the old home of the Buxton family is beautiful and peaceful. The learning community is gracious, prayerful, diverse and loving. name: Alma College
reviews:
['My experience at Alma College was defined by a toxic environment that failed students on every level—academically, culturally, and morally. Prospective students should be fully aware of what lies beneath the glossy surface of this institution.

1. Failure of Academic Integrity and Faculty Discrimination:
The official 4-year graduation rate for my cohort was alarmingly low, near 33%, symptomatic of poor curriculum and advising. The academic environment was compromised by a prevalent, unchecked cheating culture (via Greek test banks) that rewarded connections over merit.

More personally, I experienced overt classism and gender bias from faculty. I was told by one professor that I "did not belong" in an advanced STEM course because of my gender, and was criticized for wearing simple clothing due to my low socioeconomic background. Other faculty displayed shocking ignorance of life below the poverty line, forcing students to educate them on basic survival economics. The environment is actively hostile toward students who do not fit the college's expected profile of wealth and privilege.

2. Profound Failure in Student Safety and Administration:
The most grievous failure of this institution is its neglect of student safety. After reporting instances of sexual assault, I was institutionally discredited and actively silenced by the administration—essentially told to "sit down and shut up." This created a known environment of impunity, where my aggressors and others were aware that nothing would happen to them. This institutional negligence resulted in further victimization.
Conclusion: Alma College Was Wrong.
I am now a highly successful academic, holding a Master's Degree in Statistics (3.8 GPA) and having first-authored 17 academic papers. The faculty and administration at Alma College systematically underestimated, judged, and endangered me based on bias and prejudice. The college's judgment of me was categorically incorrect, proving that its culture is fundamentally flawed and incapable of accurately recognizing or supporting capable students.

I strongly caution anyone considering attendance here. Alma College is an institution where your safety will be compromised and your academic potential will be actively discouraged if you do not fit their privileged mold.', 'Wonderful program! Enjoyed the variety and high quality performance. They delivered!', "Class of 2016.

Don't go here. Dorms are terrible. The food is bad. If you are able to find a way to live off campus or get out of the meal plan, they cut your financial aid.

They treat their most loyal community members poorly. I just found out they are no longer allowing Mr. Trent to take photos at live events anymore. If you've graduated from Alma in the past few decades, you probably have a Mr. Trent picture framed in your home. He would take photos for no charge for the broke college kids.

SAD.", 'As an Alma alum, the student and faculty ratio was a greatest benefit to my learning experience. You’re able to forge relationships that will last a lifetime given the small community atmosphere on campus. If larger colleges aren’t your style and you want a liberal arts education, consider attending Alma College. Feast your ears for bagpipes and prepare to randomly shout “Go Scots” and “Plaid Works”!', "I'm a sophomore, and I absolutely love this college. They have provided me with every opportunity I was looking for in a college, such as travel, good academics, and professional experience, all while the support from the faculty has helped me be successful and actually be able to receive said opportunities. I couldn't imagine going to any other college, Alma is truly my home away from home!"]

name: Almo Collegio Capranica
reviews:
['The 15th-century Palazzo Capranica is located in the historic center of Rome, in Rione III (Colonna), at Piazza Colonna 101, a stone's throw from the majestic Pantheon.

I am reviewing the historic site, not the current activity.

On the architrave of the central portal, there is an inscription indicating 1451, the year of its construction by a Cardinal of the Capranica family, as the family's first residence. A few years later, another building with an adjoining tower was built next to it on the left, intended to become a college. The architrave of the portal, in fact, bears the inscription "COLLEGIVM CAPRANICENSE."

A theater was built in the Palace as early as the 17th century, which lasted until the end of the 19th century, then became a cinema in the 20th century until 2000.

The simple façade features Roman Renaissance elements; it is the union of two adjacent buildings that were later added to in the 17th century; the one on the right was the older one.

On the ground floor, the large, framed marble portals of the two buildings stand out, and on the left side, the corner tower, with a square base, clearly visible is the property plaque of the Archconfraternity of the Savior. It is surmounted by a roofed loggia.

On the main floor, six windows are beautifully displayed, including:
- on the right, three splendid Gothic mullioned windows, with columns featuring capitals and the noble family's coat of arms, and stone frames;
- on the left (the more modern section), three Renaissance cross-shaped windows.

On the second floor, only one of the six windows is not bricked up.

If what you read was enjoyable and helpful, I invite you to read my other reviews.', "A college founded in 1457 by Cardinal Capranica for those preparing for the priesthood. It likely inspired the Council of Trent's establishment of seminaries.
It was attended in their youth by Giacomo della Chiesa and Eugenio Pacelli, later popes Benedict XV and Pius XII. Its fifty-odd students attend the faculties of ecclesiastical universities, particularly the Pontifical Gregorian University in Piazza della Pilotta.
It is arguably the oldest seminary in the world.", 'The Capranica College for ecclesiastical studies was founded in 1457 by Cardinal Domenico Capranica.
The architectural style is characteristic of the first generation of the Renaissance.', 'We give it 5 stars because it was the first Roman seminary.']

name: Al Nahrain University
reviews:
["Al-Nahrain University, is a prestigious institution of higher education located in Baghdad, Iraq. Established in 1987.

Al-Nahrain University Overview:

1. History and Development: Al-Nahrain University was founded with the aim of providing high-quality education and fostering advanced research in various fields. Over the years, it has grown to become one of Iraq's leading universities.

2. Academic Programs: The university offers a wide range of undergraduate, graduate, and postgraduate programs across various disciplines. It includes colleges of Medicine, College of Information Engineering*, Engineering, Science, Political Science, and Information Technology, among others.

3. Research and Innovation: Al-Nahrain University is known for its strong emphasis on research and innovation. It hosts several research centers and institutes that focus on scientific and technological advancements, contributing significantly to the academic and practical knowledge in Iraq.

4. Campus and Facilities: The university's campus is equipped with modern facilities, including well-equipped laboratories, libraries, and lecture halls, providing a conducive environment for learning and research.

5. Community and Collaboration: Al-Nahrain University fosters a collaborative academic environment and has established partnerships with various international universities and institutions, promoting academic exchange and research collaboration.

6. Role in Society: The university plays a crucial role in the socio-economic development of Iraq by producing skilled graduates who contribute to various sectors, including healthcare, engineering, technology, and public administration. Also, the University open the scholarship for the international students.

Al-Nahrain University continues to uphold its mission of excellence in education and research, striving to meet the evolving needs of society and contributing to the global academic community.

*One of the unique and specialized colleges intended with the computer networks, computer communications, and systems engineerings.", 'Loved this college the spaces the gardens it is just such a beautiful environment to study', 'Engineering college is good, and the labs are well equipped but with not enough equipment.
