name: Altınbaş University
reviews:
["I put one star forcely they don't even deserve a minus, go there drop 5k dollars for every year than what ? got nothing in return There’s no proper management, no proper university professors, and the bus is so dirty it literally has fungus growing on the windows. The bus is usually late, so you either freeze to death or get roasted in the heat while waiting for it to show up.
All the professors care about is making you fail so you’ll take summer courses — and last year the summer course was 70 dollars per credit.
They fire any good professor,
and last year they literally kicked out so many of them. It’s basically a university that gathers people who don’t even have the qualifications to get hired anywhere else..
Math courses in the engineering department are online, and the professors can’t even speak proper English, so half of the explanation gets lost because they simply can’t talk in English.
There’s no midterm schedule — nothing. You can literally have four exams on the same day, and the professors won’t even care. It’s just failure after failure.
If you want to destroy your life, your health, or your dreams, I definitely recommend altinbas.
And as for the administration, calling them “careless” is an understatement. You send them an urgent email and they won’t reply for 3–4 days, but when you go to the university in person, you find them drinking coffee.
They don’t help you with anything — and I mean ANYTHING . So you either cry your eyes out or just accept being a failure.

And again, the professors act more like children than actual adults.
You basically end up babysitting the professors, not learning from them.
And if they notice that you’re actually smart, they will tear you apart — because they want money, not for you to get proper education.", 'First of all horrible university do not apply. Just a waste of money you won’t be learning anything from it especially in the medical field you’ll just be cram studying for an exam to have multiple wrong questions and end up with 0 knowledge, And that’s aside from the assignments you spend weeks on just for the professor to grade it based on how much they like the student. The staff and professors are extremely unprofessional and not helpful at all you can never find them in their offices let alone get a response to an email aside from the professors that tell you to not bother asking questions cause you’ll fail anyways. Terrible system that is built to just fail students and get more money out of them absolutely crucial with the end of year grading even if a student needs less than 0.5 points they will not give it don’t bother asking. We pay thousands of dollars just so that we would walk into a campus that is fully damaged the ceiling is always leaking, the bathrooms always dirty, the projector never works. The attendance system is extremely crucial especially with how heavy the workload is in med school you’re forced to attend classes from 8:40am till 5:30pm and have an attendance of 70% otherwise you are not allowed to sit the final which immediately results in you failing the year, leaving the students absolutely no time to study let alone have a life outside of uni. To conclude all of this if you genuinely care about your future and love your major stay away from this university.', 'WARNING: HORRIBLE UNIVERSITY DON’T COME ❌
I graduated from the architecture department and left with a sour taste like many others students because the experience was horrible and unpleasant so i don’t recommend at all

POOR ENGLISH: teachers speak turkish in the english department even tho it is prohibited and we complained about it many times because international students miss lots of informations about the courses. Teachers and the stuff have extremely poor english knowledge and lack good communication skills
PROGRAM: always the same boring projects, there is no variations because that’s what the teachers are comfortable with
STUFF: bad stuff, misleading, provides wrong information, late support or no support at all
TEACHERS: some grade students according to the love they have for them but not according to the work provided
ADVISOR: absent, rude, very hard to reach and misleading. I had so much delays and infos not communicating to me that cause me problems during my study and graduation period
CAMPUS: not much to do', 'Please , DO NOT APPLY TO THIS UNIVERSITY NO MATTER WHAT, IF YOU DO YOUR RISKING YOUR OWN FUTURE. ESPECIALLY MEDICAL FACULTY and in general other majors but specifically medical faculty is disrespectful, rude, unprofessional and ignorant do not expect yourself to learn something from them they explain in turkish considering the fact your paying over 20,000 usd dollars to learn medicine in English yet they speak in turkish. Teaching is a joke 😂 just like the professors from the vice dean to assistants to everything they are a BIG MASSIVE JOKE they are money animals they will deliberately come up with excuses to fail you or make you repeat the year weather it was ( attendance, marks, course registrations) or so on . They treat students like random selection to fail them even if they are good academically , they suck the joy and passion out of you towards the major you choose . Please i request anyone reading this do not apply and keep me in your prayers because i will save you if you read this, the international office is a joke too they don’t help with anything ( registration, diploma, residency) but one day this university will pay for taking haram money each cent they took by harming students will return to them . حسبي الله ونعم الوكيل', 'One of the best private Universities in TÜRKİYE ( TURKEY 🇹🇷) …Mahmutbey Main Campus']

name: Alvernia University
reviews: ['Beautiful campus! Grounds are beautiful. Staff and students are beyond nice, respectful and helpful. Nice dorm rooms. Clean buildings.', "there's no signal for our electronic devices, phone calls,  no wifi but other than that, it was okay", 'Beautiful university. My experience was amazing. I had three teachers who were my favorites. The classrooms were clean. I graduated with a degree in business administration.', 'Cleanest campus. Friendly, most respectful students you will meet. Would recommend this campus to anyone looking for OT, nursing and AT program. New cafeteria providing g meals for 2025 -2026', 'Beautiful campus. I personally loved the newly opened indoor recreational coplex. The university was founded in 1958 by the  Bernardine Franciscan Sisters. Any high school graduate interested in attending a prestigious university at a reasonable cost, should add Alvernia to their short list. Located 1 hour from Bethlehem near Reading taking route 222.']

name: Alverno College
reviews: ['This great college has always been a landmark in my life on the Southside of Milwaukee. Well, it became uber important to me when I started my Master’s degree. My first college suddenly closed. Alverno stepped up and helped this man complete his degree. I always liked Alverno and all the great women I’ve known who were taught there. I just never knew how deeply I’d appreciate this fine school. Thanks, Alverno. 🙏 🥹', 'Volunteer at Alverno. Beautiful campus with maintained buildings and property. Free parking garage for visitors, students, marked ADA and staff parking.', "Stay away from this school, especially their graduate nursing programs. They don't help you find a preceptor leave you hanging to dry. Then it's on you to find preceptor like its so easy. Many of the instructors also suck. Stay away!", "Don't waste your time here. Their nursing program is a joke. Half the time you are teaching yourself yet paying thousands of dollars. The professors make up their own rules as they please and don't follow their own syllabus. They are constantly changing their rules and criteria, they will not tell you the changes until you meet with your advisor about something. Nothing transfers to other schools so be sure you are going to finish here if you plan on attending. I would never recommend this school to anyone. They don't value you as a student. They screw you over. Definitely look elsewhere!!! Don't waste your money or time. I've done enough of that for all of us.", "I will be as blunt and honest as possible. The business office/financial aid office has absolutely the worse kind of people. The women in this office are super rude to you and even when they make mistakes on their end regarding payments...they still don't apologize!! They make you feel like a criminal standing in there being accused of not paying, when payments are made in advance! Oh and when they are proven wrong...all they have to say is, oh this is taken care of now..TERRIBLE. TERRIBLE. TERRIBLE. You would think with the amount of money they make on students tuitions that they would get their business office together. Just get it together Alverno..your students are not your enemies. In fact, your students are why you even exist. Also they don't transfer credits, so you are either there until you graduate or just threw away a boat load of money."]

name: Alvin Community College
reviews: ['We went to watch a softball game. Really nice ballpark. Concession stand needs to take cards.', 'Me and my husband made our very First visit to the campus for Spring Admissions. We were lost figuring out how to get to registry. I got out of the car when we stopped by the student center to ask for directions. Once I got help we were making our way back to the front of the school by the college police center. We had made an honest mistake by driving into one way drive thru where other ppl were driving towards the exit. But honestly in all fairness it looked like a simple parking lot. While my husband was trying you correct his mistake of being on the wrong side, Officer E. Davidson (short, bald, Caucasian, late 40s) with black shades stopped us with intimidation with his big community college police truck yelling outside of the window telling us he is pulling us over. As we pulled over. He came with vengeance and anger and not once asked how we’re doing or do we need help or at least give us a fair warning about the one way street since this was our first time visiting. He just seen a black family in the car and wanted to reprimand us for any little thing he can hold us accountable for. He gave us 2 violation tickets without hearing anything we had to say. For that, it really dimmed our spirit of coming to the campus and trying to make our way to become students here. We prayed as a Christian family and hope we were going to have a safe and cheerful journey to Alvin Community College due to all we heard was great things about the college. OFFICER E DAVIDSON MADE IT CLEAR HE IS DISCRIMINITIVE AND NOT A NICE PERSON AND HAS NO REASONING. HE RUINED OUR EXPERIENCE HERE', '4th July Celebrations was awesome here. Not to big not to small plenty parking and live band. Recommend for families', 'Shout out to Evelyn, Gigi and Natalie for helping me with my fafsa when I had difficulty getting it done', 'I ♥️ Alvin Community College!!']

name: Alzahra University
reviews: ['I like the asmosphere of my former university in Iran, I am grateful that I had a good time there with good professors and friends. The new canteen and central library  are so good. I hope that young girls and women of Iran can have the best chance and facilities to study!', 'A female-only university in Tehran with delightful environment and facilities. Established by the former Queen of Iran, Farah Pahlavi, in the 1960s.', 'One of my friends was also studying there and his opinion was that during the past year, the quality of food and welfare has dropped sharply, especially in the dormitory section of the university, so that more than a quarter of the meals were not of good quality for use, which is about three meals per week compared to the previous semester, and the quality of other meals has decreased compared to the previous semester. The protest of the students, who are all girls, will be punished.\nBut still another additional star for some professors .', "as the rest of the buildings built before iran's revolution, this university also has the real quality and respect to the users, but the maintenance is mainly focused on administrative sections and the dormitories really need  to be refurbished.\ngod bless iran's ever queen Farah Pahlavi.", 'I have many friends in this university and most are sharp and smart.']

name: AMA Computer University
reviews:
['I enrolled at AMA Education System with the expectation of receiving proper instruction, guidance, and support. Instead, my experience has been marked by repeated neglect and failure to deliver even the most basic educational services.
•	Unanswered tickets: Despite submitting multiple requests (Ticket Nos. 175779, 177299, 180172), AMA failed to provide any meaningful response.
•	Incorrect quiz marking: My correct answers were marked wrong, and repeated requests for manual checking were ignored.
•	Lack of faculty support: At no point did AMA provide promised academic assistance or monitoring of student progress.
•	False claims: AMA incorrectly stated that I failed due to not taking exams, despite my completion of all LMS materials.
This pattern of neglect has not only wasted my time and money but has also jeopardized my future career plans in teaching. Because AMA failed to deliver the services they advertised, I am now unable to proceed with my professional application, delaying my employment opportunities and causing significant distress.
AMA’s refusal to process a refund violates the Consumer Act of the Philippines (RA 7394), which guarantees redress when services are misrepresented or not properly rendered. Their “no refund” policy cannot override statutory consumer protection rights.
I am escalating this matter to CHED and DTI for investigation and enforcement. I strongly caution prospective students to reconsider enrolling in AMA Education System, as my experience demonstrates a serious breach of trust, accountability, and educational standards.', 'Do not enroll in this school! They may be scammers, as you cannot obtain your credentials even after paying. They fail to provide or send any proof of completion, which can seriously ruin your future and career. This is, without a doubt, the worst school. I hope the administration is held accountable. Shame on them!', 'Back after a long long time :)
The HS building was demolished last year, even the old college building.', "I really like Ama university I'm still studying here right now ang it's so good", "AMA  University needs a tutoring center where students need peer tutoring for math,  English, science, etc. Most students need coaching; fellow teaching students make the tutor learn more and become competent. If the AMA University school system has this peer tutoring, more students will graduate at a successful rate and find their careers. This leads to peer mentoring.

A teacher should respect students who work hard for what they earn and do. It's a private matter. Because disclosing their social status to staff and students makes it discriminatory and detrimental to all students' well-being since this is a private matter and unsuitable for the student's environment.
Why do the chapters and the test not add up regarding the examination? Are the students just sheep to bite to enroll and fail? Since AMA stands to have, everyone educated, not fail every student. It is to be successful and more graduates are what counts, not more students who are failing and not graduating and lost hope because educators are the mentors for students, not the cause of failing them.
For example, for exams, the book or chapters part of the syllabus must match what the test is all about, and no surprises not come from the book chapters, which is not fair to students who study and research because the money people earn these days don't grow in trees these come from blood and sweat they have made this is why AMA should recall the way educators mistreat their students."]

name: Ama International University
reviews:
['Excited to see such awesome university in kingdom of Bahrain.. University having latest Morden education system with good atmosphere and all the facility.. Prime location easy access to all the services like restaurant cafe petrol station and ATM.. Near by.. Must rate star.. University of technology salmabad Bahrain', 'Excellent University !👌 👏 Excellent Management!
👏 Library well equipped and very nice place to study, different sports, coffee shop to study , restaurants, parking ..Timings of courses until evening
Different courses
Professors and teaching staff very qualified and international curriculum ..
Great Career services and employability with coaching  sessions..
The university campus and facilities are amazing 👏
Distinguished Student life and international class  .. wonderful Tuition fees and value for money course..
University location excellent 👌
There are Other services like student clubs and societies for all..  different workshops..
Online courses but no online degrees and no accommodation', 'For as long as I was a student at the university it was okay. Once I applied to get a transfer certificate which was on April 2022 and currently it is the end of September 2022, I still haven’t received the transfer certificate and everytime I followed up or asked the only answer I ever got was “it’s not ready” and told me wait. The reason they even took time to process was due to their negligence of misplacing or not noticing certain documents.
Other than that, even asking for the simplest things is a task. Had asked for a recommendation letter and had to go from person to person just to ask where to get it from.
Even to get a simple course syllabus, nobody is able to provide it easily rather keep transferring calls to calls. Had even texted a number that was provided to me by the university and didn’t even get a reply back.
Not trying to be rude, but if I knew this was how the university worked I’d not apply plus their tuition fee isn’t very cheap.', 'Studying at AMA was truly an amazing experience, the students community is so diverse and open. Gaining my Bachelors of Science in Computer Science was somewhat challenging but worth the while, It was a nice journey at AMA Bahrain.', 'The accounts side, not to sound rude or anything, but the staff there most specifically, the Filipino ones from either windows 1-4 between them, are rude as hell. They are not only rude to other nationalities, but also to their own. I don’t get why they have to talk in a bad manner, we ask politely, they respond in a way you just want to talk back at them, but at the same time not, because you respect them.

They need to change the facilities as well, it’s been having this rotten and sewage smell.

Fix the classrooms.

When it’s winter the Air Conditioner’s on, but when it’s literally hot during Summer, they turn it off. We’re not enjoying the heat outside and the sauna we get inside the campus, we all need some cooling after getting drained from the sun.

Some of the male teachers should respect everyone, especially towards girls.

Class timings are so off, especially the slots available are always less than 2 and it all has a huge gap between each class.

WE PAY A LOT MORE, SO WE EXPECT SOMETHING A BIT DECENT IN RETURN. ALL YOU PEOPLE DO IS TAKE MONEY, BECAUSE FOR YOU GUYS, IT’S ALL ABOUT THE MONEY. AMA IS CLEARLY A JOKE. IT’S LIKE WE’RE THE PUPPETS BEING PLAYED AT. NOT EVEN SORRY.']

name: Amarillo College
reviews: ['From 1967 to 1970 I was a part-time student at Amarillo College.   I eventually accumulate about 36 hours of college credit. I later attended two 4 year Universities in Kentucky and Oklahoma on a part-time basis where I eventually received a Bachelor Degree in Public Administration and worked on an MBA Degree.  I attended all of the classes on a part-time basis because I always had to work a full-time job.  I went on to have a very successful Federal law enforcement career.  I give thanks to Amarillo College and their great Professors for giving me an opportunity to do well in life. So good luck to all the Students and Professors.', 'Classes are not too pricey but when you put them together with access codes for professors who want convenience over having to do any actual work and then said access codes are coupled with software that is awful to say the least, this college is going to cost you double after you figure out how bad it is that you are being ripped off and have to switch to another college.  I do not know why access codes are a thing.  It is awful.  Like you are a professor and teach a distance course, do some professor work and put together an actual criteria for your class.  I have classes where all due dates are from years behind or past semesters.  They do not even update that?  Really?  Do not bother with this college.  The amount of procrastination I have seen is tantamount the eiffel tower and for the student needing some tolerance of the same kind and you will be met with an oh, that is not allowed!!!!!!!!!  NO late work accepted under any circumstance.  No leniency at all for the student, it is all for convenience for the school.', 'I had seen this campus before but never went on the actual campus. I called the library to see if there was a textbook that they might have for a class that I’m taking at another university. The lady was helpful and was able to find me one of the books that I could use. So we went to the campus today. It was a nice campus and looked like a good place to attend and makes you feel like you’re at home. Overall I would attend this college for further education or certifications.', "The June Jazz concert was fun, now I know why it's been happening for 27 years ❣️😃", 'Very clear structure, pathways, and directions. Very clean and high quality interiors.']

name: Amasya University
reviews: ['Worst university ever..', 'Good', 'Hem Aöf sınavlarında hem de öğrencisi olmama rağmen kampüs içerisine araçla alınmıyoruz.Öğretim görevlileri/sınav gözetmenleri alınırken yapılan bu ayrından dolayı öğrencisi olduğum üniversiteye asla ısınamadım', 'It\'s a terrible, awful school. I can\'t explain my problem to anyone. I have to contact student affairs. They keep transferring me from one number to another. I can\'t explain my problem to anyone. I\'m fed up. Most of the teachers answer the students by cursing them. I got a ridiculously low grade on the exam of a course I was confident in. When I objected to this, they said, "Are you giving me an order?" NEVER WRITE ABOUT THIS SCHOOL. DO NOT WRITE ABOUT IT.', "This is my first time here. I haven't had much chance to explore. But it's a university worthy of Amasya. Generally, it has most departments. Security at the entrances is very good. It's considered within the city. There are places to stay for students in the surrounding area. However, I don't have much information about the prices. Generally, the overall expenses for students coming from outside the city shouldn't be too high."]

name: Ambassador University
reviews: ['We visited Ambassador Baptist College and were blessed to see this school. This is a great college.  This semester is starting and many students are back. Every one is busy and getting situated. We were able to meet several students and staff.\nThe college is located in a very nice small town in North Carolina. Very nice campus. We enjoyed our tour of the campus. The commons is a very nice to visit and relax a little.\nThis is a good Bible college. We recommend this college to anyone wanting to further their education.', 'I have not been inside but I have talked to several students outside the college. The students I have encountered have been polite and friendly.  They wave at me every time I see them. So I would say if they are a reflection of the school, it has to be a good place.', 'I visited the college recently and in my short time there it was evident that God is at work in Ambassador. The students and staff are all super friendly and welcoming. The classes are well taught and everything they teach is Bible based (KJV). I visited during their Bible conference week and was even challenged by the great chapel services. If anyone is considering Bible College I highly recommend Ambassador.', 'I came here for camp one summer. The campus was nice, the faculty was godly, the fellowship was encouraging, but most importantly, the preaching was amazing. I grew more spiritually during that one week of camp than I had ever grown. I deffinately recommend this collage to anyone who wants to serve God with their life.', 'My wife and I attended Ambassador and are both thankful for this solid, Biblically based, ministry focused, training school. While no institution is perfect, our time at ABC was a huge blessing. We made some of our deepest friendships while there and count many of the faculty as dear, godly friends to this day.']

name: Ambon State Polytechnic
reviews: ['Attending the SENTRINOV 2024 National Conference at Ambon State Polytechnic was a fantastic experience. The campus provided a great venue, and their hospitality was truly exceptional, making everyone feel warmly welcomed. A well-organized and memorable event!', 'sangttt bgussss', 'State Vocational Education of engineer in Ambon.', 'Wesss', 'Politeknik negeri ambon (polnam) adalah perguruan tinggi vokasi unggulan di Maluku dengan akreditasi institusi B, dikenal menghasilkan lulusan terampil dan siap kerja. name: Ambrose Alli University
reviews:

The hostel standard is just fair. Nothing spectacular. Teaching standard is really, really poor. Far too numerous disruptions to the academic sessions.

Many programmes are neglected, especially the medical programs.

The staff are NOT very concerned about student welfare, just like any other Nigerian university.", 'Ambrose Alli university is not for the faint hearted. It's a fairly good school offering a wide curriculum, and has great alumni such as Pastor Chris Oyakhilome and Tony Elumelu.

Unfortunately, the school was popular for violent secret cultism and protests in the past. I can't tell about now.", "A place to be but don't stay in the hostels,they might look pretty but they r awful and dirty inside.", "How is it possible for a university's contact number to be switched off on week days and emails are left hanging. Third parties are not allowed to pick certificates ! I can not even go pass the payment ! Someone should please respond because time is of essence!"]

name: Ambrose University
reviews:
["My daughter transferred to Ambrose from another institution because she wasn't very engaged there.  Ambrose was completely different.  The profs' interest in teaching and attendance was much better in her experience and extra tutorials are offered regularly.  She became much more engaged, GPA improved and is confident will complete Bachelor's degree", "Overall, my experience here was good not great. The community is what's best about Ambrose, especially when you live on Residence and some of the professors are great. However, Ambrose is quite expensive and a good chunk of the theology is misguided and does not align with the Word of God.", 'Seems like a great place.
Visiting for the first time and I like what I see.

Dan David

Award winning worship violinist featured on over 30 television programs reaching millions of people for Christ.', "I didn't study in this university, but I had a great day in it. It was a lecture about astronomy, and it was so great. The hall was so big, and there were a lot of chairs for everybody. The lecture was introduced by dr. Stephen Jean's, and he was so knowledgable about this subject and easy to understand. He explained everything to everyone, even children. Children asked him questions also, and he replied in a way that they understand him. After the lecture, we drank hot chocolate, and went outside. Then we watched stars by telescopes. It was a fantastic day. I hope they will do more lectures in this university.", 'Caution advised. The courses offered are not accredited and cannot be transferred to other institutions. According to the University of Calgary, Ambrose has not taken the necessary steps toward accreditation. This creates significant challenges for students planning to continue their education elsewhere.

Very expensive with inflexible plans for students living on campus.']

name: Amedeo Avogadro University of Eastern Piedmont
reviews:
["As a student pursuing a Master's degree at the University of Eastern Piedmont in Vercelli, Italy, I can say that this institution has exceeded my expectations in many ways.

Firstly, the faculty members are highly knowledgeable and passionate about their subjects, which creates an engaging and interactive learning environment. I have had the opportunity to learn from professors who are experts in their fields and have conducted extensive research, which has significantly impacted my learning experience.

Secondly, the university's facilities are top-notch. The modern and well-equipped classrooms, libraries, and research labs provide students with the necessary resources to excel in their studies. The campus is also situated in a serene environment that promotes focus and concentration, which is crucial for a postgraduate student.
The university has an excellent support system for students. The academic advisors are always available to offer guidance and support throughout the course of the program. The university also offers career services that help students to prepare for their future careers by providing resources such as internships, job fairs, and networking opportunities.

Finally, the international community at the University of Eastern Piedmont is diverse and inclusive. I have had the opportunity to interact with students from different cultures and backgrounds, which has broadened my perspective and exposed me to new ideas.

the University of Eastern Piedmont is a great institution for students, With its knowledgeable faculty, modern facilities, excellent support system, and diverse community, students can be assured of a quality education that prepares them for career success.", 'Hello, do you recommend this university for medicine, but which one do you recommend?', 'Very nice', 'Very boring meetings, they make you lose the desire to enroll. Incompetent professors, they barely know how to use PowerPoint and a microphone. Disappointing, pitiful, quite horrifying. I absolutely do not recommend it. It is the ruin of our generation. The same comment applies to the UPO in Novara. DISAPPOINTING!!', 'La mia recensione si basa sulla Segreteria. Personale empatico disponibile ed efficiente oltrechè professionale hanno gestito una mia dimenticanza con le tasse in modo impeccabile. Davvero bravi a tutti e grazie della vostra comprensione. Auguro  e tutti voi un buon anno!']

name: American Academy of Dramatic Arts-New York
reviews:
['Went for admissions tour, and daughter performed audition. This place is authentic, genuine,  and most historic.  We are excited about opportunities for school and career. Professors and staff are magnificent!!', 'I attended and completed the certificate
program. It is great .', 'I love going to the shows here.  There is so much talent and enthusiasm :-)', "I went to class during the summer of 2022, I took a lot of optatives including my favorite: Writing with the amazing actor and writer David Dean Bottrell, I enrolled myself in Acting for Film and Television and Musical Theater,  I loved both programs, my professors, my classes and my classmates. The only thing that I would recommend is to relax a bit with all the covid restrictions, we have to move on and get going, also we are actors we need to see our eyes and faces :).
Another observation: at the time I was 30 and I was one of the oldest, the average age of people going to AADA is early 20's, except for the acting for film and television program, everyone was a bit more and around my age.  I love the American Academy Of Dramatic Arts, it's an historic building, an historic Academy, great location, great neighborhood, it was an honor and an a dream for me going there. I'm very grateful for the experience.

Shot out to: Nurit Monacelli a great human being, actress and teacher and  to my professor and music director Christopher Mcgovern with whom all my singing for the actor classes were like an episode of glee.", "Went to a month long summer program which involved taking classes and meeting the students. I honestly was not very impressed. The classes were literally the same as the ones I had been taking at my community college, with the only difference being the much higher price tag for the Academy. Most of the full time students were all very pretentious, many acting as though they were already famous, particularly the girls. The only professor I really liked was the guy who ran the improv class, the others weren't anything special."]

name: American Baptist College
reviews: ["I graduated from American Baptist College in 2022 and it was worth it!! The staff truly embraced me, the Professors helped  me develop academically, and American Baptist College gave me tools that I need in my profession, my life, and to further my education. They want students to succeed and they encourage each individual to be a change agent in whatever capacity they will lead. They also challenged me to always be open minded and have a willingness to evolve within myself and within the world. They taught me to never grow stagnant and always be open to learn. My flame will forever lead the way for me and others. Similar to Dr. King, John Lewis,  and leaders that have made powerful impacts within the world. I will use my flame for beneficial purposes and that's to lead people out of dark places into the marvelous light. The most important thing is knowing that it all starts with the internal work and then you can project it outwardly.\n\nThank you, American Baptist College", "This College has allowed my two sons and myself a wonderful undergraduate education again it's great", 'Attended the Theology and Peace Conference here. Wonderful hospitality and great facilities!']

name: American Business School of Paris
reviews:
['I paid for a full year of the BBA program but could only attend one semester due to surgery. Despite providing all required proof and following up multiple times after my operation, no refund was issued and no response was given. Two years have passed, and I am still waiting for a reply.', 'Hello everyone,

I’m a student who had many dreams before coming here. If you’re planning to pursue a bachelor’s degree, you can go ahead. However, if you’re thinking about a master’s degree, reconsider. In this program, master’s students often attend the same basic courses as bachelor’s students, which don’t relate to our advanced studies or internships.Furthermore, the institute doesn’t even provide ID cards, which complicates our access to various services and facilities. As an English-speaking student who doesn’t speak French, I’ve found the institute’s lack of guidance frustrating. Even when you secure an internship through the institute, if you don’t speak French, your application may be rejected outright.The administration does not seem invested in our academic progress; their main concern appears to be to simply show that they have submitted applications for internships, ensuring they meet their quotas. I’ve noticed that students from India are particularly targeted for money, being asked to pay a re-enrollment fee of 1,000 euros in the first year alone.

My advice to my fellow Indian students is to avoid starting in May and, in fact, consider avoiding this institution entirely. Many educational consultants may recommend it because they are well-compensated by Indian representatives here.
This institution doesn’t have proper classrooms; it is essentially just a floor in a building. You might find yourself shuffled from one building to another frequently. Don’t waste your money if you are seeking quality master’s level education.
Take heed of both positive and negative reviews, but pay particular attention to the negative ones. Many Western students are only visiting, not staying long-term, which limits networking opportunities. During most of the program, you will be placed in classes with bachelor’s students, only joining exclusively master’s students in your final semesters.I’m sharing this because I understand the financial sacrifices that Indian families make. Consider this a warning. Choose another institution in Paris. Do thorough research before committing. Thanks to my financial stability, I was able to leave, but I don’t want you to have to drop out as I did. I have documented proof of all these issues.In summary, while this school might be suitable for bachelor’s studies, I strongly advise against enrolling here for a master’s degree. ABS, if you delete this comment, I will persist like a phoenix to expose your shortcomings.', 'Worst business school ever ABS
No response/poor communication
Only focused on Money
Disqualified or disengaged faculty
Zero return on investment
Fake reviews&manipulated ratings', 'I wouldn’t recommend this school if:
1. you don’t want to have unqualified for some subjects “professors” (one of our teacher was an ex-flight attendant and another guy wanted the whole class to learn an obsene poem without any other given choice and rudly operessed those who didn’t want to learn it)
2. You don’t want to have your schedule changed several time during the year without your notice. The administration also speaks very haughtly with students who wonder why it happends despite the fact that the studies cost quite expensive.
3. You don’t want to be disrespected by the administration with poor people skills.', 'To all the students who have been scammed by this so called university.
I am a lawyer and I have taken it upon myself to serve you justice. A student group has been started, if you have signed a sketchy contract or are being refused documents send me an email to join our movement.']

name: American College of Greece
reviews:
['Truly an amazing experience. I love that they have other activities and sports for students like volleyball, football, drawing ,planting etc. I especially like the gym since it is big and clean with many gymnastic machines and a couch to help you in your workout if you ask for help along with yoga and pilates group workouts. Something important that  Deree also has is that the teachers try as much as possible to make the lesson interesting and encourage students to participate.', 'Very expensive offering poor facilities.
Their focus is on  assessments and exams restricted the time for real comprehension and knowledge. Disappointed experience.', 'Stay away of you want to use your degree. I am disappointment regarding the ongoing challenges Deree graduates face when attempting to have their degrees attested for international recognition.

After contacting Deree offices several times for assistance, I was advised to “handle it independently.” Unfortunately, this is not a reasonable solution given the complex situation surrounding Deree’s recognition status.

As it currently stands, Deree graduates are caught in a difficult position: Deree degrees are not recognized by Greek authorities, so the Greek Ministry of Foreign Affairs will not legalize them.

The U.S. Embassy in Athens does not attest academic credentials, and therefore cannot stamp Deree-issued degrees.

Deree provides only a “letter of attestation” rather than a stamped or signed document that meets embassy requirements — a letter that no embassy or ministry accepts for legalization.

This leaves graduates unable to authenticate their degrees for professional or legal use abroad.', 'Excellent facilities for students. The library is great and a nice place to sit and study with updated book catalogue. Sports facilities include a pool, gym, basketball courts etc. Nice environment in general.', "Great experience, best American education in Greece.
At least from a 1990's perspective."]

name: American College of Thessaloniki
reviews:
['ACT is the tertiary division of Anatolia, a private not-for-profit educational institution founded in 1886. ACT has been a bridge connecting America to Greece and South East Europe. It is one of the few institutions outside the United States with full, free-standing U.S. accreditation by NECHE, and EU validation of its undergraduate programs.

At ACT, we have created a dynamic, innovative, and demographically diverse learning community that prioritizes critical inquiry, intellectual development, and breadth and depth of knowledge. At ACT, students are empowered to learn and think differently, interact with the world differently, and become catalysts for change. Our faculty is dedicated to teaching and research, and together with our staff, takes great pride in the accomplishments of our students', 'The American College of Thessaloniki (ACT) was established in 1886 and is a private, non-profit university. It is a privately run American institution that has received NECHE accreditation and a Greek government permission to operate as a private post-secondary college. With a diversified faculty and students from 20 different countries, ACT is a thriving learning environment. ACT respects the diversity of the campus community and does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, national or ethnic origin, religion, or sexual orientation.The campus is perched on a hill overlooking the Aegean Sea.', 'I visited the campus and the university facilities are absolutely amazing
The American College of Thessaloniki (ACT) is the tertiary division of Anatolia, a private, not-for-profit educational institution founded in 1886. ACT is an independent American institution chartered in the State of Massachusetts and accredited by the New England Commission of Higher Education (NECHE). ACT also holds EU program validation and has been granted a license by the Greek government to operate as a private post-secondary College.

ACT is a vibrant learning community, where students of 20 different nationalities meet a highly diverse faculty body to engage in discourse characterized by sharing and mutual respect. In addition to knowledge acquisition, the hallmarks of the ACT experience are critical inquiry and service to others.  The ACT curriculum promotes open inquiry in a conscious and systematic way, in order to broaden students’ horizons, cultivate their talents, enhance their understanding of the world, and prepare them for a life of empathy.
At ACT, we strongly believe in the role that the environment and in particular Gaia plays in fostering intellectual growth; that is why we have built a beautiful campus on a hill, overlooking the Aegean Sea. Under the Grecian blue sky and amid a plethora of ancient trees and shrubs (cypress, olive, plums, pomegranate, etc.), our students have the opportunity to use our state-of-the-art educational facilities in pursuing their educational goals. This is the same environment that, a few miles to the East became the birthplace of Aristotle, while a few miles to the West, the birthplace of Alexander the Great.
ACT - The American College of Thessaloniki has no political, governmental or religious affiliation. ACT values a diverse college community. ACT does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, national or ethnic origin, religion, sex, sexual orientation, marital or parental status, age, or disability in the recruitment and admission of its students, in the administration of its educational policies and programs, or in the recruitment and employment of its faculty and staff.', 'The best foreign college in Thessaloniki Greece I had graduated friends who took scholarship as they came as refugee , ACT gave them opportunity for a new and bright future , Thank you ACT', 'My son gratuated today. Best day ever. Best College']

name: American Graduate School in Paris
reviews: ['Extremely unprofessional across the board. I am an alumni and I had a very negative experience. Most positive reviews are from faculty.', 'GREAT School with a GREAT mission.  Things that are so important to our life and our human experience are usually not taught in schools or in our society. Here is a school with a mission who are finding that this is a subject of the highest importance in life and in our educational system.  I am so happy that they exist and that they are doing their best in the struggling hope that Peace and Democracy are possible.   Thank you for being there!', "I'm a former student of this school and wanted to chime in with my fellow alumnae to discuss some of the great things about this school. First, the staff and faculty are overwhelmingly fantastic and incredibly supportive. I had some exceptional circumstances arise during my studies and from the financial aid to academic advisement, I was met with so much kindness and flexibility that completely wowed me. That is the best part of this school because it's small, you're able to get the kind of attention that larger schools can never give you. You're also able to be more flexible with your course load/ assignments because each student is treated individually and not only as numbers. The second reason why this school ranks highly for me is it's access to practitioners in the field of IR, like former ambassadors, heads of NGO's and employees at UNESCO/ OECD. It's refreshing to have professors who know more than just theory/ academic ideas that don't fly in the real world. It makes you feel like you're getting an education that can actually help you with your work and not just pass a class. Lastly, the location of this school is great. Right in the middle of Paris at the Alliance Francaise, which allows students to participate on their events and take classes. We're also near quite a few other University's which gives us access to other students and learning communities. Overall, I think this school does a lot of things well and depending on your attitude and focus, it's a great place to prepare for a career in IR.", 'I am a former graduate student from AGS, my experience has not been at all like that. Yes, I had a few disagreements with the administrative staff but be realistic that happens everywhere! What I was able to get here are professors that teach in the best institutions in Paris and former ambassadors who truly care about education in a more one on one scenario just like it happens in AGS. These same professors  teach in bigger schools but students do not have access to them as we did in AGS and for that I am very thankful! This is a small school, but the level of education is not at all relative to the size of its student body, I dont think I would have had access to such experienced educators (in practice and in theory) in any other school. John and Jennifer, give some sustainable arguments about what you are complaining, indeed your rants not only show a low level of education, but are of the nature of a spoilt child -which I strongly doubt has anything to do with the education you received, and failed to capitalize, from AGS.', 'I am so happy I chose this school to receive my M.A. It is definitely hard work but I have a whole team behind me at AGS, definitely my home in Paris!']

Everything else is very mediocre compared to the surrounding schools. Everything from on campus affairs to intercollegiate athletics is messy and unorganized, and lets not even get into how unkempt the dinning hall is. AIC makes for a good starter school! and only that. Its far too expensive and does nothing to cater to all students.', 'I was on campus to attend a summit about broadband access for the state. We had a meeting in the Schwartz campus center. It really was perfect for the group we had!']

name: American International School of Cape Town
American International University - Bangladesh
reviews:
["I can only tell about the campus outlook and location. It is one of the best private universities of Bangladesh and situated in a prime location of Dhaka city. The campus is not so large as a university campus should be but not very small too. Adequate buildings for academic and official purposes along with the famous Dome building which hiuses the Universiti's library and gives a 360° view of the campus. It is a green camous and deliberate effort is given to maintain various flower plants, shrubs, and decoration plants throughout the campus though there is no big tree there. During my visit I was amazed by the presence of a lot of sunbirds which feeds on the abandant yellow flowers and fruits in the campus. The presence of these birds says a lot about the environment of the campus. One negative point I found is there is no hostel in the campus and students has to stay out of the campus and has to come to the University by their own means. Moreover the roads leading to the University are very narrow and not very swetable to support the bunch of vehicle coming and leaving the campus.", 'As a proud student of American International University-Bangladesh (AIUB), I am delighted to share my wholehearted endorsement of this remarkable institution—a place where academic excellence, innovation, and personal growth converge.

From the moment I joined AIUB, the university’s core mission struck me: to develop innovative & globally competitive graduates equipped with strong ethical values and professional skills.  The modern, purpose-built campus in Kuratoli Road, Khilkhet—complete with its distinctive glass-covered central globe structure—provides an environment that is dynamic, inspiring, and fully suited to 21st-century education.

Academically, AIUB offers a broad and robust range of undergraduate and postgraduate programmes across disciplines including business, engineering, science & technology, and the arts.  The medium of instruction in English ensures all students are not only domain-experts but also comfortable in global professional contexts.  The learning culture here emphasizes critical thinking, peer discussion, and real-world relevance—making the classroom far more than simply a venue for lectures.

What sets AIUB apart is its commitment to student success both inside and outside the classroom. The university fosters a lively campus life—through cultural events, clubs, sports, and research activities—which enriches personal development and builds leadership.  On the research front, the institution has formal partnerships with elite bodies such as Asian Institute of Technology (AIT) aimed at boosting faculty development and doctoral opportunities.  This demonstrates that AIUB not only cares about undergraduate education, but also the ongoing advancement of its academic community.

Furthermore, AIUB’s quality policy underscores striving for standards aligned with national and international benchmarks.  This means that as a student, I feel confident I am receiving education with global relevance and local impact.

In my time here, I have grown not just as a student, but as a thinker, collaborator, and global citizen. If you’re seeking a university that balances academic rigour with practical readiness, that cultivates leadership rather than just grades, and that situates you in a thriving, English-medium, forward-looking environment—then AIUB is a fantastic choice.

In short, American International University-Bangladesh is not just a place to earn a degree: it’s a transformational campus experience where you’re prepared for challenge, ready for opportunity, and empowered to lead.', "AlUB is a beautiful university with a nice campus, but there are some serious management issues. The entry and exit areas are too small for the number of students, which causes heavy crowding and traffic. During rain, the water drainage system doesn't work properly, and the pathways often get flooded. Overall, the university looks good from the outside, but the internal system
Not bad", 'AIUB is one of the leading private universities in Bangladesh with a modern campus, excellent facilities, and a friendly learning environment. The university offers quality education, experienced faculty, and a wide range of programs. The campus is clean, well-maintained, and vibrant with student activities. A great place for academic growth and personal development.', 'American International University-Bangladesh (AIUB) stands as a leading institution in the country’s private education sector, known for its commitment to academic innovation, technological excellence, and holistic student development. Established in 1994, AIUB has built a strong reputation for providing globally competitive education aligned with international standards and industry demands.

Situated in the vibrant area of Kuril, Dhaka, the university boasts a modern and eco-friendly campus that integrates architectural sophistication with functionality. The infrastructure includes state-of-the-art classrooms, advanced computer and engineering laboratories, and an extensive library equipped with both digital and physical resources. These facilities collectively create a conducive environment for research, learning, and collaboration.

AIUB offers a wide spectrum of programs across faculties such as Engineering, Business Administration, Science & Technology, and Arts & Social Sciences. The curriculum is designed to balance theoretical foundations with practical applications, preparing students for dynamic professional environments. The university’s emphasis on innovation, entrepreneurship, and ethical leadership is reflected in its diverse academic and extracurricular initiatives.

From an administrative and operational standpoint, AIUB maintains a structured governance model that ensures transparency, quality assurance, and continuous improvement. The institution prioritizes student welfare, safety, and engagement through well-managed facilities and supportive academic services.

While traffic congestion around the campus can occasionally affect accessibility, AIUB’s academic standards, organizational efficiency, and modern learning environment continue to position it among the top private universities in Bangladesh. Its strong focus on academic excellence, digital transformation, and global collaboration reinforces its role as a forward-thinking leader in higher education.']

name: American Jewish University
reviews: ['What a great forward thinking Jewish nonprofit!', 'We attended the Lit drive through Hanukkah light program at AJU. It was an entertaining and well organized event. Our children (10, 12 and 15) were as enthusiastic about the program as my husband and I were. We are grateful for a safe and engaging drive through experience during this time of ""Stay Home" order in California. While the price of the event is steep ($75/car), if it is affordable, the event is worth the cost of the engaging and festive light show.', "On a recent Saturday night, I held my daughters Bat Mitzvah party on the elegant Chan Soon-Shiong Patio of American Jewish University's Mulholland campus. Jessica Levy, the Catering and Special Events Manager, created a beautiful, magical party above the city and under the stars. Each detail was perfect in every way, thanks to Jessica's professionalism and standards of excellence. I am quite particular and no request fell through the crack. Jessica patiently guided me through the planning process and arranged for the menu, bar, wait service, lighting, floor plan, parking, bakery order, and more. Jessica is knowledgeable, experienced and was an indispensable asset in my planning process. The guests were so impressed with the gorgeous facility and the absolutely delicious (and plentiful!) food. Jeff Stuart, Director of Campus Dining and Catering, was very understanding and careful regarding food allergy concerns. It is a wonderful service that AJU provides to the community making this special setting and lovely hospitality available at a most competitive and reasonable cost. We will never forget my daughter's fantastic celebration at the American Jewish University!", "Really enjoying the introduction to Judaism class I'm taking. Having a hard time getting my mind around their belief system, but loving the activity of trying. From this class I'm convinced that the school is Conservative. Good, but challenging.", 'I have now been in the course for 13 weeks and thus believe I am qualified to evaluate the course and thus at least part of the university.\nFirst of all, I must say that the contact with the administration, namely Ben, has been extremely smooth and friendly. I have been warmly welcomed, aided in everything and my mails were answered very quickly. When I then started the course itself with the lecturer Rabbi Adam Greenwald, I was overwhelmed with his knowledge, kindness and devotion. Also, all the participants are extreemly frindly and engaged. It\'s not just frontal teaching via Zoom, but regularly we are divided into subgroups to discuss particular topics in a small group before reflecting on them in the whole group. What particularly touches me is that I, as a "Hassan", am welcomed so warmly amid many Jews and potential converts to Judaism. The participants have ZERO prejudices; on the contrary, they are interested, as I sense from their questions and interaction. The course is originally for people who want to convert or are interested in Judaism. I can only recommend everyone to participate in this course. After several years of reading and learning the language, I find that this course closes the circle, pulls the threads together, puts things into context and expands what I have learned. Without a doubt, one of the best distance learning courses I have ever taken or will ever take.']

name: Americanos College
reviews:
['Went for admissions tour, and daughter performed audition. This place is authentic, genuine,  and most historic.  We are excited about opportunities for school and career. Professors and staff are magnificent!!', 'I attended and completed the certificate
program. It is great .', 'I love going to the shows here.  There is so much talent and enthusiasm :-)', "I went to class during the summer of 2022, I took a lot of optatives including my favorite: Writing with the amazing actor and writer David Dean Bottrell, I enrolled myself in Acting for Film and Television and Musical Theater,  I loved both programs, my professors, my classes and my classmates. The only thing that I would recommend is to relax a bit with all the covid restrictions, we have to move on and get going, also we are actors we need to see our eyes and faces :).
Another observation: at the time I was 30 and I was one of the oldest, the average age of people going to AADA is early 20's, except for the acting for film and television program, everyone was a bit more and around my age.  I love the American Academy Of Dramatic Arts, it's an historic building, an historic Academy, great location, great neighborhood, it was an honor and an a dream for me going there. I'm very grateful for the experience.

Shot out to: Nurit Monacelli a great human being, actress and teacher and  to my professor and music director Christopher Mcgovern with whom all my singing for the actor classes were like an episode of glee.", "Went to a month long summer program which involved taking classes and meeting the students. I honestly was not very impressed. The classes were literally the same as the ones I had been taking at my community college, with the only difference being the much higher price tag for the Academy. Most of the full time students were all very pretentious, many acting as though they were already famous, particularly the girls. The only professor I really liked was the guy who ran the improv class, the others weren't anything special."]

name: American Public University System
reviews:
['My husband was a student at this place, and I wouldn’t recommend it to a single person. He submitted his FAFSA each year, never changed, while on track to complete his Associates. WHEN he applied for graduation, not before, he was informed that he owed $3,698 because the university had messed up his financial aid. Tried to fight it since we had not received ANY prior notice to him applying for graduation, but was consistently met with the response that they would withhold his degree. Fine, whatever. We scrounge the money together and prepare to pay it. When he called AFTER we had gotten the money together, they told him that they “found something else” and his new balance was $7,395. Obviously, in this forbidden economy, it’s impossible for anyone to come up with $7,400 on a whim. No explanation, no notice, and they continued to allow him to enroll in courses throughout his degree. They waited until he was APPLYING FOR GRADUATION to drop this bomb. I have absolutely nothing positive to say about this place, but if you’re looking at applying, I would strongly suggest you find any other option, as nothing could be worse than this place.', 'I have absolutely, never in my life dealt with such a crazy process. I filled out all the application, started my financial aid, was even informed from fafsa that the funds were released, uploaded my drivers license, proof of residency, and my had my official GED transcripts sent from the granting institution just to be argued with and told that those weren’t the official documents. I then, was told that they did have the correct GED transcripts and that for whatever reason they were saying that my date of birth was incorrect in their system. HOW? I’ve given you all my information, sent my FAFSA information, and you have to go through so much identity verification for FAFSA alone it’s crazy. And then they ask me on a Friday to send them my birth certificate, they’re closed on the weekend, even though the document was due on the 30th? A Saturday and the class was supposed to start today (Labor Day) and they’re also closed. I was then sent an email telling me that I’ve been dismissed from their application process and I am not welcome to apply again all while literally spending days crying over this because I was so excited to go back to school. No one could call me, no one could provide me a phone number. I was told that the document people had to deal with it and was provided an email address. I’m so upset right now I’m going to be informing everyone about this because I’m devastated. And if anyone has any questions I’m happy to provide screenshots.', 'Very good instructors who seem to care when or if you reach out to them and establish comms right away, very helpful!

Financial Aide is not a team effort., different answers on different days unless you are able to reach a supervisor.
You need to understand how everything works including any Veterans Ed Payments or you will not like the outcome,    Do yourself a huge benefit, when responding with any support dept (Admin, Fin Aide, Student Support) and keep copies of every single conversation!

The requirements for almost every class are not discussed  during the enrollment process., this was very challenging for myself with long gaps in schooling!

With the growth of AI,  you need to be very careful with where you find your research for assignments.
I had serious issues with some of the strangest things when papers were checked for plagiarism and this ended ip causing me a LOT of problems and academic issues with classes and school In general.
Was able to determine much later on that the Keyboard i was using (Grammerly) became a serious issue.

When using auto correct or any other functions like word suggestions etc,   These were AI based and caused TURN IT IN  system to  wrongfully grade my papers !

One example:  I used the words.  “24 Hours & 7 Days”
Which counted against me grade several  times, in turn the instructor only looked at my Scores and not in detail.  This was reported to Admin as Plagiarism and I received a (ZERO)!

You need to be very fluent with MS OFFICE & (Power Point) from day 1.

Power Point is critical if you want to move along smoothly!', 'This university will not teach you anything except what red flags to look for at other universities. If you want you lesson plan to be a brief slideshow of generic definitions and links to outside websites, this is the school for you. You will walk away with nothing except lighter pockets. School support is a joke, you submit classroom tickets that take 24-48 hours for a response and if you don’t get it resolved on the first email, it’s another 24-48 hours. The instructors give you lower grades if they don’t agree with your perspective on topics, even if you addressed everything in the assignment. The grading rubric is merely a suggestive grading scale. They don’t have a true grievance process, you send an email to a “feedback” campus email. They are currently being investigated by the higher learning commission due to accreditation concerns. Do not give this school your money if you care about getting a quality education.', "It seems as though every online school has it's downfalls. I earned a bachelor's degree in psychology from this school and truthfully, it was the most difficult and challenging period of my life. My professors were extremely professional and I had no issues with my GI bill. I saw someone post about obtaining their master's program in Psychology that may not hold value. When I looked into the program myself I asked those questions to see if it holds value in anything and it does not hold much in what I wanted to do specifically. Instead of mastering in psychology, I am moving on to Human Resources. I've had the best experience so far, so I have no complaints. It is military friendly, and that's why it associates itself with the military. My professors were understanding when I had to leave for JRTC training. I think in any school things will happen, you just have to do your part in researching."]

name: American River College
reviews:
["I have visited the application lab at American River College about three or four times this month. Each time, I noticed that the staff are professional in appearance, wearing heels and professional clothing. However, their behavior does not seem to match their appearance. Some staff members have been rude and unfriendly. I observed girls speaking negatively about others and not offering help unless they favor someone. During my visits, which have totaled five, I felt quite upset about how some girls behave. I have seen others facing similar issues. I believe the staff at the lab lack professionalism; many only focus on their appearance rather than assisting students properly. It's important that the college trains its employees better, especially those in the application lab and financial aid office. Respect and helpfulness should be priorities. Staff should treat all visitors with kindness, regardless of appearance, and recognize that they represent the college. Right now, I feel disappointed and unwelcome, which has made me decide not to use the services there anymore.
They speak favorably and offer help only to people they personally like, while others are ignored. Instead of focusing on education or student support, the attention is placed on appearance—such as makeup, clothing, which is completely irrelevant.

This raises a serious concern about whether American River College prioritizes education or favoritism. I specifically experienced discrimination from two young female staff members who openly treated me differently. Their behavior appeared to be influenced by bias related to language and religion.

I clearly heard them speaking negatively about me in another language, assuming I would not understand. When I addressed it and informed them that I do understand the language, I expressed that this behavior was inappropriate and unfair", 'Nice staff but rude counselors. Most low star reviews will agree with me. The staff and professors here are pretty nice and helpful but I haven’t had one nice counselor ever sense I attended here and I’m in my final semester. These counselors are unprofessional and I have seen 4 different ones. I originally thought it was just one mean one when I first attended but after seeing 4 I come to realize the school needs to restaff there counseling department. My old CC counseling department had to help me with my classes for ARC from a completely different district. This is how unprofessional the councilors are here and that’s very embarrassing. Also I keep sending my transcripts to the correct email and ARC still claims they don’t have a record of them.', "I love studying here!!!
Everyone always willing to help, everything is accessible and easy.
There are free books in library, lots of places to study inside or outside, water, very clean toilets with hygiene items.
I'm very grateful to workers who planted these extremely beautiful flowers we have in our college.
I really love walking in parks, so I really enjoy walking around campuses
You can choose good professors from ratemyprofessor website.
All of them are really helpful in my nursing journey.
I became an A grade student in this college and I have honors for good GPA
College is accessible for me and now it's free because of the FAFSA which they can help you apply to", 'if I could rewind time, I absolutely would have chosen ARC over the disaster that is UC Davis.

UC Davis looked great on paper — respected UC, good rankings, etc. But the reality? Overpriced, toxic, and full of ego-fueled nonsense. Professors were checked out, advisors were unreachable, and the entire place ran on cliques and politics. Career support was a joke. I got a degree and zero direction.

I’ve spoken with ARC students who got practical, affordable, and actually supportive education — for a fraction of what I paid. While I was drowning in student debt and academic gatekeeping, they were building skills and transferring out with real options.

So no, I didn’t go to ARC. But if you’re stuck choosing between a “prestigious” name like UC Davis and something grounded like ARC — don’t fall for the brand. Learn from my mistake. Go where people care….', 'Terrible counseling experience with Mays J counselor. Very rude and condescending during the entire practice. Unprofessional. As someone who’s graduated from this college it’s very disappointing seeing the counselor treat my wife so poorly. After explaining the situation, Mays J chooses to be rude and unhelpful over the situation. It’s my wife’s first time at college and just trying to prepare for her program. And 2nd guessing her after she chooses every program or classes needed for her road map of success. We remained professional of course just wish she did the same and not try to talk down on my wife for trying to just get started.']

name: American University
reviews:
['I love American University. My daughter just graduated with a BA in international studies and minors in environmental science and computer science.  The college is located in s safe area of DC, the research professors in the international studies school are passionate and the school also has semester abroad, which is an amazing programs to Brussels (UN) and other part of the world. Highly recommend this college; money well spent.', 'American University — Washington, D.C.

American University is a beautiful, well-organized, and intellectually vibrant campus located in one of the most livable areas of Washington, D.C. The architecture blends classic collegiate style with modern facilities, creating an atmosphere that feels both traditional and forward-looking. The main quad is peaceful, filled with trees and well-maintained lawns, and it’s common to see students studying outdoors when the weather is pleasant.

Academically, AU stands out for its commitment to global engagement and interdisciplinary education. The professors are approachable, and many of them bring real-world policy and legal experience into the classroom. The Washington College of Law, in particular, has excellent resources for students pursuing careers in international law, business law, and technology law. The campus is also well connected to downtown D.C. via the Metro and several shuttle routes, making it convenient to attend events, internships, or professional meetings around the city.

Outside academics, the environment feels inclusive and community-oriented. The student services are efficient, the library is open late, and the athletic facilities are modern and well kept. Cafeteria food is decent by university standards, and there are good coffee spots nearby. Overall, American University provides an excellent balance of academic rigor, professional opportunity, and a comfortable, welcoming atmosphere for students from around the world.', "We only took the college tour so take everything with a grain of salt.
We loved that AU has a traditional college layout with quad and everything even though it is still with DC.
Almost all the facilities look new and well kept. They had the nicest dorm rooms as well.
They had the best orientation of all the schools we've gone to so far. Molly, our guide, was great.", "American University is a special place, in every iteration of the word haha. The people and teachers are unique, the education is rigorous, the campus is compact and beautiful, the facilities can be a hit or miss but are overall good, and much more. There's always something to complain about, but that's the case anywhere. I enjoyed my four years here!", "American is a gem of a school tucked into a remote section of DC.  It's a super safe area.
If your look for a quaint school with big city resources AU is the perfect blend."]

name: American University in Bulgaria
reviews: ['A very successful liberal arts University in the Balkans. Nice campus, good location, impressive students, an array of programs. A real American higher education experience.', 'Still my favorite university! I spent 4 years studying for History and Civilization during my 2019 until 2023 Class Period and also the colleagues provided more assistance in applying for US Work and Travel, resolving issues, helping through my Bulgarian residence card and many more. More further improvements should be taken in stopping the bureaucracy, improving the food quality at the Canteen, establishing a new Psychology and Advising Center with an American psychologist and advisor and providing more foreign professors than Bulgarian ones. A tip: do not make so much friends in the university and try to stick with few of them, as I used to had many incidents and learnt from these ones. An Alumni student from 🇦🇱 studying in 🇩🇪🇪🇺 and 🇪🇬... In the end, I’ll truly miss Bulgaria. As Ataturk once said, he loved Bulgaria because he left someone there who cared for him...', 'Great atmosphere and community. Well set campus and amenities.', 'Spending the semester here, everyone is so friendly and welcoming.', "I studied there for 1 semester, but had to transfer to another institution. The main problem with the university was mostly with the system itself, for example, with payments: them asking money for every single step was too much, I almost went broke. The problem was with the bureaucracy: you have to sign tons of documents and pay a lot of money (in my case it was 200€!) just to apply for a leave of absence (a special document with which you can rest from your studies for a length of a semester). Everything there was expensive for me, I don't think it's fine to pay 30$ per day for your food, and you're actually not supposed to eat anywhere else aside from the university canteen, because you can only spend 2 hours a day in the dorm kitchen, which is extremely low. By the way, I wasn't happy with the dorm staff either, because I got fined several times for pretty much no reason, and couldn't even appeal them later on. The RAs (resident assistants) and the dormitory director seemed toxic to me, they were acting like kings, and you can't really ask them anything, even if it's something serious. The quality of education is partially good, depending on the professor, but generally it was fine. AUBG is not a bad university, it's just not for everyone, but it is still too expensive, they give you too expensive value for money."]

name: American University in Cairo
reviews: ["I'm a newly admitted graduate student in The American University in Cairo. Since my arrival here on September 7, 2017 for a two-year master's degree, I've been exploring a lot of interesting places and spots in and around the university and several other places in Egypt. It has been a fantastic experience."]

name: American University in Dubai
reviews:
['This university deserves zero stars. The dorms are terrible — washing machines make your clothes dirtier, dryers don’t dry, and the kitchen is full of cockroaches, dirty, and smells awful. We’ve been complaining for years, especially to Sara Montero, who is responsible for these issues, but she simply doesn’t do her job. Nothing ever changes.

If you’re a scholarship student, think twice before coming here. Unlike other American universities that cover housing and meals, here you pay for everything — books (around €100 each, sometimes two per subject), visa, and expensive food. And since class schedules make it impossible to work, you’ll quickly realize the “scholarship” isn’t enough to live on. We’ve been asking for years for at least one or two free meals a day for scholarship students, but this million-dollar university doesn’t care about that — only about unnecessary rules and appearances.

Classrooms, especially in the EMBA building, are freezing — around 16°C. Professors have had to stop lectures because it’s too cold to stay inside.

Overall, this place is a disappointment. They ignore real student problems and care only about control and restrictions.', '“Unfortunately, my experience with the American University in Dubai was disappointing. The scheduled Zoom meeting never happened, and even after visiting the campus twice, I couldn’t get clear answers to my questions. Emails were also left unanswered. I expected a more professional approach.”', 'AUD is an incredible place for innovation and collaboration. I recently attended an AI event there, and it was impressive to see how open they are to new ideas and partnerships. It’s more than just a university; it’s becoming a hub where professionals, students, and creatives come together to exchange ideas and build the future.

Their vision for the next five to ten years is exciting. They are working toward becoming a central space for AI, entrepreneurship, and collaboration, a true “third place” where people connect beyond work and home. If you’re looking for a dynamic environment that fosters creativity and forward-thinking, AUD is definitely a place to watch.', "Pretty disgusted with this institution. They have a policy of keeping your exam certificates so even if you decide not to attend, AUD says they are entitled to retain your exam certificates. My mother had to go to the University and get my certificates back. They weren't going to do anything with them. Why not just return them?", 'Awesome big university crowded with students but quiet and disciplined. Beautiful building & great qualified faculty, clean and good atmosphere to study but expensive though ☺️']
