name: American University in the Emirates
reviews: ['One of the best universities in UAE, the University offers a variety of degrees in different disciplines like international studies, management, communication, journalism, computer science, among others. Proud to be the part of AUE team nurturing youth and shaping minds.', 'I met Sylvie at the Registration Office. She welcomed me warmly, and without her support I wouldn’t have had the courage to join the university. At first, I only came to ask some questions, but she guided me with advice, followed up with me, and even kindly invited me to reach out anytime if I had more questions. She was truly different, supportive, and classy in the way she dealt with me.', 'I would like to share my appreciation for Sylvie, who works in the administration at the American University in the Emirates. She is always extremely friendly, approachable, and kind. Sylvie goes out of her way to help others and makes sure everyone feels supported. Her positive attitude and genuine willingness to assist create a welcoming environment for both students and staff. It’s rare to meet someone so dedicated and caring, and Sylvie truly makes a difference.', 'I had the pleasure of working with Fargana, and I must say she is incredibly professional and knowledgeable. She guided me through every step as an international student and made the entire process smooth and stress-free. Her expertise in student recruitment is outstanding, and she always goes above and beyond to ensure everything is clear and well-organized. Truly a pleasure to work with! Highly recommend her to anyone needing guidance in this field.', 'I just want to say a big thank you to Ms. Sylvie from the Registration Department. She’s honestly one of the most helpful and kind people I’ve met at AUE. She’s always patient, explains everything clearly, and makes the whole process so much easier. You can really tell she cares about helping students, and that makes a huge difference']

name: American University of Antigua
reviews:
['The faculty, support staff and the student population is well equipped, diverse and kind. The facilities and opportunities provided at AUA help in a holistic education experience to be better and well rounded aspiring physicians. There is a major focus on integration of hands-on experience to aid in the medical education received.', 'Professors are great, we are constantly being pushed to do our best and the university provides all the support it can to help us. Manipal university is already very reputed and it is one of the best options for Indian students to practice in the US. The campus and the syllabus is constantly being updated to improve, and so we are completely prepared for our clinicals', '“ Guru Brahma , guru vishnu , guru devo Maheshwara … is a verse which is often associated with the Gurus … in Indian language a word for the mighty Teachers . As a mother , I was the first teacher to my daughter Anahita when I began teaching her the  alphabets , not knowing that someday when she will turn into a grown up girl she will move into the direction where the education she received will help her motivate to study medicine … a huge leap to save the lives of people around her. That is where her journey began with the most prestigious university enabling her to make her dreams turn true . The name of the university is American university of Antigua. Starting her journey from the cradle of Manipal situated in the beautiful state of Karnataka to a leap to the beautiful Antigua was something every parent would desire for their children . The university offers an education which is dedicated to teach Medicine in all its capacities to its students. The professors guide the students with a smile and try to explain subjects so that the words get ingrained in the Brains of the young minds . My daughter is in her med 2 and loves each and every moment she spends at the institute , studying hard to reach her goals thus making everyone proud of her . This could only happen with the guidance of the Guru’s ( professors ) she has at the beautiful American University of Antigua . They are her Role Models of the dream she has of becoming a Doctor…', 'They let me go all the way to fourth year and when it was time to take step2 Ck, they gave me only a year to pass their version of the boards. I paid thousands in tutoring and board review to help me pass their practice boards and I started doing very well, got 220, 230 and even one 240 on my timed NBME exams. Then, they screwed me big time. They gave me a their practice 4 hour NBME and if I passed it I would sit for the real step 2ck. This would be my final attempt towards the end of one year. When I sat to take the exam, they made it a 4 hour exam without any breaks. Previous practice NBME had timed breaks between each question block. I obviously had to take 1-2 bathroom breaks and a mental rest break between each block, not to mention the stress of knowing this is your last chance and you have todo well but you can’t pee and you can’t eat or drink and can’t give your brain a rest for the next four hours. A lot of added stress and anxiety to say the least.
Surely enough, I missed passing by a few points. They kicked me out of the program and no matter who I tried to contact, they would not let me Back in-the program. After all the years spent and money spent and hours and hours of studying and I was finally doing well and the tutors trained me how to read and understand standardized medical questions, and it all went down the drain because no matter how hard I pleaded, they would not let me back in the program to complete my medical degree and apply to residency.
These schools will take your money gladly, let you “play doctor” in their program, and then kick you out like trash and you are now stuck with 300k in high interest private loans and a huge gaping hole in your heart, and all you wanted to do was just become a doctor.
Do not go to any offshore schools. Stay in the states and get federal loans with low interest and don’t get sucked into these high paying schemes. It’s just a facade to be able to charge high tuition and loans with high interest.
This school screwed me big time and stole my years and my effort
I will never forgive them and do not be fooled, save yourself, these med schools are just a financial vehicle……….
Be smart and go to city schools and programs and try to graduate without debt, or it will be a shackle in your life for many years and will affect and your credit and future adult purchases like a mortgage or other important loans.
I will be checking that this review is not removed, and if it is I will rewrite it again for life and I hope that all people this happened to speak up as well…..', 'AUA is a college where you find a very diverse group of students. It’s a safe environment to study and learn. The professors are genuinely interested in your well being and making sure that you understand the concepts well. We follow a system based approach allowing us to master and integrate each system we learn. Overall my experience at AUA has been amazing.']

name: American University of Armenia
reviews:
['This university is ridiculously expensive, and yet the quality of education hasn’t improved one bit. Tuition fees keep going up year after year, seemingly just “manually adjusted” without any real justification.

The registration process is an absolute nightmare — slow, disorganized, and stressful. I am on and off  with this university for 4 years now and I am shocked to see that nothing has improved since then, aside from a new website (which doesn’t really solve the bigger issues).

While there are some professors who are genuinely professional and knowledgeable, there are also plenty who can barely speak English and seem completely lost in their own classes. I honestly don’t understand how some of them were even hired.

Overall, for the amount of money they charge, the experience is disappointing and frustrating. This place needs serious management reform, not just cosmetic changes.', 'Can’t comment on the quality of education here, but the amenities and staff here is very good. We had a conference here two years ago and facility is amazing. The design and interior is well thought out. I hear it was designed by American architects. Very impressive inside. Student life is very active judging from posters inside. A lot of art paintings inside and nice food offered in cafeteria.', 'One of the best universities of Armenia. EC, Business & Computer technologies are the main facultets of AUA. Good library full of interesting books', 'Ive had my english courses here and the teacher was absolutely flawless. I also had my tests here, TOEFL, SAT. The staff was always professional, helpful and kind. I would also thank the admissions office for being helpful with any questions.', 'The best university in Armenia by far.']

name: American University of Central Asia
reviews: ["The best university in Kyrgyzstan. Some people might say it's an oasis in Kyrgyzstan. However you can it, it was the best decision of my life to choose this school as my college.", 'I like this place wgoch is quite great looking with great faculty and campus. Lots ofbright students and spacious classes. Canteen is so so but accepts cards. Lots of international students and super facilities. Recommend for studies for sure!', 'University is the best in the region. Campus is amazing, the view, the atmosphere is amazing.\n\nEven just for walking you definitely should visit!', 'A quality educational institution. The staff provided very clear and precise information. Also, all the staff were friendly. 👍', "Really wish we had another choice. Sad to say but this is the best we have. Most of the teachers are too tied up with putting their nose where it doesn't belong. I send my children to school to learn how to think, not what to think. I utterly despise it when ignorant teachers try to attack God, as if they were capable. If one wishes to believe in God it is their own business. If one chooses not to believe in God, let them alone too. I send my children to school to learn how to think, I will send them to Sunday School to learn about God."]

name: American University of Iraq, Sulaimani (Kurdistan Region)
reviews:
['Very amazing Saeed Iman is definitely winning the competition and um also Ziad or Iad not sure that guy egyptian dude, THE BEST!! ALL OF THEM!! It was amazing coming here yall ilysm and miss u 🥹🤧💔', 'Fabulous campus and number one in Iraq for higher education.', "Amrican university of sulaymanih

The University opened its doors in 2007 to a cohort of 10 students from across Iraq who were admitted to the first undergraduate class. The University simultaneously launched a professional MBA program for students planning to study business and leadership at the graduate level. The university has grown to roughly 1,600 students and now offers undergraduate degrees in Business Administration, Information Technology, International Studies, Engineering and English and Journalism. All students at the University, regardless of their chosen field of study, take core courses in the humanities and sciences. The requirements of every major program are balanced by multi-disciplinary core courses grounded in the liberal arts. These courses foster self-reliant thinking, creativity, familiarity with the great ideas of human tradition and a greater knowledge of the world.

As the University expanded, so too did its extracurricular programs and activities. Many AUIS students are engaged in volunteering, community outreach, internships, athletics, and the creative arts. The University has also had men's and women's sports teams in basketball and soccer as well as student clubs which have grown from the first club in 2011 to include more than 20 different clubs focusing on various activities such as reading, chess, music, development, model UN and drama, among others.", 'Throwing the hijab to the ground is a direct insult to all muslim women worldwide. Such a disgraceful act cannot be tolerated - this university has lost all credibility and must be closed.', 'Worst uni ever, very biased and only care about themselves and advertising for their uni and not about their guests or students, especially in competitions.']

name: American University of Kuwait
reviews:
['From my experience as a freshman: You gotta attend your classes on time, do some hard work, and always respect the professors’ rules. It is a great college to be in, most of the students there are very welcoming, open-minded and polite. This college has so many events and ton of activities and also offers places to eat; AUK Diner, Starbucks, and Fat Olive restaurant. Then to top it all of there are some downs, parking is very limited so be there twenty minutes early, and this is the only college in Kuwait where calculators are prohibited.', 'The American University of Kuwait (AUK) is a private liberal arts institution located in Salmiya, Kuwait. Established in 2003 by Amiri Decree 139, AUK is affiliated with Dartmouth College and offers an education based on the American model of higher learning. The language of instruction is English.

AUK offers a variety of undergraduate programs leading to bachelor’s degrees. The College of Arts and Sciences provides degrees such as:
•	Bachelor of Arts (BA) in Design
•	BA in Communication & Media
•	BA in English
•	BA in International Relations
•	BA in Social & Behavioral Sciences

Additionally, the College of Business and Economics offers programs in business-related fields and has been accredited by the Association to Advance Collegiate Schools of Business (AACSB) since 2019.', 'modern n perfect for queit mornings and meetups.. its a hidden gem try it once', 'I visisted Auk today and Ms Nadia was with me the whole time and was very friendly and professional with her work , and told me everything about registration and many things . Thank you Nadia !', 'Better than any other coffee shop I have been to...best in kuwait']

name: American University of Madaba
reviews:
['I would not recommend anyone to enrol in this university for many reasons, such as the toxic environment and stupidly manic doctors who think that they know everything but they actually do not know anything, especially in the pharmacy department, like Nabil Darwazeh, Waleed Zalloum, and Islam Hamad.', 'The university academic, social, and activity wise is perfect. The doctors are friendly and helpful, you will even probably find jobs easily. My complain is about the admission and registration department. It is one of the worst customer service I have ever encountered. Every single person there never helped and they are always frowning in our faces , they just try to ignore us and are never helpful. Though if they do they are still frowning. I wish the university would do something about this issue.', 'Most beautiful, friendly and caring university 💕🧡', 'One of the best universities in Jordan
Let me start with Professors
1 they really care Their student needs
2 i have Learned so much from my professors
3 Learning methods Are impressive and I actually have learned a lot

The university itself Really cares about their students And really care about providing The complete package Of services That any student would need For example Sports center and Mental and health support  and not to mention, they are really fast When it comes to answering any question
What I like about this university that They listen to their students in order to become better And after switching to this university I realized  that The American university of MADABA is one of the best universities here in Jordan .', 'High quality education, good doctors, satisfied places, everything is available
But the thing is; it is so far from amman, and there is nothing around it']

name: American University of Middle East
reviews:
['As a Computer Engineering student at AUM (American University of the Middle East), my experience has been truly rewarding. The university offers excellent facilities, modern labs, and well-equipped classrooms that make learning practical and enjoyable.

The professors are knowledgeable, approachable, and always willing to guide students through complex engineering concepts, which helps me grow both academically and professionally. The campus also provides great opportunities for extracurricular activities, clubs, and events, allowing students to develop teamwork, leadership, and technical skills beyond the classroom.

Overall, studying Computer Engineering at AUM gives me the perfect balance of quality education, hands-on experience, and a supportive environment. I highly recommend AUM to anyone looking for an enriching academic experience in Kuwait.', 'في البداية المواقف I took my daughter to take the aptitude test. We arrived according to the instructions, but we were not allowed to enter the shaded parking spaces under the pretext that they are designated for students. I was forced to stand in the outdoor parking spaces under the sun, and it is the beginning of summer, and the duration of the test is two hours, so there is no coordination and organization.', 'Good Place But Small Prayer Room', 'Pros:
Beautiful architecture
Nice plants
Huge campus

I went into the college as it’s ranked number one by a company I’m sure that company was impressed because they care about impressing outsiders. Their target is fame, social media clout and impressing teens with soccer nights open for all, concerts, events etc. Remember  that.
My experience:
At this college i have called and emailed student affairs about my inappropriate experiences with peers. Their response was “ well, if you don’t want to cheat with the students then you should just tell them you will do your part” that was in my first year. It was my first time i needed true guidance on how to handle dishonesty professionally. They said to ignore it. I went forward to explain this to the multiple groups whom wanted me to pay a tutor to complete our group project with them. I was called cheap, I was told that I am complicating things, that i was being difficult, that I could make a mistake that would lower their grades and how selfish that was of me, I was told no and I had to get into an ordeal just to not pitch in. I have to this day get into an ordeal to simply have my work represented in a group project. Every semester. One time I sent my completed part to a group member and she said yes i will submit it go into your class no worries. She did not submit it because her tutor didn’t complete it in time. I went to student affairs in person and they said well we cant do anything its a group grade doesn’t matter if one person did the whole thing but its all members responsibility to deliver it on time. I got -7% of my total grade that day. Imagine an 18-22 year old being graded by other adults actions? 😆 I as a 20 year old need to go run after another student to tell her to do her part or else i do the whole entire work myself but both our names stay on the paper as once groups are made, they can not be changed. Group work is worth minimum 30% of your total grade in courses. 30% of my grade is determined by outside factors who have no designated responsibilities. Their lack of action = my lack of action. My hard work = our hard work. I have gone to student affairs multiple times in person begging for a fix. The last “advice” i got was to choose a better group next semester… Each semester as they see students with A+, they will make the classes harder. This affects students who are within the educational level as they will be competing to keep up with tutors.
Tutors:
They share their numbers with students and range from 15-45kd on average to complete college work. They get added to all whatsapp class groups. Who are they? Idk. One time a tutor told my group he would send the file after his cricket game. These tutors also having nothing to lose but money. Low grade? Ill pay you back 50%. High grade? Recommend me to your friends. Are they teachers, students, or just randoms who want to make extra money. No one knows not even the cheaters.
Professors:
Professors know this as the language used in slides never measures to the students education. Professors see students with their phones during class tests but look the other way. Professors even let students read off their phones during presentations. Professors sometimes act to care by asking follow up questions but most of the time the students dont even comprehend basic english. Shoutout to some professors tho their teaching is outstanding.
These students graduate knowing nothing but knowledge can always be learnt. Integrity however, is something that can not. Some one out there put his everything into a business just to hire graduates who will cost him more than they will make him.
What does AUM get from having so many students even those who don’t understand english😏 what do they get from having students who all have A+ from blatant cheating 😏 it all circled back to fame, reputation and the personal gains that come from that.', 'As an employee, I decided to apply at this university as part of my study leave plan. I applied with high expectations and an honest belief that this was a good educational establishment (what with all their marketing propaganda). Unfortunately, often times in life our expectations couldn't be farther from reality. Everything was fine initially as I went through the registration process. I brought all the necessary documents and passed the entry examinations. The only step remaining was to pay the registration fees.
There's an obvious fixation on money with private universities in Kuwait and this one is no different. I had to pay the registration fees first before receiving an acceptance letter (which I needed to submit to my potential sponsor). This meant that I had to pay the fees out of my own pocket as my sponsor couldn't green light my study leave without the letter of acceptance. I shouldn't have to pay first to get an acceptance letter. That's red flag no. 1. Despite that, I was willing to pay as they gave me a clear impression that I had been accepted and that all I had to do then was pay the fees.
When I went to pay the fees I was told that there was a problem with my submitted documents. I was also told that the issue was irreconcilable and to just apply elsewhere. I wasn't even let inside the campus but rather told via phone while waiting outside in the blazing sun. Furthermore, it didn't make sense to tell me something like this during the final stage of the registration process. They had my documents for an entire month but just discovered this "issue".
I called them hoping to resolve the "issue" but they just ended up giving me conflicting information regarding their registration policies.
The administrations staff were dishonest and unprofessional and I'm certain that my denial of acceptance was a political move on part of this school's upper management (which they obviously won't admit). Applying here was an utter waste of my precious time and effort. There's better universities out there, do yourself a favor and apply elsewhere.']

name: American University of Paris
reviews: ["Life changing find! My review is based on the education and experience I received at AUP as a transfer student, and it exceeded every expectation. The quality of the classes was far beyond what I imagined, small class sizes taught by professors who are leading professionals in their industries. I can’t imagine another university that offers this level of mentorship and access while studying in Paris. Beyond the exceptional academics, AUP made the entire transition seamless. The visa process was incredibly well organized, and I never once felt stressed about the paperwork. They also provided extensive support with French bureaucratic processes, including opening bank accounts, setting up phone plans, and navigating housing. Their housing network, by the way, was stellar, allowing me to find landlords who have worked with AUP for years with a selection of affordable properties in the best neighborhoods of Paris. This was one of the best experiences I had lived, and I couldn't recommend AUP more! I grew as a person and took some of the best life lessons from this experience. I highly advise those interested to be as communicative as possible, their team is supportive and will answer all your questions.", 'I wouldn’t let my pet goldfish get a SWIMMING CERTIFICATE from this waste of wood. According to Google, a good institution is defined by its “strong leadership, qualified and supportive staff, a clear mission, and a safe, positive environment that fosters learning and well-being” AUP lacks most of these, ESPECIALLY the “qualified and supportive staff” piece.  It’s truly sad, because the TEACHERS and STUDENT ACCOUNTING at AUP are actually amazing, but the rest of the administration staff is nothing short of lazy, pathetic morons, who just clearly need a paycheck…nothing else. They barely respond to their emails, they give you false information then get upset with you when you show any sign of annoyance of THEIR incompetency, they prioritize their “colleagues’ reputations” over your well-being, and they have many scandals that are disgustingly true; including the one where the literal PRESIDENT of AUP has said the N-word and a plethora of other racist things in the past. There is even an entire article written about her and the things she said in the literal AUP magazine. Honestly, the only promising thing this school has going for itself is the fact that it’s located in Paris, but other than that, don’t waste your time, money, or energy coming here. This school literally ruined my Paris experience, I couldn’t even stand being in the city because it was always SOMETHING. So please, don’t let them get the chance to do the same for you. There are other ways to get a degree and/or live in Paris.', 'Incredible alumni with fake degrees spanning from Quantum Physics to Haitian Law', 'A testament to great [food/service].', '!!! This is my first ever review which says a lot. If you are debating coming to AUP DO NOT.  This university was one of the worst things that has ever happened to me. Specifically their administration and housing. I was harrassed daily in my housing and once I told administration they did nothing and they forced me to endure unsafe living conditions. Do not waste your money and time at this terrible terrible university.']

name: American University of Rome
reviews:
['My housing experience with AUR was extremely disappointing—so much so that I ultimately withdrew from the program, despite enjoying the travel opportunities and social aspects.
Upon arrival, our apartment was in unacceptable condition. There was leftover food in the refrigerator, dirt and trash throughout the unit, unclean beds, and visible stains on the walls. Shortly after moving in, we discovered a cockroach infestation and even live rodents. Despite repeatedly requesting a professional exterminator or alternative housing, my concerns were ignored until I personally went to the Student Life office in tears after finding more than ten cockroaches in my bathroom. Phone calls I made about the issue were disregarded entirely.
Additionally, the housing amenities were not as advertised. The apartments were promoted as having air conditioning in the common areas, yet there was none. AUR also stated that towels and bed linens would be provided for each student; however, only some of my roommates received them, and those that were provided were molded and unusable.
Overall, my experience with AUR housing was misrepresented and mishandled. The lack of cleanliness, pest control, and responsiveness created an unlivable environment. I would strongly recommend that students consider other institutions that prioritize their well-being and deliver on their promises.', 'The best universities go beyond a simple name. If what is destined to be done by someone seeking leadership and training is in this unusual and perfect combination between american education and the history of Rome, one can only expect the best results, but it depends first and foremost on dedication and continuous search for training, not on perfectionism. That is why Rome can also be a great option for all of this.', 'Proud alumna ! Amazing time at AUR can’t recommend the world class faculty and staff', 'Hard to talk in-depth about the graduate programs, but after talking to many of my friends and classmates we all agreed that the student body is what makes the school shine, rather than the institution. Specifically, one grad student, whom I love dearly, makes my life so sweet because they are my cherished partner.', "I cannot speak for the undergraduate school, but I was extremely disappointed with the faculty and graduate program overall. I felt I was deliberately set up for failure and misled about the supports I would receive. My advisor repeatedly failed to show for meetings and left emails partially answered if she replied at all, as though she could not be bothered to read through my questions and thoughts. She gave substantial feedback on my full thesis only after my defense. My program director openly insulted my intelligence during my thesis presentation and added assignments to her classes without updating the syllabi (she had emailed. I had contacted student services prior to the beginning of the year to update my email, but communications went to an inbox I can no longer access all the same. I frequently was left off of email communications from the school.). We were assured that AUR has internship connections around the world, only to find out halfway through the program that we were responsible for securing our own internships and that AUR couldn't do much for us outside of Rome. The one internship AUR offered which was connected to my thesis topic went to another student who was studying something else. We were discouraged from utilising the Career and Internships Office to help find leads. I chose a small university in the hope that I would not be lost in a crowd, that I would be supported and guided in the research process. I was not anticipating conducting research entirely independently, at a school that doesn't even provide access to most online databases and doesn't publish theses. What a waste of $20k. I am, frankly, surprised that AUR has accreditation. I urge anyone contemplating graduate school to investigate thoroughly his program of choice and not merely take it at its word. I should note that there are a handful of absolutely wonderful staff and faculty, without whom I would have lost all hope in the very institution of academia. My deepest thanks go out to them for getting me through the day on my darkest ones."]

name: American University of Science and Technology
reviews:
["As a former student of AUST Master's program in Toxicology, I feel compelled to share my experience to help prospective students make an informed decision. Unfortunately, my time in the program was marked by a series of disappointments that have had long-term implications for my academic and professional goals.

The most critical issue is the program’s lack of accreditation. While this information was technically available, it was not clearly disclosed or emphasized during the application process. Many students, myself included, only realized the implications after enrolling.

Please pay attention visit to the ministry of Education before you apply.", "Been here 3 years, final semester, GPA 3.4/4 I was a few days late on my last payment. ($700) and explained I’d pay by the end of the month when I get my salary. They didn’t care. Just shut down my access to all the online material we need for the finals and refused to help. After all the time and effort I put in, it’s clear they only care about money, not students. This university was not like this when I enrolled it had people in administration who cared about students. Now it down't. If your looking to enroll, don't. Pay a bit extra and go to AUB", "I just graduated with TD in Business, all I can say that it’s well established university in terms of structure and educational team, all it’s professors are highly reputable in their occupation what makes this college stand strong among its peers in age surpassing them in reputation and quality of education.

For those bragging about their historical universities like AUB, LAU and putting irrational comparisons all I say : It depends on your goals. If you want to enter a competitive profession, an elite college name will get you in the door when another might not. In the end, the college experience itself is all about how much you put into it. The prestige of a college doesn't matter a whit to many, but can be very important to some.", 'What you will face if you are a new student who decided to continue his studies or a new major at AUST:

-Just create that account, no need to finish the process because on the second day, they will call you. In less than 24 Hours and your EEE will be scheduled, your legal paper| ID .. can be sent over WhatsApp (low photo resolution, it is fine). They are helpful and quick to fish, finish the process, sorry typing error

-You won’t be accepted without the extra courses. They will find you a thousand ways to add these courses … don’t worry. unis are unis that love to teach. dah!

-Fees amount and percentage are nothing but extra decorative ink on the page. So don’t fall for the 75% 50% even if you decided to drop the semester before the semester begins and you don’t know your classes and the teacher’s name. in fact, they might charge you because you thought about it.

-Always they are next to you helping you to get lost more and ask more and wait more .. especially “the student representative something “Mrs. OOO, a nice lady who will forget to give you a call but will tell you that she will take care of things. So don’t call and don’t send them an E-mail just wait for that no reply then go personally to the campus, coz you might end up on hold for a long time with no answer for a concern you stated since day 1.

Finally, whatever you pay in AUST stays in AUST.  same results at the end when it comes to paying. So, if you feel like being Caritas and doing charity AUST is your place. Highly recommended! After all, it is AUST – A University Seeking The $.', 'Pretentious, underwhelming, and ironically superfluous. They are too concerned with nothing, low effort, preoccupied with keeping up with the Joneses, and bad faith actors to such an extent that the instructors, at times, voice their frustration to their students. All in all, has potential, but avaricious, and facile.']

name: American University of Sharjah
reviews: ["Had an amazing visit time at the AUS Smart Fashion Market organized by the Fashion Club and AUS Carnival 2025. The campus is huge and with lot of parking space. The campus is clean, with clean washrooms. The university has well organized teams and management. Student council was good at managing the carnival and fashion club at the Fashion market. Many good clothing , F&B and vehicle brands were seen. Students are friendly, very helpful, professional and we'll mannered. Took some interviews had great conversations regarding sustainability and fashion. Overall it was a great experience to visit this prestigious university.", 'American University of Sharjah\nModern campus and architecture. Short campus tour videos attract many viewers.', 'I was invited to attend a conference here so this is just for the excellent venue and hospitality.', 'If I could give it a -5 star rating I would, when I graduated high school the American university of Sharjah was my first choice (reputation, ranking, environment, blah blah) I joined and I wish to go back in time and run, RUN away from this university. Its a scam you’ll always find yourself paying for things you don’t even recall purchasing, when you’re a freshman they’re cute and welcoming once you’re welcomed watch their true colors. Dorms are horrible, people are always getting drunk and smoking fighting and stealing clothes from the laundry room and money (yes they will enter your room using the Mastercard). Studying wise I promise you it’s the same everywhere in Dubai and Sharjah. (I am currently studying in the US for half the price and duration). Professors will treat you like you’re a slave and the amount of pressure will make you bald. This university is beautiful from the outside but once you really experience it and see all the catastrophes happening there you’ll understand (oh and just remembered the sheik of the mosque got stabbed, report is uploaded online). I am not writing this to cause any harm to anyone I’m saving you from the harm itself. If you love yourself or your children or have any kind of self respect, don’t stay don’t enroll don’t bother. I left AUS and I feel human. Don’t let what you see online fool you. BOYCOTT AUS.', 'This university has successfully wasted four months of my life because of irresponsible administration. If you have even slightly non-traditional qualifications, even with exceptional grades, don’t bother applying.']
